**"You've got to win the midterms...
...They'll find a reason to impeach me. I'll get impeached."

President Donald J. Trump**
_This is President Trump's warning, Fellow American._

Democrats are so obsessed with Trump Deranged Syndrome that they cannot celebrate ANYTHING he's done for our country.

Even after President Trump helped bring in Venezuelan dictator Nicolas Maduro, a man who is responsible for millions of American deaths. He's closed our borders. He's restoring American excellence. But if we don't have a firewall of a Senate majority, they will do whatever they can to impeach and remove him from office.

clean_body: President Trump has issued a warning to all conservatives. I need your help to make sure it doesn't happen. | "You've got to win the midterms... ...They'll find a reason to impeach me. I'll get impeached." President Donald J. Trump This is President Trump's warning, Fellow American. Democrats are so obsessed with Trump Deranged Syndrome that they cannot celebrate ANYTHING he's done for our country. Even after President Trump helped bring in Venezuelan dictator Nicolas Maduro, a man who is responsible for millions of American deaths. He's closed our borders. He's restoring American excellence. But if we don't have a firewall of a Senate majority, they will do whatever they can to impeach and remove him from office. Fellow American, I need your help. Democrats are not just coming after President Trump. They are coming after his allies, too. I'm a Trump-endorsed candidate. I am responsible for helping Republicans take back the Senate majority in 2024. Democrats like Elizabeth Warren are flooding states with liberal dollars. They are making "down payments" to defeat our president. Don't let that happen. Steve Daines Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. | | Charlie Kirk built a movement for truth -- but his final message pointed to something eternal.

It wasn't about politics... or elections... or even about the culture war.

His final book was deeply personal, and unlike anything Charlie had written before...

**It was about the Sabbath. **

This book is his blueprint for how we do that -- as individuals, as families, and as a nation.

clean_body: This is what gave Charlie strength| | | | | Fellow Patriot, | | Charlie Kirk built a movement for truth -- but his final message pointed to something eternal. It wasn't about politics... or elections... or even about the culture war. His final book was deeply personal, and unlike anything Charlie had written before... It was about the Sabbath. About sacred rest. Reconnecting with God. Charlie wrote this book because he knew what America was missing... and what he nearly lost himself. He found that in a world addicted to busyness, noise, and scrolling... The most radical act of resistance is to STOP. To rest. To reflect. To return to God. This book is his blueprint for how we do that -- as individuals, as families, and as a nation. 🔥 You'll discover how Charlie learned to honor the Sabbath in a culture that never stops 🔥 How setting aside just one day a week gave him clarity, peace, and strength 🔥 Why he believed this message could spark a spiritual revival across America I know he would've wanted you to have this book. When you give today to Turning Point USA, you 're not just supporting the movement Charlie built -- you're helping pass on the message he felt most urgently in his final months. Fellow Patriot, thank you, in advance, for helping reach the next generation with truth, faith, and moral courage -- the very things Charlie gave his life for. And as you do, may the message of this book lead you -- just as it led Charlie -- to a deeper trust in God, a stronger love for your family, and a renewed peace in your own soul. For Charlie, Justin Streiff Chief Operating Officer Turning Point USA P.S. Charlie Kirk's final book, Stop, in the Name of God, is a heartfelt call to slow down, remember what's eternal, and rest in God. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

name: Kyle Blomquist for Congress
subject: Why What We Do Now Matters
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blomquist for Congress
body:
I remember the moments in history that spurred people to reach for | | | Peter, Every time this administration commits another act of violence, I remember the moments in history that spurred people to unite and demand justice, from Bloody Sunday in Selma, Alabama, in 1965, when the state violently attacked peaceful demonstrators advocating for the right to vote, shocking the nation and leading to the passage of the 1965 Voting Rights Act to the Stonewall Inn, New York, in 1969, when police raided a gay bar, sparking mass protests, the rise of the Pride movement, and eventual legal protections for LGBTQ people. What defined those moments was not the tragedies inflicted by hateful government officials, but how the people responded in the months and years after. They were not deterred, intimidated, or silenced. **They got louder, they organized their communities, they fought back through our democratic systems and prevailed.** Their fight for justice continues today. This administration and the extremists it has empowered are murdering Americans with impunity, demanding that we disbelieve our own eyes and ears, and dismissing rational thought or any semblance of humanity. They have rolled back protections against racial and gender discrimination, ended women's right to choose, cut healthcare for millions of Americans, and weaponized government to silence dissent. And there are still three years left. **Peter, we cannot allow ourselves to be silenced now.** I need your support to flip this seat in 2026 so we can win back Congress and end Trump’s reign of terror. Together, we can channel our political power toward building a future not of division, fear, and hate, but of prosperity, dignity, and justice for all. – Kyle If you've saved your payment information clean_body: I remember the moments in history that spurred people to reach for | | | Peter, Every time this administration commits another act of violence, I remember the moments in history that spurred people to unite and demand justice, from Bloody Sunday in Selma, Alabama, in 1965, when the state violently attacked peaceful demonstrators advocating for the right to vote, shocking the nation and leading to the passage of the 1965 Voting Rights Act to the Stonewall Inn, New York, in 1969, when police raided a gay bar, sparking mass protests, the rise of the Pride movement, and eventual legal protections for LGBTQ people. What defined those moments was not the tragedies inflicted by hateful government officials, but how the people responded in the months and years after. They were not deterred, intimidated, or silenced. They got louder, they organized their communities, they fought back through our democratic systems and prevailed. Their fight for justice continues today. This administration and the extremists it has empowered are murdering Americans with impunity, demanding that we disbelieve our own eyes and ears, and dismissing rational thought or any semblance of humanity. They have rolled back protections against racial and gender discrimination, ended women's right to choose, cut healthcare for millions of Americans, and weaponized government to silence dissent. And there are still three years left. Peter, we cannot allow ourselves to be silenced now. I need your support to flip this seat in 2026 so we can win back Congress and end Trump’s reign of terror. Together, we can channel our political power toward building a future not of division, fear, and hate, but of prosperity, dignity, and justice for all. – Kyle If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Or mail a check to: P.O. NPR:
Local voting officials are preparing for possible federal interference in the midterms
Peter ,
Election officials from BOTH parties are sounding the alarm: They’re preparing for the possibility of federal interference in this year’s midterms because Donald Trump is once again toying with abusing presidential power – floating wild ideas like deploying ICE agents to polling places, banning vote-by-mail, or even using emergency powers to meddle with election processes.
It’s outrageous, and it’s terrifying.
At GOTV PAC, we’re working to make sure voters aren’t scared away. We’re running the largest voter outreach program we’ve ever attempted – especially in communities targeted by intimidation and misinformation.
But to scale our effort, we need resources. Will you chip in now to help us fight voter suppression and protect democracy in 2026? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpac2026?refcode=Email1112026]

There’s no time to waste. Trump has shown us – again and again – that he’s willing to break every norm to hold onto power. Our job is to make sure voters know their rights and feel safe casting their ballots.
Thanks for being in this fight with us,
– GOTV PAC

name: Mark Kelly
subject: Do not be afraid. Do not be silent. Speak out.
body:
We all remember what happened five years ago this month.
---
Hey Derek, it’s Mark. We all remember what happened five years ago this month. Donald Trump incited a mob to storm the Capitol to try to steal an election. He had just lost an election, fair and square, and he didn’t like the result. So he decided he was going to try to silence the voices of over 81 million people. It was one of the darkest days in the history of our country, but we made it through. Now he’s back, five years later, and it’s the same scheme. His Secretary of Defense, Pete Hegseth, is trying to silence me as we speak. He thinks he can make an example out of me, and intimidate others into staying quiet. But here’s the thing: Americans really like freedom of speech. You sign an oath to defend it when you join the military. Trying to silence people into compliance is just about the most un-American thing you can do. Period. There is nothing Trump or Hegseth or anybody in that administration can do to shut me up. I am going to keep fighting for our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. We’re not going to let him win. Not a chance. So, five years on from January 6, I want you to take this to heart: **Do not be afraid. Do not be silent. Speak out.** We’re going to get through this the same way we did back then: together. [**Please, split a grassroots donation between the DNC and me to help support our fight against Trump. This is our toughest yet, and I need a huge team behind me.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-kelly-split?refcode=em_20260111_5744_fr&amount=20&refcode2=5744_22930333_Pbwto5&akid=5744%2E22930333%2EPbwto5) _If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your split contribution between Mark Kelly and the Democratic National Committee instantly:_ | | | **[Donate $10](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-kelly-split?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=em_20260111_5744_fr&refcode2=5744_22930333_Pbwto5&akid=5744%2E22930333%2EPbwto5)**

****

Thank you.
Mark

name: Voter Action Project
subject: We must defend vote-by-mail
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Donald Trump can't help himself, Team. He keeps openly talking about how every election-related move he makes is to benefit his party and to silence any dissent. That's why we have to fight for our fundamental right to vote.
If you're with us, we need you to RUSH $25 or more right now to defend ballot access and increase voter participation across the country >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=011126_2&amount=25]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
This is the whole ball game, Team:
@JeffMerkley: Why does Trump want to ban Vote by Mail? Because it blocks Election Day interference: closing polling places, long lines, voter suppression. Vote by Mail is the best tool we have to put power where it belongs—in the hands of the people. I'll fight like hell to protect it. [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=011126_2&amount=25]
This is the whole reason behind President Donald Trump's massive anti-voting executive order. After every election in which he's been a candidate or in power, Trump has raged over the use of mail-in ballots in general and ballot deadlines in particular . He's tried to claim fraud for every single ballot counted after Election Day.
It's worth noting that Trump himself has used mail-in ballots to vote , most recently in an election in his now-home state of Florida.
This isn't about safety or security. It's a blatantly partisan exercise to suppress the vote and make it harder for you to have your voice heard in our elections. It's about their anti-democratic authoritarian desires and need for total control.
Team, we're fighting every day to protect vote-by-mail and increase participation in our elections. Help us protect our fundamental right to vote with $25 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=011126_2&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=011126_2&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=011126_2&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=011126_2&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=011126_2&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=011126_2&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=011126_2&amount=35]
We all know that mail-in voting laws are about ballot accessibility, making it easier for voters to participate in our elections . Elderly voters, the disabled, our military service members -- they all vote by mail.
Mail-in voting has happened dating back to the Civil War. It's always been safe and secure, mail-in voting fraud is supremely rare. States have spent millions of taxpayer dollars investigating voter fraud since 2020's "big lie" and found virtually nothing. Mail-in voting has been used widely in some states for decades and in most of the country since the pandemic. There's no good faith reason to get rid of it.
Opponents of voting rights have proven they'll go to any length necessary to stop people from voting and make sure that they stay in power. Will you chip in $25 or whatever you can to fight their unending desire for control and protect mail-in voting? Because it blocks Election Day interference: closing polling places, long lines, voter suppression. Vote by Mail is the best tool we have to put power where it belongs—in the hands of the people. I'll fight like hell to protect it. [] This is the whole reason behind President Donald Trump's massive anti-voting executive order. After every election in which he's been a candidate or in power, Trump has raged over the use of mail-in ballots in general and ballot deadlines in particular . He's tried to claim fraud for every single ballot counted after Election Day. It's worth noting that Trump himself has used mail-in ballots to vote , most recently in an election in his now-home state of Florida. This isn't about safety or security. It's always been safe and secure, mail-in voting fraud is supremely rare. States have spent millions of taxpayer dollars investigating voter fraud since 2020's "big lie" and found virtually nothing. Mail-in voting has been used widely in some states for decades and in most of the country since the pandemic. There's no good faith reason to get rid of it. name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 1/11/2026
body:
The Tina Peters case is heading into a critical week
| | | | [](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
---
**January 11, 2026** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST**
| | Dear Rocky Mountain Voice family,
We want to take a moment to walk readers through where the Tina Peters case stands as we head into a critical week — and why the timing of last Friday’s filing matters.
Late Friday, Rocky Mountain Voice published [the defense’s **final reply** in Peters’ appeal](https://link.email.joinrmv.). This was the last word from her legal team before the Colorado Court of Appeals either issues an order or moves forward with **oral arguments scheduled for THIS Wednesday, January 14th**.
What stood out about the defense’s reply is how directly it raises the stakes. Rather than asking the court to simply correct errors or revisit procedural issues, Peters’ legal team is arguing that **the state never had jurisdiction to prosecute her at all.** Their position is that Peters was acting under federal authority while administering federal elections — and that Colorado therefore lacked the power to bring charges against her. On that basis, they are asking the court to order her immediate release, rather than allowing the appeal to proceed while she remains incarcerated.
Earlier in the week, [the state filed its own response](https://link.email.joinrmv.), rejecting those claims and urging the court to move forward on a standard appellate timeline. Colorado maintains that Peters was properly prosecuted under state law, that the presidential pardon has no legal effect on her conviction, and that federal supremacy arguments do not apply in this case.
Adding another layer to the moment, **Governor Jared Polis publicly acknowledged this week that he is[considering clemency for Tina Peters](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** as he begins his final year in office. In an interview with CBS Colorado, Polis described Peters’ nine-year sentence as “harsh” and said his administration regularly reviews clemency requests, particularly for elderly inmates and cases involving long sentences.
That acknowledgement doesn’t change the court process — but it does underscore how many decision points now surround this case. Between the appellate court, the unresolved jurisdictional questions, the rejected presidential pardon, and the governor’s clemency authority, Peters’ future is now being weighed across multiple branches of government at once.
For readers who’ve followed this case from the beginning, this week represents a narrowing of the issues. For those newer to it, it helps explain why the case continues to draw national attention. At its core, the court is being asked to weigh the limits of state power when federal election law is involved — a question that has still never been addressed on its merits in this case.
As we move into the week ahead, we’ll be watching closely for any court orders and for developments surrounding Wednesday’s oral arguments. We are planning to live-stream the arguments and will release a full report on the outcome.
As always, we’ll focus on what actually happens — not speculation — and keep you informed as clearly and carefully as possible.
— Team RMV
---
| | |
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[Colorado Taxpayers Miss Out on TABOR Refunds Under Polis Budget Plan](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Gov. Jared Polis on Friday submitted revised budget requests for the next two fiscal years, calling for new public safety spending, changes to Medicaid growth, and renewed efforts to privatize Pinnacol Assurance. Notably, the governor said Colorado residents won’t get refunds from the Taxpayer’s Bill of Rights (TABOR). __
**[The Arkansas Valley Conduit debate: What headlines leave out](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY BOB COOPER, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) _In the last week we have seen media all over the state cover Trump’s veto of the Finish the Arkansas Valley Conduit Act. All the headlines point to Trump punishing Colorado and depriving people of having clean water. However, none of the media covered important details about the project._

**[Colorado to Enforce New Gun Purchase and Ammunition Restrictions in 2026](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[KKTV 11](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Some of the new gun laws set to take effect in Colorado this summer have gotten mixed reactions. Those who support the new laws say they will improve public safety. Those against them say they infringe on Second Amendment rights.

|
---
**STATE**
**[State fires back at Peters’ jurisdiction challenge, rejects pardon and supremacy claims](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Fifteen months after Tina Peters was taken into custody at sentencing, and as she marks a second New Year behind bars, Colorado’s Attorney General moved to answer her latest court filing, pushing back on a motion that asks the Court of Appeals to decide whether it even has jurisdiction to proceed.

**[“I’m not a politician”: Montrose commissioner Sean Pond enters U.S. Senate race](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) “I’m not a politician,” Sean Pond said. “I’m just that guy that stood up and said no to federal overreach.” Pond said that decision eventually led him beyond local fights. Appointed to the Montrose County Commission in February 2025, Pond said the conversations didn’t stop once he took office.
**[Colorado ratepayers foot the bill for the “Just Transition”](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY CORY GAINES, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) _There’s a lot of detail in the Sun article linked [in this article] about various communities and how they feel as if Colorado’s “Just Transition” for coal-fired power plants isn’t too just for them. I don’t blame them. With a vote and the swipe of a pen, Colorado Democrats have hamstrung communities that were built around coal-fired power plants in the name of their arbitrary climate mandates._

_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[New York’s Collectivist Experiment: A Cautionary Tale for Colorado Too](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) _In his inaugural remarks, Mayor Zohran Mamdani said New York should “replace the frigidity of rugged individualism with the warmth of collectivism.” It sounded philosophical. For many residents listening, though, it also sounded practical—a signal that the city was preparing to step in more forcefully as everyday costs continued to rise._
**[Federal freeze targets childcare money in Colorado and four other states](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** [AXIOS](https://link.email.joinrmv.) The Trump administration says it is freezing $10 billion in funds for child care and poor families in five blue states, the Office of Management and Budget confirmed to Axios Monday. The White House is pointing to reports of social services fraud in Minnesota to threaten funding for child care around the country.
**[Media Reports Omit Key Detail As ICE Agents Targeted By Vehicle Attack](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[THE FEDERALIST](https://link.email.joinrmv.) It didn’t take long, but America’s propaganda media have (unsurprisingly) already begun to whitewash the anti-ICE violence at the heart of a shooting involving U.S. immigration officials. On Wednesday, a U.S. immigration official reportedly fired upon and killed a woman who was allegedly antagonizing agents during an enforcement operation in Minneapolis.
_**[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**_
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[Denver Mayor’s Sanctuary City Legal Costs Reach Full $2 Million Limit](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) The bill for Denver Mayor Mike Johnston’s legal defense for that March 5 hearing before the U.S. House Oversight and Government Reform Committee on “sanctuary” cities has reached its $2 million contractual limit.

**[Feds Join Investigation Into Massive Denver Apartment Fire](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[DENVER7](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Federal agents have been deployed to help investigate the massive fire that broke out at an under-construction apartment complex Friday, the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF) announced Tuesday.
**[Douglas County Commissioners Emphasize Local Control Without New Home Rule Election](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**[DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) The future of Douglas County chasing “home rule” status may not be in the hands of commissioners, but rather by residents who are considering placing the issue back on the ballot, according to one commissioner.

**_[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.joinrmv.)_**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[When outages become policy: Colorado’s energy accountability gap](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY RMV EDITORIAL BOARD, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) _The mid-December power shutoffs weren’t a weather anomaly or a one-off emergency. They were planned. And for tens of thousands of Coloradans, that fact changed everything. Families scrambled for generators. Hospitals shifted to contingency plans. Small businesses began calculating losses they may never recover._ __
**[All Electric Mandate Or ‘All of The Above’ Energy Policy? You Decide](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY RUSS MINARY, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) _In December, Excel Energy shut down electricity along the CO Front Range due to “high winds.” It’s difficult to estimate the costs and impacts for individuals, businesses, homeowners, hospitals and grocery stores. Those who had propane, natural gas or diesel-powered generators were able to weather the storm. Everyone else was out of luck._

**[There is No Such Thing as “Non-Partisan”](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY MICHAEL J BADAGLIACCO "MJB",[ ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) I recently stumbled across yet another sanctimonious article whining that school boards, city councils, and other local bodies are supposed to be “non-partisan.” The author practically clutched their pearls at the thought of politics creeping into these sacred spaces. Absolute BUNK!

**[Strategy First: Obey Before You Engage](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
**[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY DRAKE HUNTER,[ ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.) _Before acting, consider who is guiding you—God or man. The divine plan or human influence. Or, good versus bad influence. We live in a world that prizes action. People, society, and negative influences often push us with “make the move,” urging us to take a shot, trust our instincts, and do what feels right._ __
**[READ MORE COMMENTARY](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|
---
**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** Email [submit@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:submit@rockymountainvoice.com).
|
---

|

The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!

|

|

**WOOF! WOOF! HI, HUMAN PERSON! 🐾
**
**It’s me,****Bailey the dog!** I’m a very good girl, a treat expert, and a proud campaign pup. My mom is Katelyn, Haley Stevens’ finance director. That means she stares at spreadsheets all day while I nap (priorities, obviously 🐶💤).
But between belly rubs, I’ve picked up some impawtant info:
🐾 **Michigan is one of the biggest Senate races in the country.** All eyes (and ears!) are on us.

🐾 **Haley has the best shot of ANY Democrat to win.** The polls say so.

🐾 **And Haley herself? She’s like me – tough, loyal, and totally pawsome at protecting the people she loves.** 🐕💙
That’s why I’m begging: **[Will you chip in $5 today to help Team Haley flip the Senate and keep Michigan blue?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11612?t=4&akid=1892%2E49239%2ET-ej-v)**
| | [Donate](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11611?t=5&akid=1892%2E49239%2ET-ej-v)
---
If you donate today, I get extra pets! And most importantly, you’ll help one of my favorite humans, Haley, win this ruff-and-tumble race.
Thanks fur everything,

Bailey 🐾
(on behalf of my mom, Katelyn, and the whole Team Haley pack!)
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11607?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=6&akid=1892%2E49239%2ET-ej-v) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11610?t=7&akid=1892%2E49239%2ET-ej-v)

Team — Jocelyn has what it takes to win the race for Michigan governor. **[But she can’t do it alone. This is Jocelyn’s fourth statewide campaign, and each time, she’s earned the trust and votes of more and more Democrats, Republicans, and Independents than before.
_**She won her last election by _14 points_.**_
As Secretary of State, she works across the aisle every day to save Michiganders time and money, no matter their zip code. That’s exactly what she’ll do as governor.
Voters can count on Jocelyn to make government work so it’s there when you need it, and gets out of the way when you don’t. That resonates with voters across Michigan, regardless of party affiliation.
**[We need your help to spread this message and remind voters of how Jocelyn’s experience will make her a fantastic governor for Michigan. clean_body: Team — Jocelyn has what it takes to win the race for Michigan governor. This is Jocelyn’s fourth statewide campaign, and each time, she’s earned the trust and votes of more and more Democrats, Republicans, and Independents than before. She won her last election by 14 points. As Secretary of State, she works across the aisle every day to save Michiganders time and money, no matter their zip code. That’s exactly what she’ll do as governor. Voters can count on Jocelyn to make government work so it’s there when you need it, and gets out of the way when you don’t. That resonates with voters across Michigan, regardless of party affiliation. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | Thanks! name: Brian Lenney
subject: He's back. Again.
body:
You're not going to believe this.

| |
---|---|---
|
# He's back. Again.
### You're not going to believe this.
| | | [Brian Lenney](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---
| Jan 12
---
| [](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---
Someone just filed to run against me on Saturday...
Funny thing is, he's the same guy I beat badly in 2022. Then he came back in 2024 with the establishment's money behind him, went on this revenge tour thing, and I beat him by double digits. Again.
Round three? Apparently that's happening now.
(Some people can't take a hint)
Idaho's ruby red America First territory. But this challenger's voting record when he was in office looked like it came straight from San Francisco. Nampa sure didn't send him there to vote like that.
We showed him the door the first time.
Then... we did it again:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8fcf89d8-361e-4743-ae9e-0f8265b43fb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Mind you this is the same guy who told the Idaho Statesman all those "culture war bills" we passed are just "solutions in search of a problem." That's what DEMOCRATS say (yikes). So those bills protecting kids from porn in libraries? He called them solutions in search of a problem. Keeping men out of women's locker rooms too. Gender reassignment surgeries on the taxpayer dime? Same response from him.
Protecting kids from pornography matters to me. Biological girls deserve privacy in their locker rooms. And my constituents shouldn't be paying for someone's gender surgery.
My challenger disagrees with all of that.
So who's he fighting for?
Not Nampa conservatives. The establishment bankrolled him last time because they want Idaho to turn into Oregon-lite. That's his team. His whole career in the statehouse was about two things: (1) doing whatever the Governor told him to do and (2) doing whatever the bureaucrats wanted. A perfect "yes man."
_We don 't need that in Idaho._
I'm different. I've never had a conversation with the Governor. Not once. But damn near every single bill I sponsored in 2025 got signed into law because I went around the establishment and straight to the people.
For example...
My bill combating lawfare that silences free speech got signed into law (S1001). Remember how the Idaho Dept. of Health and Welfare ran a $15 million child care budget overrun? My bill curbing their overreach passed and got signed by the Governor (H90). Mask mandates? Permanently banned them a few months back with another bill I put up (H32).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e1d2b1df-40e5-4236-990c-295bd669f51b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
You know how I got those passed?
I went straight to the people. That approach works. Fighting back costs money when they come at you with everything though (in 2024, they outspent me six to one).
But we won anyway because our message was true and you had my back.
And that guy I beat twice? The one who brought us the needle exchange (thank God we REPEALED IT) and voted for nearly _every spending bill_ that hit his desk? He just won't seem to go away.
So I need your help keeping him at bay.
_**Remember when it felt like conservatives in Idaho were "alone?" **_
Like we were crazy for questioning the "pandemic" response? Like nobody in government was listening? I do. And since 2022, you've had a voice in Boise that can't be bought, won't be silenced, and actually delivers results.
I'm not special.
I'm just a regular guy with a big mouth and the willingness to use it. And after a few legislative sessions I learned something critical:
_You don 't have to compromise your principles to win._
_You just have to refuse to lose._
Out of 35 Idaho State Senators, I'm one of a handful who will tell the Governor no. Who will stand against the lobbyist class. Who will actually read the bills before voting. And in a chamber where one vote margins happen constantly, that matters more than you think.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6bebdc1b-396a-4c5d-bbfa-a621476f87f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Borders, language, culture?** Pat Buchanan was right about this. Idaho needs to stay Idaho. Your kids growing up safe. English as our language. Idaho jobs for Idaho workers. Our culture protected, not replaced by whatever California brings with them.
**What about "health freedom?" **We made huge strides, but bureaucrats never sleep. They're already plotting how to bring back mandates the next time there's a "health emergency."
**And those government workers bees?** I want bureaucrats to fear getting fired for wasting your money. I want transparency that makes corruption impossible. I want a government that works for you, not against you.
_But I can 't do any of that if I lose in May 2026._
**[Please send a contribution of $50, $250, $500, $1,000, or $2,000 to help me fight off the establishment machine one more time](https://substack.com/redirect/f9f9a5fe-08d9-499d-9191-a4f2e0cc8c64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
Your contribution today funds what I call the "Idaho Fighter's Defense Fund."
Truth mailers. Thousands of Republican primary voters in Nampa getting my actual legislative record, plus what we survived when they dropped $400K on attack ads. Door hangers work too. We're doing postcards for new Nampa residents (lot of people moving in lately). Phone calls in those final weeks before Election Day hit different when voters are actually paying attention. Texts. Online ads targeting primary voters specifically. Emails reminding them about my wins in the statehouse and who tried to buy their vote last year.
That's where your money goes. Direct contact with voters who need to know the truth.
**» [Click here to contribute](https://substack.com/redirect/f9f9a5fe-08d9-499d-9191-a4f2e0cc8c64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
You've stood with me twice already. Let's make it a three-peat. Because Idaho literally can't afford to let the other guy get his hands on the state's checkbook ever again.
For God, Family, and Idaho,
Brian Lenney
Idaho State Senate
District 13, Nampa
**P.S.** Let me know if you want to chat or have questions about any of this, or anything at all. I'm all ears.
(C) 2026 Brian Lenney
[Unsubscribe](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9icmlhbmxlbm5leS5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2FibGVfZW1haWw_.ByXyeujh3IpxOKVn8Q62ypmBmHF9S46BnIdAIt_77lU?)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d27b8e06-49d9-492a-8f93-face6e14c99b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[](https://substack..TIx9n5SABs1-yxTQkB7OdrMJSD_NNIpPDRaAeVX4edM?)

body:
#
Peter —
We just finished our first statewide weekend of action. We completed 40 blockwalking events in 20 cities — fueled by 13,000 volunteers who are coming together to win this race and take back our country.
[](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-2026q1.AKJ3HX&refcode=em260111-1-ak&t=1&akid=377%2E1347274%2EPbVATk)
We’re building the largest grassroots mobilization effort Texas has ever seen. It’s an enormous and expensive undertaking — but it’s critical work if we’re going to flip Texas this year.
**And we’ll need to scale up our operation even more in the coming days. The primary is March 3 — but more than half of voters will cast their ballots during early voting in just a few short weeks.**
So we need to make sure our organizing team and volunteers have all of the resources necessary to reach the voters we need to win this election and take back power for working people.
**[Will you please consider donating $5 to fund our mobilization efforts in every corner of Texas? Organizing in a state as big and diverse as Texas requires massive amounts of resources, so anything you can spare will make a difference when the votes are counted in a few weeks.](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-2026q1.AKJ3HX&refcode=em260111-1-ak&t=2&akid=377%2E1347274%2EPbVATk)**

James is grateful to have you in our movement. We try to send only the most important updates and opportunities to participate via email, but if you want to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.jamestalarico.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=29&akid=377%2E1347274%2EPbVATk).
clean_body: Peter — We just finished our first statewide weekend of action. We completed 40 blockwalking events in 20 cities — fueled by 13,000 volunteers who are coming together to win this race and take back our country. We’re building the largest grassroots mobilization effort Texas has ever seen. It’s an enormous and expensive undertaking — but it’s critical work if we’re going to flip Texas this year. And we’ll need to scale up our operation even more in the coming days. The primary is March 3 — but more than half of voters will cast their ballots during early voting in just a few short weeks. So we need to make sure our organizing team and volunteers have all of the resources necessary to reach the voters we need to win this election and take back power for working people. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly. | | ** | | | | | | | ** Team Talarico Paid for by Talarico for Texas Talarico for Texas PO Box 14508 Austin, TX 78761

clean_body: Friends, We were in court all last week fighting back against Democrats’ latest lawsuit to try to steal our congressional seat. Help me defeat them, again. | Friends, We were in court all last week fighting back against Democrats’ latest lawsuit to try to steal our congressional seat. | | The courts have previously struck down New York Democrats' congressional map as unconstitutional. So, of course, they are trying again. I am the ONLY Republican Member of Congress representing New York City. There’s just this one seat standing in the way of total one-party control in our city and flipping the House of Representatives so Hakeem Jeffries and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez are in charge. They cannot beat me on merit, debate or policy, so they are trying to silence our voice by changing our map so another Republican can NEVER get elected. | Drag it out. Flood the courts. Outspend the opposition. Eventually, they hope the resistance runs out of oxygen. That is the strategy. Every day in court costs money. Every filing costs money. Democrats are perfectly happy to burn through cash if it means wiping out the last Republican voice in NYC and locking in power for years. Friends, if you do not want Democrats buying themselves a new map and calling it justice, this is the moment to step in so we have the resources to keep fighting regardless of how many rounds in court it takes. | | | | | | | | | | | | | Thank you for standing with me, Nicole | Nicole for New York, PO Box 60487, Staten Island, NY 10306

| Peter,
After the murder of Renee Good by an ICE agent on Wednesday, Minnesota authorities moved swiftly to open an investigation into the incident and bring justice for Renee.
But then Vice President J.D. Vance took to X yesterday and claimed that the ICE agent had "absolute immunity."
Now, the FBI is refusing to cooperate with the state on the investigation, blocking access to case materials, scene evidence, and investigative interviews.

| The first major test of this year for Democrats is not a congressional or senate race.
In April, the Wisconsin Supreme Court is back on the ballot, and MAGA has their eyes fixated on the court again.
You remember what happened last spring. Elon Musk dumped over $20 million into the race to try and buy the court. Democrats fought back, raising enough money from small dollar donations and outorganized the richest man in the world.
It embarrassed Trump so badly he threw Musk out of the White House and his role at DOGE. Then Elon and Trump had a public breakup, and Musk mostly vanished from politics for the rest of 2025.
But now, Elon is back... just in time for Wisconsin Dems to kick his ass again.
---
| [](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001tq8SM8t76Lqd60tIaPn529g1QN0EmgVtpxoJBIeRxEwFR45swJWN4-9PsR5y7owVHWq--5D2eqJBB0kQ6yy6WDrjEspPkAYNaCsz6OhFNZYohT3SE1HhyJXwoSsOmCeWoa5wRerL9HLaiP_OtWRBE1EYJ4CUqDjjKtgXEHZAVSQOQgP2Wyy-95AhORddMTi7u7ARMxD-75CwzTYkfJII0A==&c=T0gT-Tq8kpSSOcvwY8AJGJqyAWcB1z98mO2hdXVkERiwetluHXYiGw==&ch=SR6yvKw5CbN27bu0EeguAJ6Q0ryRzFEQloGTa_AiOivySH0JjwGotA==)

It's the rerun from hell.
We have four months to organize another defense of this court and MAGA sees it as their opening to claw their way back while Democrats are focused on the House and Senate. Elon is out from the shadows and bankrolling MAGA again and they're plotting revenge for what happened in 2025.
Just like last year, this race will set the tone for Democrats in 2026.
Defend Our Courts is suiting up for this battle. After our huge success in helping protect the PA Supreme Court in November, we are expanding nationwide. Wisconsin is our first major target and we are bringing the same winning formula forward.
But here is the reality. When we launched our defense of PA, we had six months to build our operation. For Wisconsin, we only have four. Two fewer months to raise the money, build the program, and get ready to defeat MAGA again.
We don't have time to waste, we have to ramp up our efforts NOW.

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Colorado Draws the Line
body:
Florida’s gerrymander could make or break this entire fight for the House before any votes are cast in November.
| | | | | [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001i5t2h4E5ApA0RYQQL_RhbZ3t-2JWCUNi-eZrstp6OooBRXfRIRnY9c7_HKlLzMSN9Pd8B8J8ho122kJj-JefiPUOL6lQzV4O3iLVb3VWnNzURmSoGQVwDYO9IbbflnrsNpVppgzFoFnV-WiB3t95ZKksqSCiqHg1wg_S7w2DkXL67t3NnfHRh2n5al9na8S-3cKHWmygOlTJSMgGRalt9g==&c=pPxoQsluscZ28dbywosyKgiQ3Zk07qoXvEOiVYGAJfS5EvE97Lrcmg==&ch=h7suLRCDOvmm5TmNUqCVsKg0LFldonYD0cbISFjyJ2tks2g2GzFCQg==)
---
Peter,
When little Ron DeSantis ran for president, it was revealed that he put lifts in his shoe heels to add an extra 3 or more inches to his height.
That pathetic ploy didn't help him. He still failed--and was humiliated repeatedly by the man he now answers to: Donald Trump.
But now that Runty Ron's boss has ordered him to find him extra GOP seats in Florida to help MAGA survive the coming midterm storm, DeSantis is trying to give Republicans an artificial boost with the same old trick he pulled in the 2024 campaign: putting lifts in MAGA's shoe heels by forcing the Florida legislature to add 3-5 more red seats before April.
---
| [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001i5t2h4E5ApA0RYQQL_RhbZ3t-2JWCUNi-eZrstp6OooBRXfRIRnY9c7_HKlLzMSN9Pd8B8J8ho122kJj-JefiPUOL6lQzV4O3iLVb3VWnNzURmSoGQVwDYO9IbbflnrsNpVppgzFoFnV-WiB3t95ZKksqSCiqHg1wg_S7w2DkXL67t3NnfHRh2n5al9na8S-715g7aLEMxrAbJ9hXBId8A==&c=pPxoQsluscZ28dbywosyKgiQ3Zk07qoXvEOiVYGAJfS5EvE97Lrcmg==&ch=h7suLRCDOvmm5TmNUqCVsKg0LFldonYD0cbISFjyJ2tks2g2GzFCQg==)
---
DeSantis' "Floriduh" gerrymander could make or break this entire fight for the House before any votes are cast in November.
That's why our ballot initiative campaign to allow blue state Colorado conduct redistricting is so important right now, Peter.
We can't sit back and hope that DeSantis' Florida gambit backfires--we need to get to work making sure blue states follow suit and fight MAGA head-on.
Colorado Draws The Line is leading the ballot measure to redistrict blue Colorado. Our effort could flip 2 or even 3 GOP-held seats, virtually wiping out any advantage Trump may get from Florida's gerrymander.
[This is the fight that will define the 2026 midterms. Democrats must rise to this occasion-or watch as democracy dies before our very eyes because we weren't willing to do what it takes to win. Please chip in anything you can do to stop DeSantis, Trump, and MAGA from stealing the election before a single vote is cast:](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001i5t2h4E5ApA0RYQQL_RhbZ3t-2JWCUNi-eZrstp6OooBRXfRIRnY9c7_HKlLzMSN9Pd8B8J8ho122kJj-JefiPUOL6lQzV4O3iLVb3VWnNzURmSoGQVwDYO9IbbflnrsNpVppgzFoFnV-WiB3t95ZKksqSCiqHg1wg_S7w2DkXL67t3NnfHRh2n5al9na8S-10Av8ofBvpcSg6Z96gpyfQ==&c=pPxoQsluscZ28dbywosyKgiQ3Zk07qoXvEOiVYGAJfS5EvE97Lrcmg==&ch=h7suLRCDOvmm5TmNUqCVsKg0LFldonYD0cbISFjyJ2tks2g2GzFCQg==)

name: The Flyover
subject: Powell Investigated, Figure Skating, and Found Wedding Dress
body:
The Justice Department [opened a criminal investigation](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHSnCAcxJ9GOxxBRC0o0thd2pQOyqr_DIIBTnNvn7lc8T3A1-7s7O5vfz2mhyFHfvtpDui8hsnjTg45I05nuOEc-Qsp02Yy7ErbMyJNohaK1ZxxMVlD7jXJ0uoVEGhKaN6pXvB2aIZy1wPauZFzL2ellkV1UD2sVP0sDR-gZEn6nVZzLjiv26ar7AAuesklV4toa1PIGpZyiNXvQD7vWN0rXIjVEo7S6T3nMxXNj0KMhRjv-67W8Hd-JvJI6e0pxHgGR5mWVIhxhUU0jkUkxAts2Q1F9kFlT28R2RLpIG8c-0Kn4Nm7k-1_WIQP10eKOEPMZdT2vMk5SqorZL0dhtz_dflbl15a_AsAAP__WJ6Pwg) into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, serving the central bank with subpoenas and threatening criminal indictment over his June testimony about the Fed's $2.5 billion headquarters renovation project.

# Monday, January 12, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1926, the radio show _Sam 'n' Henry_ debuted on Chicago’s WGN station. Two years later, it would be renamed [_Amos 'n' Andy_](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2O6yAQxfGnwR0WDP4sKG7jLrfd0pqYsT0RhghILL_9Kqt9g61_0tHR_-s235A9h-0_HmTLGudAZ_ZUCqX5GU_ynsObcuENC7mVt1eiPIOCTiotNcwO2V9zUzm7EgGZiqzuuwF0O5qx2u2971tqRnVXQ29WRKPBDOTcqJYR29VVbD9rSmvQWrWmr91IGoZGN7pboFUoGkWOS0z1I3IoO63-im9K9RKPytu9lGcW5p-AScB0nme9cy4xXR8XMJWds3R4SQ7yVwRMDwwvTJfUIGCKiTcO6CUeMcsgMbhLOrq_SpYxyGXnBbcoEzqOVbLuebL3nLteNGo7kP3PlfKnfm8L3wEAAP__zAGGZw) and become one of the most popular radio programs in American history. With the U.S. Figure Skating Championships wrapped and the road to the 2026 Olympics officially set, as covered in _Sports_ , all eyes are turning to the skaters poised to define the next Games. Among them is [Alysa Liu](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2upDAQBODTmAzkbv4DB5uQzaYbogYaplfGRrgHNLd_mrnCy7-qkurfY3yQeAnbX9rZ6RrHwHfyrMrneMSbvZdwcVLZSHlZZXudnEa02OQWcsBxIfHvscoWtzIjlxk7aJsOoe7LPnu6qbcEluapJq4n4BbqebZEre2qeammTNynzQIggK3Ltlh6BuwqqKCZsbZkKsuLaDyL_1GCPnn173jxWcxxz7x7qh7JlH8MDgaH-76LOaY9HtGLUvgggwMH5VNJws5BDQ7U9B1iC2Bw-PI8HfHUlJN_J8q9vHL5RC7h2-CQnW45bvFeUtOaym47if_u669Ouxz-BAAA__9KPYIt), a former prodigy turned comeback story, now skating with a joy that’s reshaping both her career and the sport itself. Former Trump administration official Steve Bannon is laying the groundwork for a presidential run in 2028. How serious is he? Listen to Ayla Brown as she breaks down Bannon’s not-so-quiet moves toward a run in 2028 in today’s _Flyover Podcast_ episode. [Watch it here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykz7FuwyAQgOGnsbdY3IEhHhi6ZEvXjuhiDucqYixMk_rtq_YV-gD_L30f13AlybIu7_Rg31IJK7_2zK1xDVt5cc6yPnlvslDjmGT5qrwHVGhPCk6AIZLkI5g--sSMrHv24OwZYZz01N_9TAn0mezkEMnQDONtsjo55RQ6GFMv_vemABBAjdoNcWLAswEDdsZRUWcUR2mlDp9F1nbnlI_y5DrM5dFnf29t2zv91uGlw0vlW6U1Dvno8LKVyN-tEvTVx-0lOctuXWfU8iDJf337F_rp8ScAAP__0YdqgA). Correction: In yesterday’s reference to Venezuelans shot by ICE agents, we omitted that the couple had ties to Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua gang, entered the country illegally, and weaponized their vehicle against the agents. This was an unintentional oversight, and we elaborated more on the story’s details in Saturday’s edition. Today’s sponsor, [Particle for Men](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u4yAQhZ8Gd7Zg8E9cUKwUucu2WyICgzMRGAvjWHn7la9y7wvc9pxPn2bOv5u-GQq0zH9NRFV80gseW8BSMOs1HRgCLS_cCs2moPM07xk3DRz6motagHaGwlu3lVMeEVBWqMTQX0B0oxyrh0I_mMGik9Dfu7azTtwdR-tG8GIcBFSkThsXAoTgnRwaN6KASyta0VvouGEtR0cl5eaZaCkP9OGdXpgbm2IV1KOUdWPyD4OJwXQcR7OaXMgG9ClHXE6MwRRWMyOD6busvbFY24wm1hjqj_OUyGkvUW9pzxaZvP40_RlHdLRHJq-rIffJrImroXk5YaufZjkfqrJy60Eh0NYPrOVzNBS-Ti6_2vml4H8AAAD__zCBlEw), makes looking your best simple for guys who don’t think about it—until the mirror gives them a reason to.
|

## Fed Chair Under Criminal Investigation
The Justice Department [opened a criminal investigation](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHSnCAcxJ9GOxxBRC0o0thd2pQOyqr_DIIBTnNvn7lc8T3A1-7s7O5vfz2mhyFHfvtpDui8hsnjTg45I05nuOEc-Qsp02Yy7ErbMyJNohaK1ZxxMVlD7jXJ0uoVEGhKaN6pXvB2aIZy1wPauZFzL2ellkV1UD2sVP0sDR-gZEn6nVZzLjiv26ar7AAuesklV4toa1PIGpZyiNXvQD7vWN0rXIjVEo7S6T3nMxXNj0KMhRjv-67W8Hd-JvJI6e0pxHgGR5mWVIhxhUU0jkUkxAts2Q1F9kFlT28R2RLpIG8c-0Kn4Nm7k-1_WIQP10eKOEPMZdT2vMk5SqorZL0dhtz_dflbl15a_AsAAP__WJ6Pwg) into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, and Powell said the Fed has received grand jury subpoenas and an unprecedented threat of criminal indictment over his June testimony about the Fed’s $2.5 billion building renovation. Powell said Sunday [the probe is a "pretext](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOtSAUhJ8GO8wBEbSg-Bu7-7dbGpSDnhsUg-w1vv3GfYXtJpnkm8z39RpfjiLty3-3oS0hjTteZ8RSMI9HujBG2j94FlpcQR9o-c54jhKk5iC4kKN3FO9RVd4GRIlNhVYY3UnR9k1frXYSGMw0g-_U1ExgApoOjO600QBh6iuyDw2EkEJA25ja9yhkp4QSepYtOKYAPZWU63eivawY4p0-mOs5bVW0aynHyZp_TA5MDtd11bdbU3paJofnDpODy4XmiE_06c3TgfvJj5wm5AE9f3AcoO1AQa_rtWyxytYfF8VIpzZMwbI5ir-T5U-ePlb-BAAA__-7bXvM)" to undermine Fed independence, stating _"This is about whether the Fed will be able to continue to set interest rates based on evidence and economic conditions—or whether instead monetary policy will be directed by political pressure or intimidation."_ The investigation centers on whether Powell accurately characterized the scope and cost of the renovation when he told the Senate Banking Committee, _"There's no new marble, there are no special elevators."_ Republican Senator Thom Tillis said he'll [oppose any Fed nominee](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuOrDAQRL8GMkbuxsYQOLjJZHPTDZEf3YxX5iHbw2j-fsX-woalkqp0ztdjftiY4rb8tyuZyvu80bskqpXyfOxvSiluJ5UaF1spcFxemcqMAodOQAc4BxvTZ5ZtMEyE1LdkQA8jgpr6qX0aRdyz1cpNgYMalUAYtXe91I4nO4Q2mmtNACCAUL2-hYkARwkSBo9K2EYKCrHu-fa9x60-idNnPynf_L62yTxrPUrT_2vw3uDdHhfAVV0h1-gTNXhnCpRt6jIVyid1Nb_Woysvd-y02c6xZD_4YRJsUY7svOrB-cl5zaDV2GYTjndMKZZBN1Isq43p97_-Sdpp8CcAAP__oHiAuw) until "this legal matter is fully resolved."

President Trump [warned Cuba](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2OhCAUBeCnwQ4DV_GnoNjGbrbd0jBwde4GxQBKZp9-477C1ic5J9_5eswPQ5729dNsqPMS5h1L8pgzxvkIBb2n_cKUaTUZ3ULrGTHNIKDjQnIJszPk33NbOb0gAjYVatl3A0g1NmP10qoDVI2xY2eWAcbnYKHrW2WEVLZVT1uRvtuElCClUE1fuxElDK1sZWdBCcNagY5yiPV3oD2_cPHvcGGsbdgqr185H4k1HwwmBlMppbbPdCvunMHk6UJ-Hs5kTAymC3f8OdEbnuO5HTyQ58VEfmNj4ptxZwwMpipqdxTynlLXs1asmyH_N5n_9dOl4TcAAP__eTN9zg) on Sunday that Venezuelan oil and money will no longer flow to the island nation after years of Cuba providing security services to Venezuelan dictators, telling Havana to "make a deal, BEFORE IT IS TOO LATE." No one tells us what to do"_ and vowing to defend the country "to the last drop of blood." ## Bob Weir, Grateful Dead Icon, Dies at 78
Bob Weir, [founding member](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGupiAUhJ9GOwwcUbSg2MbubrulOcjB_9wg_EH8zX37jfsK281kksnM9-dr_UKOnPbfeJCtIa-J7jNSrVTWd74pRk4fOivvWMkH3q9C5woSRiGVULB65Piz6tbbQATUt2SVGSdQw9zP7ctKI2dpZJgMOuxxntwW0GgIwzaMA8qW7dMmlQKl5NCbzs-kYNJKq3GDQWKjJXmuuXTfmVN9UYg_-UOl2_LRRvuq9X02_a8GlgaW-7675LbnxZM3sDyygSU7rhcWpse47MRNXMResFK4ovCEXoR8Jc9pFwcdjor4xkM4TF7wlpPwTKcwkyhbQtDaqL4t1r9vjpHP0TRa7gdy_Der_hfLj4W_AQAA__-Zuolr) and rhythm guitarist of the Grateful Dead, whose six-decade career helped define the jam band genre, died Saturday after a battle with cancer that led to complications from underlying lung issues. Diagnosed in July, Weir began [treatment just weeks](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O4yAURp8Gd1hw_UMoKFZaucs2q9GUFpgLuSNsIiCx8vajzCtM_Umfzjmf1_VqKdER_9kdTQt5PfCsCVvDst7ziSnR8cTaKNqGPlB8FKwrCJi5kFzC6i2l1zp23gREwKFDI9V8ATnpQXc3I2dQs9KTnjYvHIiw-QnBWevU6GZQHZn3m5ASpBTToHqvUcJllKOcN5iEZaNATy2X_ivT0W4Y0is_sfRb3rtkbq3dKxv-MFgYLNZtb4M-5vfMYPn4z2Bx2fETqfBYbMPwSNyj9Tzkx-HpiHzH3WHhnrBydWGw1JbLiw0LeTb8laCFHiahumL8_aSUqM6KjSLultIPRvtVu6eB7wAAAP__FWWBng) before Dead & Company's 60th anniversary concerts at Golden Gate Park last summer, which would be his final performances. The 78-year-old co-wrote Grateful Dead classics like _Sugar Magnolia_ , _Truckin'_ , and _Mexicali Blues_ , and helped build a cultural phenomenon through relentless touring and a policy that allowed fans to record and trade shows, which organically grew the band's devoted Deadhead following across generations. [See Weir's life in photos here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGupCAYhZ8GOwz8Ko4FxTZ2s-2WBuRH_10UA8yQefuNN7lPcKvTnHz5zvnzXJ6GAp3bb3OgLj4uJ9YcsBRMyxUrhkDnG3OhzRR0nrZXwryAAMWF5BIWZyh8lr5x2iMCdg1qOaoHyGHqpmbXTihvlRB2RfCj740aHeDDjkqtg-igIX3ThJQgpRi6sXUTSnj0spdqhUEY1gt0VGJq_0Y6y44-fOIbU7vGowl6L-XKrPvFYGYw11rbFG_rLZd44l1iMB-vTOt38ovWcu9gMNtoeUVK_NpjiZlvyRT0r8AdGscldEM_jTB1N9qkENd_3EZrPwzmJml3VQqBshpZL7bDUPiSKj968q3hfwAAAP__qkeIhQ).

clean_body: The Justice Department into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, serving the central bank with subpoenas and threatening criminal indictment over his June testimony about the Fed's $2.5 billion headquarters renovation project. | | | | | | Monday, January 12, 2026 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1926, the radio show Sam 'n' Henry debuted on Chicago’s WGN station. Two years later, it would be renamed and become one of the most popular radio programs in American history. With the U.S. Figure Skating Championships wrapped and the road to the 2026 Olympics officially set, as covered in Sports , all eyes are turning to the skaters poised to define the next Games. Among them is , a former prodigy turned comeback story, now skating with a joy that’s reshaping both her career and the sport itself. Former Trump administration official Steve Bannon is laying the groundwork for a presidential run in 2028. How serious is he? Listen to Ayla Brown as she breaks down Bannon’s not-so-quiet moves toward a run in 2028 in today’s Flyover Podcast episode. . Correction: In yesterday’s reference to Venezuelans shot by ICE agents, we omitted that the couple had ties to Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua gang, entered the country illegally, and weaponized their vehicle against the agents. This was an unintentional oversight, and we elaborated more on the story’s details in Saturday’s edition. Today’s sponsor, , makes looking your best simple for guys who don’t think about it—until the mirror gives them a reason to. | Fed Chair Under Criminal Investigation The Justice Department into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, and Powell said the Fed has received grand jury subpoenas and an unprecedented threat of criminal indictment over his June testimony about the Fed’s $2.5 billion building renovation. Powell said Sunday " to undermine Fed independence, stating "This is about whether the Fed will be able to continue to set interest rates based on evidence and economic conditions—or whether instead monetary policy will be directed by political pressure or intimidation." The investigation centers on whether Powell accurately characterized the scope and cost of the renovation when he told the Senate Banking Committee, "There's no new marble, there are no special elevators." Republican Senator Thom Tillis said he'll until "this legal matter is fully resolved." Trump Warns Cuba: Make a Deal President Trump on Sunday that Venezuelan oil and money will no longer flow to the island nation after years of Cuba providing security services to Venezuelan dictators, telling Havana to "make a deal, BEFORE IT IS TOO LATE." Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel Bermúdez** immediately "Cuba is a free, independent and sovereign nation. No one tells us what to do" and vowing to defend the country "to the last drop of blood." Trump also reposted a suggestion that Secretary of State Marco Rubio, born to Cuban immigrant parents, should become , commenting, "Sounds good to me." Bob Weir, Grateful Dead Icon, Dies at 78 Bob Weir, and rhythm guitarist of the Grateful Dead, whose six-decade career helped define the jam band genre, died Saturday after a battle with cancer that led to complications from underlying lung issues. Diagnosed in July, Weir began before Dead & Company's 60th anniversary concerts at Golden Gate Park last summer, which would be his final performances. The 78-year-old co-wrote Grateful Dead classics like Sugar Magnolia , Truckin' , and Mexicali Blues , and helped build a cultural phenomenon through relentless touring and a policy that allowed fans to record and trade shows, which organically grew the band's devoted Deadhead following across generations. . [](https://substack.com/redirect/f98a3d1d-e3da-4b7c-9cae-462c62fd6d0e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
It's the beginning of 2026, and we are already off to a turbulent start.
One of the few genuinely fun things you can still do at the start of a new year, especially in politics, is try to predict where things are headed. Most people are terrible at it. I'm probably no exception. But the exercise itself matters because it forces you to think through incentives, power, money, and momentum.
So that's what this is.
International winners and losers. National. State. Hyperlocal in Cuyahoga County. And yes, movies, because if you've been reading me for any amount of time, you know I care deeply about cinema and probably overthink it.
At the end of the year, we can come back and see what I nailed and what I completely botched. If I bat .500, I'll call that a success.
* * *
## International Winners of 2026
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4cb08fa2-2d21-41a0-9861-431b9b60a34a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### China
My biggest international winner for 2026 is China, and it's not even close.
With the United States escalating globally, from Venezuela to renewed and very real talk about Greenland, it's obvious we are now firmly in an energy and resource Cold War. Spheres of influence are being carved up again, not rhetorically but materially. Energy. Rare earths. Shipping routes. Strategic geography.
And the uncomfortable truth is that the United States is behind.
China has already done the hard, boring, long-term work. It has built energy production capacity at scale. It controls rare earth supply chains. It has already locked in relationships across the Global South through Belt and Road initiatives. It can build. It can plan. It can execute.
Meanwhile, the West is scrambling.
There is an urgency now in the U.S. and among Western allies to secure energy, minerals, and influence before it's too late. That urgency creates instability. And instability benefits the country that already planned ahead.
China is also, frankly, doing the West a favor by not moving on Taiwan right now. That restraint matters. It buys time. It stabilizes markets. And it reinforces China's image, relative to the United States.
When you combine China's energy production, technical achievements, lock on rare earths, and already established global relationships, I don't see a scenario where China does not continue to gain economic and geopolitical ground in 2026.
* * *
### Bad Bunny
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b97d1f16-4317-417d-993a-1934b3f82eaa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Yes, Bad Bunny is also an international winner in 2026.
He's already a global superstar, but the backlash around his upcoming Super Bowl appearance is only going to amplify him further. A lot of the criticism is transparently cultural, linguistic, and rooted in nonsense about Spanish-speaking artists and Puerto Rico blah blah blah.
Once people actually watch the halftime show, and once he absolutely kills it, a large segment of the population that previously ignored him will suddenly be paying attention.
That's how superstardom compounds.
I don't know how his profile gets bigger than it already is, but 2026 is somehow the year it does.
* * *
## International Losers of 2026
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c0acb75f-f03d-4643-aea7-b2c5210ca9da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### The U.S. Dollar
This is not a "America bad" take, so let's get that out of the way immediately before I get emails saying I need to say more nice things about the USA.
This is about the dollar specifically.
The U.S. dollar's dominance is being slowly but consistently eroded. Inflation matters. National debt matters. But so does intent. Countries are actively trying to transact outside of the dollar. Outside of SWIFT. Outside of American financial rails.
China does not want to transact in dollars. Russia either cannot or does not want to. And more countries are experimenting with alternative systems every year.
The dollar's power is leverage. It's control. It's the ability to shape global behavior without firing a shot. Outside of the U.S. military, it is America's most important tool.
That leverage weakens in 2026.
This does not mean GDP collapses. It does not mean asset prices crash. It does not mean the American economy cannot grow.
It means the dollar as a global weapon slowly loses effectiveness.
* * *
### Greenland
I originally thought the Greenland talk was just that. Talk.
After Venezuela, it's clear it's not.
Greenland has rare earths. Greenland sits in a strategic location as Arctic shipping routes open due to receding ice caps. Greenland matters in a world where China is restricting rare earth exports for its own strategic purposes.
For the United States, Greenland is no longer optional. It's a necessity.
Whether Greenland wants this attention or not is not the conversation. And bluntly, I'm not convinced it will matter.
By the end of 2026, Greenland will be under U.S. strategic control in some form, official or unofficial. That reality makes Greenland, not the U.S., the loser here.
* * *
## National Winners of 2026
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/58101572-da63-44ad-a9fb-8abebdacb711?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### Democratic Socialists of America
The Mandani win was not a fluke. It was the result of a disciplined, modern, and frankly brilliant campaign.
The early optics of his mayoralty have been strong, and more importantly, his success has shown young people that participation actually works. That you can win. That you don't have to wait your turn.
This has injected energy into the DSA pipeline nationally. More candidates. More volunteers. More confidence. More pressure inside the Democratic Party.
That momentum doesn't stop in 2026. It accelerates and more seats will be won.
* * *
### Political Consultants
Unfortunately, political consultants are also massive winners.
2026 is going to be a spending bonanza. Democrats and Republicans will fight tooth and nail for control of Congress, and consultants will extract every dollar they can in the process.
They add little value. They recycle bad ideas. They drain campaigns dry.
But they always win.
I'll write a separate piece breaking down how much money actually flows through consultants in an election year, because most people would be disgusted if they understood the scale and what these shit heads do.
* * *
## National Losers of 2026
### Centrists and Independents
If you're a centrist Democrat or Republican, this is not your year.
If you're a Bill Clinton Democrat, a JFK Democrat, an Obama Democrat, or a John McCain or Reagan-style Republican, you are politically homeless in 2026.
The Democratic Party continues moving left. The Republican Party remains firmly controlled by MAGA. Messaging, money, and media attention all flow toward the extremes.
The center gets drowned out.
Middle America will once again be forced to hold its nose and vote for options it doesn't love, because those are the only options presented.
I'm skeptical of a Democratic wave. Possible, yes. Guaranteed, no.
What I am certain of is that moderate voices will have less oxygen than ever.
Instead of kitchen table issues, expect more xenophobic rhetoric from the right and "The Message" from the left.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/2c3d4a8f-66a0-4446-81ad-226db7cba8dd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
## Ohio Statewide Winners and Losers
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/08bd62e5-c2d5-44e4-bbdd-fe7ab77454c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### Winners: Political Consultants (Again)
Ohio is about to be absolutely flooded with money, and the people who will benefit the most are not voters, not organizers, and not even the candidates themselves. It will be political consultants.
Sherrod Brown being back in the Senate race alone guarantees a torrent of national money. He is one of the last remaining high-profile Democrats in a state that has trended red, which makes Ohio irresistible to outside donors, party committees, and Super PACs looking for either a firewall or a comeback narrative. Pair that with a likely general election rematch energy still lingering from his race against Bernie Moreno, and the spending floor is already extraordinarily high before the race even truly heats up.
On the gubernatorial side, the matchup between Amy Acton and Vivek Ramaswamy is already nationalized. This is not a quiet, Ohio-only race. Acton brings name recognition from COVID-era politics and strong ties to Democratic donors. Ramaswamy brings national MAGA attention, billionaire-adjacent fundraising networks, and nonstop media oxygen. That combination alone ensures Ohio will be treated like a swing-state battleground, regardless of what the actual polling says.
Add in figures like David Pepper and broader national party involvement, and Ohio turns into a financial sinkhole. Money will pour in not because it is always strategically smart, but because no one wants to be blamed later for "not investing enough" if a race goes sideways.
This is how consultants win. Every competitive race becomes an excuse for more ad buys, more direct mail, more polling, more "strategic" messaging, and more digital spend. Most of it will be repetitive. Much of it will be ineffective. Almost all of it will be expensive.
There is a very real chance that this cycle produces one of the most expensive Senate races in American history, potentially eclipsing Brown's last race. And Ohioans should brace themselves now. By summer, televisions, mailboxes, phones, and social feeds will be saturated. The volume will be relentless. The messaging will be loud, simplistic, and omnipresent.
The candidates will fight. The parties will posture. But the real winners, once again, will be the consultants cashing the checks.
* * *
### Loser: The Taxpayer
Property taxes are up. Budgets are strained. Public goods are underfunded. Inflation continues to squeeze households.
In 2026, taxpayers will be asked to pay more to cover widening gaps, with little relief in sight.
* * *
## Hyperlocal: Cuyahoga County
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7278ce35-03be-4802-b2ec-8e848ffd9da0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### Winner: Justin Bibb
Whether strategic or accidental, Bibb's confrontation with the Browns worked.
Letting the Browns move to Brook Park while opening the door to redevelop Burke Lakefront Airport could be the biggest win of any Cleveland mayor in modern history.
If he pulls this off, Bibb enters legitimate GOAT territory for Cleveland mayors. And crucially, he avoided being stuck with the demolition bill for the old stadium.
Developers are already lining up.
* * *
### Winner (Structurally): The Haslams and the Browns
Even with lawsuits attempting to block public or unclaimed funds, the Browns will get their stadium.
If the $600 million doesn't come from one pot, it will come from another. The state will find the money. Construction will happen.
Brook Park will be reshaped. The Haslams will benefit enormously.
Whether the public does is a separate question.
* * *
### Loser: The Cuyahoga County Budget
The county is already deep in debt. Like $1 billion plus!
A new jail. Massive infrastructure needs. Brook Park redevelopment. Airport investment. Burke Lakefront. And still no serious effort to address basics like lead abatement, even as children continue to suffer preventable harm.
This is a once-in-a-generation opportunity to invest in disinvested areas. If it's missed, it will be unforgivable.
But the political will to tighten belts or prioritize is rarely there.
Debt is easier.
That's likely what we'll get.
* * *
## Movies of 2026
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/11fb27f1-79e3-49b9-9aae-5314ebcb07bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### Winners
**Dune: Messiah**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/26614319-d25d-44d8-99c5-97c3e1554a07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Denis Villeneuve has already delivered two modern sci-fi classics. Dune 1 & Dune 2. He proved he understands tone, restraint, and scale without turning the material into empty spectacle. Faithful enough to the books, visually stunning, and willing to let silence and atmosphere do real work. _Messiah_ is darker, more political, and less crowd-pleasing by design, which actually makes me more confident. Villeneuve has earned trust.
**Project Hail Mary**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a66e3281-93a1-482f-ab90-b0f91d5eca47?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The trailer did not inspire confidence. It felt safe, maybe even a little hokey. But this is still coming from Andy Weir, the author of _The Martian_ , which means the core ideas are probably strong. If they respect the science, let the story breathe, and don't dumb it down, this could surprise people. Toss-up, but I'm betting it lands on the right side.
**Disclosure Day (Steven Spielberg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bb8b0e77-2431-4d71-941d-499687939911?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Spielberg directing... do I need to say more?
He understands pacing, emotional payoff, and how to communicate big ideas to wide audiences. My only real concern is age, because we've watched legendary directors lose their edge when they stay too long. Still, betting against Spielberg feels foolish. Even a "minor" Spielberg film is usually better than most studios' best efforts.
* * *
### Losers
**Avengers: Doomsday**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a77b4873-5c9a-440c-b7e3-16e1bd7c444c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This feels like nostalgia bait wrapped in a corporate panic response. Marvel is trying to resurrect emotional equity that no longer exists. The audience that built the MCU is older, more cynical, and less impressed by CGI noise. This looks like a cash grab designed to trigger memories, not tell a story. I don't think it lands the way they think it will.
**The Mandalorian & Grogu**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6904a6ff-6f0e-4f84-8c83-1deffa954667?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

After _Andor_ , which is not just the best Star Wars story but some of the best TV of all time, this feels painfully shallow. The show devolved into episodic fluff and merchandising fuel, and now it's being stretched into a movie. Grogu is a product first, a character second. Disney knows exactly what it's doing here, and storytelling is not the priority. This will sell toys. It won't move the franchise forward.
**The Odyssey**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f20033c7-dce8-479c-8228-610ed2bba57c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I hope I'm wrong, and I'll be there opening night. Nolan is still a master of scale, sound, and spectacle. It will look incredible. It will sound incredible. It will probably be technically impressive in every measurable way.
But Nolan has been disappearing into his own head for years. His films have become colder, more self-referential, and less human. It won't be terrible. It just won't be what it could have been or like up to Nolan's historical wins. Like one of my favorite movies of all time Interstellar.
And yes, Nolan can make bad movies. The third _Batman_ proved that.
* * *
## Final Thought
Those are my predictions for 2026.
Some will age poorly. Some will age uncomfortably well. If I hit half of them, I'll take it.
Tell me what you think I nailed.
Tell me where I'm wrong.
Let's check back in December.
**Stay Angry**
