clean_body: It's the beginning of 2026, and we are already off to a turbulent start. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? We need to find other ANGRY DEMOCRATS. Please consider sharing and subscribing to fuel the ANGER for change. | | | Jan 12 | | | | | | | | | | | | It's the beginning of 2026, and we are already off to a turbulent start. One of the few genuinely fun things you can still do at the start of a new year, especially in politics, is try to predict where things are headed. Most people are terrible at it. I'm probably no exception. But the exercise itself matters because it forces you to think through incentives, power, money, and momentum. So that's what this is. International winners and losers. National. State. Hyperlocal in Cuyahoga County. And yes, movies, because if you've been reading me for any amount of time, you know I care deeply about cinema and probably overthink it. At the end of the year, we can come back and see what I nailed and what I completely botched. If I bat .500, I'll call that a success. International Winners of 2026 | | ---|---|--- China My biggest international winner for 2026 is China, and it's not even close. With the United States escalating globally, from Venezuela to renewed and very real talk about Greenland, it's obvious we are now firmly in an energy and resource Cold War. Spheres of influence are being carved up again, not rhetorically but materially. Energy. Rare earths. Shipping routes. Strategic geography. And the uncomfortable truth is that the United States is behind. China has already done the hard, boring, long-term work. It has built energy production capacity at scale. It controls rare earth supply chains. It has already locked in relationships across the Global South through Belt and Road initiatives. It can build. It can plan. It can execute. Meanwhile, the West is scrambling. There is an urgency now in the U.S. and among Western allies to secure energy, minerals, and influence before it's too late. That urgency creates instability. And instability benefits the country that already planned ahead. China is also, frankly, doing the West a favor by not moving on Taiwan right now. That restraint matters. It buys time. It stabilizes markets. And it reinforces China's image, relative to the United States. When you combine China's energy production, technical achievements, lock on rare earths, and already established global relationships, I don't see a scenario where China does not continue to gain economic and geopolitical ground in 2026. Bad Bunny | | ---|---|--- Yes, Bad Bunny is also an international winner in 2026. He's already a global superstar, but the backlash around his upcoming Super Bowl appearance is only going to amplify him further. A lot of the criticism is transparently cultural, linguistic, and rooted in nonsense about Spanish-speaking artists and Puerto Rico blah blah blah. Once people actually watch the halftime show, and once he absolutely kills it, a large segment of the population that previously ignored him will suddenly be paying attention. That's how superstardom compounds. I don't know how his profile gets bigger than it already is, but 2026 is somehow the year it does. International Losers of 2026 | | ---|---|--- The U.S. Dollar This is not a "America bad" take, so let's get that out of the way immediately before I get emails saying I need to say more nice things about the USA. This is about the dollar specifically. The U.S. dollar's dominance is being slowly but consistently eroded. Inflation matters. National debt matters. But so does intent. Countries are actively trying to transact outside of the dollar. Outside of SWIFT. Outside of American financial rails. China does not want to transact in dollars. Russia either cannot or does not want to. And more countries are experimenting with alternative systems every year. The dollar's power is leverage. It's control. It's the ability to shape global behavior without firing a shot. Outside of the U.S. military, it is America's most important tool. That leverage weakens in 2026. This does not mean GDP collapses. It does not mean asset prices crash. It does not mean the American economy cannot grow. It means the dollar as a global weapon slowly loses effectiveness. Greenland I originally thought the Greenland talk was just that. Talk. After Venezuela, it's clear it's not. Greenland has rare earths. Greenland sits in a strategic location as Arctic shipping routes open due to receding ice caps. Greenland matters in a world where China is restricting rare earth exports for its own strategic purposes. For the United States, Greenland is no longer optional. It's a necessity. Whether Greenland wants this attention or not is not the conversation. And bluntly, I'm not convinced it will matter. By the end of 2026, Greenland will be under U.S. strategic control in some form, official or unofficial. That reality makes Greenland, not the U.S., the loser here. National Winners of 2026 | | ---|---|--- Democratic Socialists of America The Mandani win was not a fluke. It was the result of a disciplined, modern, and frankly brilliant campaign. The early optics of his mayoralty have been strong, and more importantly, his success has shown young people that participation actually works. That you can win. That you don't have to wait your turn. This has injected energy into the DSA pipeline nationally. More candidates. More volunteers. More confidence. More pressure inside the Democratic Party. That momentum doesn't stop in 2026. It accelerates and more seats will be won. Political Consultants Unfortunately, political consultants are also massive winners. 2026 is going to be a spending bonanza. Democrats and Republicans will fight tooth and nail for control of Congress, and consultants will extract every dollar they can in the process. They add little value. They recycle bad ideas. They drain campaigns dry. But they always win. I'll write a separate piece breaking down how much money actually flows through consultants in an election year, because most people would be disgusted if they understood the scale and what these shit heads do. National Losers of 2026 Centrists and Independents If you're a centrist Democrat or Republican, this is not your year. If you're a Bill Clinton Democrat, a JFK Democrat, an Obama Democrat, or a John McCain or Reagan-style Republican, you are politically homeless in 2026. The Democratic Party continues moving left. The Republican Party remains firmly controlled by MAGA. Messaging, money, and media attention all flow toward the extremes. The center gets drowned out. Middle America will once again be forced to hold its nose and vote for options it doesn't love, because those are the only options presented. I'm skeptical of a Democratic wave. Possible, yes. Guaranteed, no. What I am certain of is that moderate voices will have less oxygen than ever. Instead of kitchen table issues, expect more xenophobic rhetoric from the right and "The Message" from the left. Ohio Statewide Winners and Losers | | ---|---|--- Winners: Political Consultants (Again) Ohio is about to be absolutely flooded with money, and the people who will benefit the most are not voters, not organizers, and not even the candidates themselves. It will be political consultants. Sherrod Brown being back in the Senate race alone guarantees a torrent of national money. He is one of the last remaining high-profile Democrats in a state that has trended red, which makes Ohio irresistible to outside donors, party committees, and Super PACs looking for either a firewall or a comeback narrative. Pair that with a likely general election rematch energy still lingering from his race against Bernie Moreno, and the spending floor is already extraordinarily high before the race even truly heats up. On the gubernatorial side, the matchup between Amy Acton and Vivek Ramaswamy is already nationalized. This is not a quiet, Ohio-only race. Acton brings name recognition from COVID-era politics and strong ties to Democratic donors. Ramaswamy brings national MAGA attention, billionaire-adjacent fundraising networks, and nonstop media oxygen. That combination alone ensures Ohio will be treated like a swing-state battleground, regardless of what the actual polling says. Add in figures like David Pepper and broader national party involvement, and Ohio turns into a financial sinkhole. Money will pour in not because it is always strategically smart, but because no one wants to be blamed later for "not investing enough" if a race goes sideways. This is how consultants win. Every competitive race becomes an excuse for more ad buys, more direct mail, more polling, more "strategic" messaging, and more digital spend. Most of it will be repetitive. Much of it will be ineffective. Almost all of it will be expensive. There is a very real chance that this cycle produces one of the most expensive Senate races in American history, potentially eclipsing Brown's last race. And Ohioans should brace themselves now. By summer, televisions, mailboxes, phones, and social feeds will be saturated. The volume will be relentless. The messaging will be loud, simplistic, and omnipresent. The candidates will fight. The parties will posture. But the real winners, once again, will be the consultants cashing the checks. Loser: The Taxpayer Property taxes are up. Budgets are strained. Public goods are underfunded. Inflation continues to squeeze households. In 2026, taxpayers will be asked to pay more to cover widening gaps, with little relief in sight. Hyperlocal: Cuyahoga County | | ---|---|--- Winner: Justin Bibb Whether strategic or accidental, Bibb's confrontation with the Browns worked. Letting the Browns move to Brook Park while opening the door to redevelop Burke Lakefront Airport could be the biggest win of any Cleveland mayor in modern history. If he pulls this off, Bibb enters legitimate GOAT territory for Cleveland mayors. And crucially, he avoided being stuck with the demolition bill for the old stadium. Developers are already lining up. Winner (Structurally): The Haslams and the Browns Even with lawsuits attempting to block public or unclaimed funds, the Browns will get their stadium. If the $600 million doesn't come from one pot, it will come from another. The state will find the money. Construction will happen. Brook Park will be reshaped. The Haslams will benefit enormously. Whether the public does is a separate question. Loser: The Cuyahoga County Budget The county is already deep in debt. Like $1 billion plus! A new jail. Massive infrastructure needs. Brook Park redevelopment. Airport investment. Burke Lakefront. And still no serious effort to address basics like lead abatement, even as children continue to suffer preventable harm. This is a once-in-a-generation opportunity to invest in disinvested areas. If it's missed, it will be unforgivable. But the political will to tighten belts or prioritize is rarely there. Debt is easier. That's likely what we'll get. Movies of 2026 | | ---|---|--- Winners Dune: Messiah | | ---|---|--- Denis Villeneuve has already delivered two modern sci-fi classics. Dune 1 & Dune 2. He proved he understands tone, restraint, and scale without turning the material into empty spectacle. Faithful enough to the books, visually stunning, and willing to let silence and atmosphere do real work. Messiah is darker, more political, and less crowd-pleasing by design, which actually makes me more confident. Villeneuve has earned trust. Project Hail Mary | | ---|---|--- The trailer did not inspire confidence. It felt safe, maybe even a little hokey. But this is still coming from Andy Weir, the author of The Martian , which means the core ideas are probably strong. If they respect the science, let the story breathe, and don't dumb it down, this could surprise people. Toss-up, but I'm betting it lands on the right side. Disclosure Day (Steven Spielberg) | | ---|---|--- Spielberg directing... do I need to say more? He understands pacing, emotional payoff, and how to communicate big ideas to wide audiences. My only real concern is age, because we've watched legendary directors lose their edge when they stay too long. Still, betting against Spielberg feels foolish. Even a "minor" Spielberg film is usually better than most studios' best efforts. Losers Avengers: Doomsday | | ---|---|--- This feels like nostalgia bait wrapped in a corporate panic response. Marvel is trying to resurrect emotional equity that no longer exists. The audience that built the MCU is older, more cynical, and less impressed by CGI noise. This looks like a cash grab designed to trigger memories, not tell a story. I don't think it lands the way they think it will. The Mandalorian & Grogu | | ---|---|--- After Andor , which is not just the best Star Wars story but some of the best TV of all time, this feels painfully shallow. The show devolved into episodic fluff and merchandising fuel, and now it's being stretched into a movie. Grogu is a product first, a character second. Disney knows exactly what it's doing here, and storytelling is not the priority. This will sell toys. It won't move the franchise forward. The Odyssey | | ---|---|--- I hope I'm wrong, and I'll be there opening night. Nolan is still a master of scale, sound, and spectacle. It will look incredible. It will sound incredible. It will probably be technically impressive in every measurable way. But Nolan has been disappearing into his own head for years. His films have become colder, more self-referential, and less human. It won't be terrible. It just won't be what it could have been or like up to Nolan's historical wins. Like one of my favorite movies of all time Interstellar. And yes, Nolan can make bad movies. The third Batman proved that. Final Thought Those are my predictions for 2026. Some will age poorly. Some will age uncomfortably well. If I hit half of them, I'll take it. Tell me what you think I nailed. Tell me where I'm wrong. Let's check back in December. Stay Angry Join otherANGRY DEMOCRATS by support Matt's work. **But that doesn’t mean they can’t take a bite out of your retirement savings.**
---
**Here’s the truth...** _‘Shadow Banks’ aren’t really banks at all!_ They don’t take deposits like a normal bank. They **aren’t regulated** the same way...
---
**But they still move trillions of dollars around the global financial system - quietly, and often in risky ways.**
---
| |
---
Think of it like this...
---
If real banks are like public swimming pools with lifeguards... ‘shadow banks’ are like deep lakes with no **rules** , no **ropes** , and **no one watching**. And now, **_experts are sounding the alarm_**.
---
According to the International Monetary Fund, traditional banks and institutions are exposed to over $4.5 trillion worth of risk tied to these shadow banking activities.
---
That’s not a typo.
Four. Trillion. Dollars.
---
---
| | Why it matters to you...
---
If you’re retired - **_or planning to retire soon_** \- the money you’ve saved could be sitting in accounts that are indirectly tied to this risk. We saw what happened in 2008 when unseen financial risks unraveled overnight. Shadow banking was part of that chaos then - **and it could be worse now.**
---
| | But here’s the good news...
---
Thousands of Americans are choosing to help protect their wealth by shifting part of their savings into **real, _physical_ assets** like **gold** and **silver**.
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
---
| | **Don’t wait for another 2008 to wake up to the risk.**
---
**Get your FREE Gold & Silver Kit before it’s too late****→**
---
[**Claim Your Kit Now**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
| |
---
You don’t need to understand every twist and turn of Wall Street to make a smart decision.
---
Just know this...
---
When trillions of dollars are flowing in the dark, the fallout could land in your lap — **_unless you’re prepared._**
---
So, get started today!

body:
You're not going to believe this
| | | | [](https://link.emails.bofrench.com/click/e636703/VaHR0cHM6Ly9ib2ZyZW5jaC5jb20/SZW1haWxzX2JvZnJlbmNoX2NvbTI2MDExMg/LbmNsNzM4dVE/qP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX3NvdXJjZT1uY2xfYW1wbGlmeSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjYwMTExLTI1JnV0bV9jb250ZW50PW5jbC1tRldqazRDcG5zJl9ubGlkPW1GV2prNENwbnMmX25oaWRzPW5jM3FsaG9VdzVNbHM/gaWTioQ/scp57302297)

| | Dear friend,
You’re not going to believe this...
One of my opponents has been __endorsed by Mothers Against Greg Abbott__.
Yes...in the Republican primary... to oversee Texas oil.
The Democrats realized they can’t win, so they’ve decided to run in the Republican primary against me.
When you go to vote on March 3rd, your ballot will have an anti-Abbott, Democrat-endorsed, ActBlue donor,**and then it will have me, an America-First MAGA Conservative**.
If you care about saving Texas oil, it’s not enough just to vote Republican in November. You’ll have to help me defeat this radical leftist in the Republican primary first.
If we don’t win this in March, the Republican candidate, on the same ballot as Greg Abbott, could be a vocal anti-Abbott Democrat.
And I just won’t stand for that. If you won’t either, I need your support to make sure she loses in March.
_Will you help me make sure that an America First candidate, and not a radical leftist, wins the Republican primary in March?_
If we don’t win, your choice for the Railroad Commission in November will be between a Democrat and a Democrat.
To me, that’s unthinkable.
**_With your help, we can do something about it._**
Texas Republicans deserve true conservative leadership and should never be forced to vote for a Democrat like Katherine Culbert. That’s why I’m running.

But I need to make sure that every Republican voter knows an anti-Abbott Democrat is running in this race, and what they can do about it. If you can contribute to this mission, it would go a long way to helping save Texas.

body:
Marlon and LaShonda Moore, a Frisco couple who appeared on the reality TV show _Family or Fiancé_ , were convicted by a federal jury of running a pyramid scheme that defrauded over 10,000 people of more than $25 million in 2020 and 2021.

| | |

| [](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu2zAMRb9G3hKQkmLHg4YCD9le144Cn8XERCXbkGQn_vvCadEfeOu9B-TF-fXpP0miTI-flNjV1332Ez9L5Fo5-2XPlCSUYeTEWTbOT4q_QyavQbcnwBNqH0ji7m0T3BeEHht22LVXjb3u-2Z0HfYXIrj3w9cdwbKxMCBcLUJ30Qy2EXccA0SNGtB0Z00aWiTDF9YUTFAWOEid87mOXPlF5R73eeN8HubURDfWuhRlfih9U_om08alnnni_NhfB3Lk5rbW5Mu85oGV-eBEEpVujzBxkDUp87GQhNNCuU6cyyjLv36gtJA8pjfxv8QrnCx0_q-DdZJK3pjWYIe2v1yb7MLylBiltJ2y8DgevvfW71nenP4TAAD__3v4kRE) | |
# Monday, January 12, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1861, the final edition of the pro-Union Marshall _ Harrison Flag_ newspaper was [published](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFygzAQheHTiA5mdyVjKFSkoXPalMyaXRtNBHiExjK3z9hXSPVX7yvez2W8cIhhvX_zoj6_btu4atmj5qxpfByJlyD7NOuiKTw1FY6_kngkoLYGrJFG4RCP0VXiryA9Vurx3HaEPfV9NfsO1DkQwe42AfJpAu4ZbMt41W5ytgr-jQEiIQHac0NM0CJbPSmxWDEOVELeUpNnzfri_RaP7ampmbalin7O-bEb-2VoMDSUUpq8z7ytMazabOluaPisauGjvh7vGBp0zeld605V8vIoIcawt2fj4L5wiB88_--Sp6e_AAAA___fk3Xf). Six weeks later, Texans voted overwhelmingly to secede from the United States. Texas is No. 7 among states whose residents are [most likely](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOozAQBOCnMR3IuyYEFy6uofuvvRJt7E1YxdjIWCG8_Sl5hb8ffaOZfz_zD0mU9PhLK7v6vuc58bFHrpXLvJ2FVgm7X3jlIi8uB8VnKDSjxqHV0ALOgSSec98Ed9PBQsMOrsOIYNHaZnEXtMy97_UYgLXBizH-Zj1p4BEtmUbcB9MACKjBXDsk1AOQ4QsjBRNUrzlIzaWrC1d-036PZ35x6Xxem-iWWrddmT8KJ4XTcRzdeu6UKNWcJH9CCqfPKoVTzJ6iwolKFR9Z4fQF20LpKenRIuCIuh-6bdma4sJ2SIyyD1fV68dKEr-d9XdPvRz-DwAA__9nUX1f) to break their New Year's resolutions, according to a study that found 42% of Texans give up on those goals. Are you still sticking to yours? Hit reply to let us know! Former Trump administration official Steve Bannon is laying the groundwork for a presidential run in 2028. How serious is he? Listen to Ayla Brown as she breaks down Bannon's not-so-quiet moves towards a run in 2028 in today's _Flyover Podcast_ episode. [Watch it here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykz7FugzAQgOGnsbeguwObMHjoki1dO1pH7hKsmoCMlcDbV-0r9AH-X_q-rvHKKafn45NnDXW_L_Gp7y1rrVriehSek2y3SWct6aXlzflbCkcC8ifAE1IUTvmInZUwggxoNWDvz4QDDYOdArrBO5C7jOThjAAj-rO60fWuB995m8LvDBAJCbDtG2ICj9yqU2JpxXSgkupSmjpp1Z23ez6Wl5bmtsw2h6nWdTPth6GLoUvRsfBTmnwYuqyL6F4Loy1B1nfKOW2-Nx08Zk75r6__U78C_QQAAP__ZOxrFw). As AI reshapes the global economy beyond just chips, today’s sponsor [EnergyX](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu2zAMRb9G3hKQkmLHg4YCD9le144Cn8XERCXbkGQn_vvCadEfeOu9B-TF-fXpP0miTI-flNjV1332Ez9L5Fo5-2XPlCSUYeTEWTbOT4q_QyavQbcnwBNqH0ji7m0T3BeEHht22LVXjb3u-2Z0HfYXIrj3w9cdwbKxMCBcLUJ30Qy2EXccA0SNGtB0Z00aWiTDF9YUTFAWOEid87mOXPlF5R73eeN8HubURDfWuhRlfih9U_om08alnnni_NhfB3Lk5rbW5Mu85oGV-eBEEpVujzBxkDUp87GQhNNCuU6cyyjLv36gtJA8pjfxv8QrnCx0_q-DdZJK3pjWYIe2v1yb7MLylBiltJ2y8DgevvfW71nenP4TAAD__3v4kRE) is positioning itself at the center of the massive lithium demand powering the next wave of innovation.

## Anti-ICE Protests Held Across Texas
Anti-ICE demonstrators [protested](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2unTAQhJ_GdEb2mstP4SIN3U2bEi14gU2MjWzr-Jy3j8grpByN9Gnm-_W9fCN7DsdPvMiW9x6XQDV7KoXScn8SXuzydtJFiV-UKvo_LuECCnqptNSwOGT_WbrG2VW5STdk9dCPoCeYpua0ZMa1W83o8Etto1I7rWZURF036EHD2LB9YEpr0KC0GVpAUL1GQ18E6IwTnSLHJaa2nFTojXn3n_ii1G7xarw9S7mzMD8EzALmWmsb1s3t9akFzM8fAbOPG_onxlRO-WBClr8jBxmwcAyVHck7xUK5ZIkHcshF8kZyj97HyuGQF4dAeEfPWeYzxsLhEDCbqe9HUALmJll3V_aecz-ITh0Xsv83s_yf3JeFvwEAAP__Ry6RGA) across the state over the weekend in reaction to the fatal shooting of Renee Nicole Good in Minneapolis on Wednesday. Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem accused Good of "domestic terrorism," saying that she "attempted to run [officers] over and rammed them with her vehicle." ## Reality Show Couple Convicted of Pyramid Scheme
Marlon and LaShonda Moore, a Frisco couple who appeared on the reality TV show _Family or Fiancé_ , were convicted by a federal jury of [running a pyramid scheme](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkM1uqzAUhJ_G7A6yDwEuCy-yYXGldNWqS2TwIbj1D7IdCG9fJekbdDfSSJ9mvs_LcFHGGn99U45kvs9h8LQnSzlTHNYjKmd0mhZyFM1GcVf2W0c1IMcGuACBg1bGHsOp0HLkuhMFSdE2_1B02HXFIgWKVtf1OE-i7bBTOOupaRrN1SioquvCyAeMC4ECuajaEhXyRqiKakKlK81OnLTJIZZ5oUx3lWZ7hI1iOQVXWLnkvCZWnRn2DPt930uX_KNj2JOHW2LYP04x7F85xLzAEwRTuK2WYKTFeA2jpZSMvyYwHnyAbBzBrwR4WYAp-M1MmTSMB3zd4sGwVxHOZ_H-8d-1RZR63Y21JjUtO_GrU8Y-h-a_6d0k_gQAAP__CqORbQ) that defrauded over 10,000 people of more than $25 million in 2020 and 2021. The couple faces decades in prison for several counts of wire fraud and money laundering. During the COVID-19 pandemic, the Moores [launched](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2SrCAUhJ8GMyw4_gABwU3M5qYbWmfhzEgtioWMjm-_5ey-wcbd9VV__XEbbxhiWB7_cSZbXvc0LnRskUqhPK5nxjn4zU00Uw475QPjl884goCeC8kljB5DPMe28vZTeCMrslL1GqQBY6rJatJArvGkdIcOwRjwrfL93RD6Tugq2AsmpAQJQjaqBgTRS2yoI0DfeNYK8qGkXJeJCr1wu8cz7ZRrl-Yq2qmUdWPNPwYDg-E4jtpNOS1XymC4dBgMmEtwkRgMbwJ36blG4o9niOXk0PE5xBjSwn-d-Y80BwlaKaHrdVqrbP16XL2tV6wVjxlDfI8of7tut_AdAAD__8arhFE) Blessings in No Time, or BINT, hosting weekly live streams in which they promised an 800% return on a $1,400 investment within a few weeks. Participants were guaranteed a refund if they weren't satisfied. "_This scheme deliberately targeted the African American community, exploiting cultural trust and community ties_ ," [said](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2SnTAQhE8jMlHS8B8ocEK2Th1SY80AsgVSCT203N61z76B8-6u_r4fH8sHOu_O7TsebPLnGpaTy-U5Z05LfBIeji6788HJ3ZwK-t-UcAEFvVRaalgInX-WtiLzU9GkKzZ66EfQE0xTtZtxIgsdTi3D1I9Nu1LXDO3Qsx6pa0eqnPkaU1qDBqWboQYE1WtsuGNAaki0isnlkOq8c-ZPvFb_hJtTbcNRebPnHC_RfBMwC5hLKfWv15Wd5XoLt4A5RBQwxyRgfrelDa_oWZY9yBA5YWaSznve0Mt_yPIvs8TE0obzdvYrZMN5RZfQPrK4xHJN-CKJJ1XJUCzOe3f1g2jVdqDz73v5_6TeBv4EAAD__-Frj08) Special Agent in Charge Christopher J. Altemus Jr. of IRS Criminal Investigation's Dallas Field Office. Marlon Moore, 39, is a celebrity disc jockey known as ASAP. body:

** World’s Richest Man Elon Musk Puts Lewiston on Blast as Ward 5 Seat Fight Nears Feb. 7 Charter Cutoff (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=622603c945&e=f093fa355c)

LEWISTON, Maine — Elon Musk, the world’s richest man and owner of X, has dragged Lewiston’s Ward 5 vacancy into the national spotlight, amplifying a viral clip tied to a recent public meeting and feeding a growing political storm over who will ultimately choose the next councilor after Iman Osman’s resignation. Musk reposted commentary linked [...]

Community Release: January 8, 2026
General Michael Flynn Releases Endorsement of [Hope Scheppelman](mailto:hope@hopeforcolorado.com) for Colorado's 3rd District Former Trump National Security Advisor Says Scheppelman 'is fighting for the next generation -- to restore the American Dream that the radical left has stripped away through inflation, indoctrination, and dependency.'

Grand Junction & Pueblo, CO: Today, the Hope Scheppelman for Congress campaign released General Michael Flynn's endorsement of her race to represent Colorado's 3rd Congressional District in the U.S. House of Representatives.
"I am honored and humbled that General Flynn has been so encouraged by our campaign that he decided to jump in and help us win. Like all of Colorado, we know we need more America First patriots in Congress, not more insider sellouts like Jeff Hurd who end up working for the most radical of Democrats instead of We the People," said Scheppelman.
"No one knows more about exposing the corruption, waste, fraud, and insider abuse of the Washington D.C. establishment than General Michael Flynn so I am delighted he has joined our growing campaign to restore economic prosperity and trust in our government," continued Scheppelman.
"Just as many other military veterans like me and General Flynn have faithfully served to establish and protect our great nation, we need more patriots from all walks of life to step up and join us in our fight to enforce the rule of law and expand economic opportunity for all legal citizens and taxpayers," concluded Hope.
See attached endorsement letter from General Flynn.
For more information and to contact Hope, please go to her website to donate, comment, or volunteer today: [www.hopeforcolorado.com](https://a5qvizhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001aCr0ucAdELz0CLVVZxaap9lMyyhmSVazk2byflFUt4-oSZzKXMucUrzOvhIvOqEb5HKFmHib3FzZu29cevq_TkmYV8cJZhfBPpTVoiMBY9o3SRxQ2jQy8vV2RACVHh0XpDJf4MfVLuwj0ZCcCkdPUHP4xGoWa6JN&c=MhT4GIPkof0qXdNHjy8IvDSRgUgmETO3lbvExRQ6jbtRQJKCcjZLCw==&ch=mp1A0FPv4j75mWsDbGqiyKxQ2E5tYYGtv0IDTzRLwdPCrTXirqE_Bg==)

The most important story in the world right now is the popular uprising in Iran.
This past week, tens of thousands of Iranians have begun marching against Iran's brutally repressive regime. The current theocracy has been in place since 1979, when it deposed a U.S.-allied monarch. Today's protests are fueled in party by a collapsing economy and deteriorating way of life. Inflation in Iran is rampant and food is hard to afford. One dollar is now worth 1.47 million rials, to give you a sense of it. The rial lost 80% of its value this year alone. Power outages and water rationing are the norm in a country of 92 million.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/17ba6ef1-07db-4dd7-b92f-cef522ab64c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is a regime that brutalizes girls for not wearing a hijab properly while its own officials' family members are shown living it up on social media. The corruption is endemic.
Internationally, Iran has been the primary sponsor of both Hamas and Hezbollah, and was the lone major country that celebrated Hamas's October 7th, 2023 terrorist attack on Israel. A couple years ago, I interviewed Masih Alinejad, a prominent Iranian dissident and author in New York. Iranian security forces not only arrested her family members in Iran, but the Ayatollah Khamenei regime then sent assassins to kill her in her Brooklyn home. She's fortunate to still be alive.
If you need a word for the current leadership of Iran, evil comes to mind.
Tens of thousands of Iranians all over the country are now rising up in protest. The Khamenei government's response has been savage: shooting and killing hundreds of innocent protesters, arresting thousands more, and a blanket shutdown of the Internet nationwide.
And yet the people march. Every hour now seems like it could make history. Will the regime succeed in quelling the protests, which are more widespread and impassioned than ever? The hope and courage being displayed by everyday Iranians is inspiring. The people, young and old, are willing to risk their lives in pursuit of a brighter day. It feels like this time could succeed where all others have failed.
But the overthrow of Khamanei likely won't succeed without help. President Trump has warned that the U.S. will respond to the killing of protesters. The U.S. bombed Iran's nuclear facilities just 6 months ago. The U.S. would be very well-served by an overthrow of Khamanei, as would the Iranian people and the world.
I hope that the U.S. does intervene on behalf of the people of Iran. This is a once-in-a-generation opportunity to move on from an evil, repressive regime that sponsors terrorist organizations and starves and murders its own people. We should seize this opportunity and support the will of the Iranian people. What is America for, if not moments like this one?

clean_body: The most important story in the world right now is the popular uprising in Iran. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more | | ---|---|--- * | | | Jan 12 | | | | | | | | | | | | The most important story in the world right now is the popular uprising in Iran. This past week, tens of thousands of Iranians have begun marching against Iran's brutally repressive regime. The current theocracy has been in place since 1979, when it deposed a U.S.-allied monarch. Today's protests are fueled in party by a collapsing economy and deteriorating way of life. Inflation in Iran is rampant and food is hard to afford. One dollar is now worth 1.47 million rials, to give you a sense of it. The rial lost 80% of its value this year alone. Power outages and water rationing are the norm in a country of 92 million. | | ---|---|--- This is a regime that brutalizes girls for not wearing a hijab properly while its own officials' family members are shown living it up on social media. The corruption is endemic. Internationally, Iran has been the primary sponsor of both Hamas and Hezbollah, and was the lone major country that celebrated Hamas's October 7th, 2023 terrorist attack on Israel. A couple years ago, I interviewed Masih Alinejad, a prominent Iranian dissident and author in New York. Iranian security forces not only arrested her family members in Iran, but the Ayatollah Khamenei regime then sent assassins to kill her in her Brooklyn home. She's fortunate to still be alive. If you need a word for the current leadership of Iran, evil comes to mind. Tens of thousands of Iranians all over the country are now rising up in protest. The Khamenei government's response has been savage: shooting and killing hundreds of innocent protesters, arresting thousands more, and a blanket shutdown of the Internet nationwide. And yet the people march. Every hour now seems like it could make history. Will the regime succeed in quelling the protests, which are more widespread and impassioned than ever? The hope and courage being displayed by everyday Iranians is inspiring. The people, young and old, are willing to risk their lives in pursuit of a brighter day. It feels like this time could succeed where all others have failed. But the overthrow of Khamanei likely won't succeed without help. President Trump has warned that the U.S. will respond to the killing of protesters. The U.S. bombed Iran's nuclear facilities just 6 months ago. The U.S. would be very well-served by an overthrow of Khamanei, as would the Iranian people and the world. I hope that the U.S. does intervene on behalf of the people of Iran. This is a once-in-a-generation opportunity to move on from an evil, repressive regime that sponsors terrorist organizations and starves and murders its own people. We should seize this opportunity and support the will of the Iranian people. What is America for, if not moments like this one? | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Trump’s IRS-approved retirement strategy is quietly spreading among Americans who refuse to let D.C. destroy their savings. | | | | | | | | | | Trump’s IRS-approved retirement strategy is quietly spreading among Americans who refuse to let D.C. destroy their savings. It’s 100% legal — and it’s the same method insiders use to shelter their wealth from inflation, market chaos, and government overreach. But here’s the catch: once Washington realizes how powerful it is, they’ll bury it. — before the loophole disappears for good. This could be your only chance to lock in the protection others will wish they had. Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | First Invеstmеnt Updаtеs brоught tо уоu bу Іnception Media, LLC. # **Trump weighs Iran action as revolutionary protests continue**
Iran said Monday it is maintaining communications with the U.S. **[as President Trump weighs responses to a deadly crackdown on protests](https://substack.com/redirect/5f8e80b2-41d4-442e-b12a-73a1a705eb7d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that began December 28 over economic hardships before evolving into defiant calls for the fall of the clerical establishment, with U.S.-based rights group HRANA verifying 490 protester deaths and 48 security personnel deaths, plus over 10,600 arrests.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5f8e80b2-41d4-442e-b12a-73a1a705eb7d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Trump considers military action, meeting with Iranian officials:** Trump said Sunday the U.S. "might meet" with Iranian officials as a meeting is being set up to negotiate on Iran's nuclear program, but warned "we may have to act because of what is happening before the meeting," with options reportedly including military strikes, cyber weapons, widening sanctions, and providing online help to anti-government sources, though Trump is set to meet with senior advisers Tuesday to discuss Iran options.
* **Iran warns of retaliation while claiming situation "under total control":** Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf warned against U.S. "miscalculation," stating "in the case of an attack on Iran, the occupied territories (Israel) as well as all U.S. bases and ships will be our legitimate target," while Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said the situation was "under total control" and claimed Trump's warnings motivated "terrorists" to target protesters and security forces to invite foreign intervention.
* **Iran blames violence on U.S. and Israel-backed terrorists:** Iran blamed the bloodshed on U.S. interference and Israeli- and U.S.-backed terrorists, with Araqchi saying 53 mosques and 180 ambulances have been set on fire and claiming "no Iranian would attack a mosque," while authorities called for nationwide rallies Monday to condemn "terrorist actions led by the United States and Israel," as the flow of information has been hampered by an internet blackout since Thursday.

# **DOJ subpoenas Fed, threatens Powell indictment over renovation testimony:**
Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell confirmed late Sunday that the Justice Department has subpoenaed the central bank and threatened to indict him **[for allegedly lying to Congress about a $2.5 billion Fed headquarters renovation,](https://substack.com/redirect/599b5631-9cdd-43ec-ac15-7a13602deedb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after he testified in June that the project included no "VIP dining room," "special elevators," "water features," "beehives," or "roof terrace gardens"--claims contradicted by the project's own planning documents approved in 2021.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/599b5631-9cdd-43ec-ac15-7a13602deedb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Trump claims no knowledge but criticizes Powell 's competence:** President Trump told NBC News Sunday night "I don't know anything about it, but he's certainly not very good at the Fed, and he's not very good at building buildings," while rejecting Powell's claim that the investigation was due to the Fed "setting interest rates based on our best assessment" rather than "following the preferences of the President," saying "I wouldn't even think of doing it that way" and that Powell "is hurt a lot of people" by keeping rates "far too high."
* **Powell 's term ends in May amid ongoing tensions:** Powell's term as chairman is up in May and Trump is expected to announce a replacement in the coming weeks, while Powell will remain on the Fed Board of Governors through 2028, with the controversy stemming from an ongoing renovation that Trump has claimed will cost over $4 billion total and which he threatened to sue Powell over for "gross incompetence" in December.

But let’s be clear:

That “warmth” isn’t comfort.
It isn’t compassion.
And it certainly isn’t freedom.

_**It’s hellfire!**_

History has shown us exactly what collectivism brings: government control, crushed opportunity, and the slow erosion of everything that made America exceptional in the first place.

And now, that same radical ideology is spreading.

Mamdani’s brand of “warmth” is coming to your city if we don't take a stand to defend the principles that make America the greatest country on earth!

That’s why Turning Point PAC is acting NOW.

# HELP US FINISH STRONG
---
| |
---
| | My friend, I’m reaching out to request your financial support for a critical part of our grassroots campaign—our canvassing staff.To effectively engage voters across North Carolina’s 12th Congressional District, our campaign is seeking sponsorship for five campaign canvassers, each working 20 hours per week at $15 per hour. Your support will allow us to maintain strong field, communications, and voter outreach operations as we build momentum toward the March 3, 2026 primary. Sponsorship Breakdown:
* 5 staffers
* $15/hour
* 20 hours per week
* Total: $1,500 per week (approximately $6,000 per month)

These canvassers will be directly responsible for voter contact, community engagement, volunteer coordination, and ensuring our message of faith, freedom, and common-sense leadership reaches every corner of the district. Your sponsorship would make a direct, tangible impact on our ability to compete, organize, and win. I earned over 90,000 votes in our last race, and with the right team in place, I am confident that we can expand that coalition and deliver a victory in 2026.I would be honored to discuss this opportunity further and answer any questions you may have. Thank you for considering an investment in a campaign that is serious, disciplined, and ready to lead. With gratitude, Addul Ali
Candidate for U.S. Congress

name: SAF National Polling
subject: Please provide your prompt response
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Second Amendment Foundation
body:
Demand Congressional action re: Nationwide Concealed Carry Bill

---
| | | | | | | Untitled Document [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z80nk4uofvc2e1mhua13g2btqnm72nf4ov21n888_rp22sh2s8i6eor0s9gm2ob4cth2bs) Friend,

The Second Amendment states it **_" SHALL NOT BE INFRINGED."_**

We think this is as CLEAR CUT as it gets, but the **radical gun-grabbers** want to slowly CHIP AWAY at your RIGHT to Bear Arms to **eliminate** the Second Amendment.
One of those rights the anti-gunners and the gun prohibition lobby have OUTRIGHT attacked and attempted to destroy is _OUR RIGHT TO CONCEALED CARRY.__ _
As a LAW-ABIDING AMERICAN, you have the **RIGHT to defend yourself and to carry to protect yourself, your family, and your community. **
_Will you stand with us TODAY to DEMAND CONGRESSIONAL action and PUT PRESSURE to pass a NATIONWIDE CONCEALED CARRY BILL?__ _

_**[Please record YOUR response to our NATIONAL Poll and DEMAND Congress pass a NATIONWIDE reciprocity concealed carry bill.](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zim756tr4gjr7tqmhksgu2qavsjhq18to4tmfc3o_rp22sh2s8i6eor0s9gm2ob4cth2bs)**_
**[SECOND AMENDMENT FOUNDATION NATIONAL POLL](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z1av6qru9d9s5e76drih7upmbns60fu9o6mv7l1g_rp22sh2s8i6eor0s9gm2ob4cth2bs)** **[DO YOU AGREE CONGRESS SHOULD PASS A NATIONWIDE CONCEALED CARRY RECIPROCITY BILL AS SOON AS POSSIBLE?](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zknq6cr9mafvie6i7fs1886ellpjk0sv24fnujno_rp22sh2s8i6eor0s9gm2ob4cth2bs)**

**[YES ](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zoa047e07q6vuo6f8m4vobj7hrmu2vpiqv87fclo_rp22sh2s8i6eor0s9gm2ob4cth2bs)**

**[NO ](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zsui5519nq7hi7qoabc7gdhatbachg7vm06iq1ig_rp22sh2s8i6eor0s9gm2ob4cth2bs)**

****[TAKE THE POLL NOW](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2ztd6mo9j83on5urij3j7nudmnhhe40gvq2eq0j90_rp22sh2s8i6eor0s9gm2ob4cth2bs)****
Demanding congressional action is one of the ONLY ways to get politicians to act to save our Second Amendment rights. **State after state has passed restrictive legislation banning concealed carry and worse.** Who knows how bad it will get if we don't step up now?

_**We NEED A NATIONWIDE CONCEALED CARRY BILL**_ to ensure our Second Amendment rights are properly protected for generations to come. Do you agree?

_**[Please participate in our NATIONAL POLL to DEMAND Congress pass a NATIONWIDE CONCEALED CARRY BILL to FORTIFY our RIGHT to Bear Arms.](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z9gu7g9ou08qgaks0edqq6gmfl52m65f165j5t28_rp22sh2s8i6eor0s9gm2ob4cth2bs)**_

Thank you for your participation.

SAF Polling Team
****[SUPPORT CONCEALED CARRY HERE](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z27boapsgskmi05607663m94e8s3a7gjdd57n538_rp22sh2s8i6eor0s9gm2ob4cth2bs)****

name: Kevin Kijewski for Michigan Attorney General
subject: 🇺🇸 BREAKING: Kijewski Demands Investigation into 60,000 Failed Voter Verifications.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kevin Kijewski for Michigan, 115 W Allegan St, Ste 900, Lansing, MI 48933_
body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/kevinformi/breaking-kijewski-demands-investigation-into-60000-failed-voter-verifications-4741264?e=4b10910eed)
---
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=0860d8aca1&e=4b10910eed)
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=5fb97a9d9c&e=4b10910eed)
| | | | | |
# MEDIA RELEASE
# KEVIN KIJEWSKI DEMANDS INVESTIGATION INTO 60,000 FAILED VOTER REGISTRATION VERIFICATIONS IN MICHIGAN
---
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=f6792b7e19&e=4b10910eed)
| | **Urges Attorney General to Review Handling of Social Security Administration Non-Matches to Ensure Voter Roll Accuracy and Public Confidence Ahead of 2026 Elections**
**Birmingham, MI** – Kevin Kijewski, Republican candidate for Michigan Attorney General, [submitted a formal request to Attorney General Dana Nessel calling for an independent investigation](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=08fc401db9&e=4b10910eed) into the Michigan Secretary of State's handling of approximately 60,371 failed Social Security Administration (SSA) verifications for voter registrations between 2011 and 2025.
[The request responds directly to a report published January 7, 2026, by the Michigan Fair Elections Institute (MFEI)](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=c48bc84f12&e=4b10910eed), which analyzed publicly available federal data and found that out of 192,928 voter registration verification attempts made without a Michigan driver’s license or state ID, more than 60,000 resulted in non-matches. The report raises questions about whether these failed verifications were properly resolved under federal and state law and explicitly notes uncertainty about whether required follow-up occurs.
“Michiganders deserve elections that are both accessible and secure,” said Kevin Kijewski. “Ensuring every legal vote counts starts with accurate voter rolls. These 60,000 unresolved non-matches warrant a transparent, independent review – especially with the 2026 elections less than ten months away. I’m asking Attorney General Nessel to take immediate action to provide clarity and reassurance, because proactive accountability builds trust in our system.”
In his formal request, Kijewski specifically urged the Attorney General to prioritize the matter and:
✅ Examine records from the Secretary of State, including through subpoenas if necessary, to trace the disposition of these failed verifications.✅ Determine whether any registrations were added to the Qualified Voter File despite non-matches and if such actions complied with the Help America Vote Act (HAVA) and state law.✅ Evaluate any potential impact on voter roll accuracy, including review of voting history for affected records.✅Issue a public summary of findings and recommendations in advance of the 2026 elections.
As a candidate committed to upholding the rule of law and protecting the integrity of Michigan’s elections, Kijewski emphasized that this action is about impartial oversight and public assurance. “I will continue to advocate for measures that protects our democratic process,” Kijewski added. “As Michigan’s next Attorney General, my office will prioritize independent reviews to ensure our elections become the most trusted in the nation.”
[You can access a copy of the request by clicking here](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=3ea767208f&e=4b10910eed).
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=fd63cbe8e1&e=4b10910eed)
| | [To win against the radical left's agenda, please contribute today and join the movement to take Michigan back!](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=7beb9f1a10&e=4b10910eed) I’m committed to keeping you informed and welcome your feedback. As Michigan's next Attorney General, I’ll fight to uphold the rule of law, prosecute dangerous criminals, protect our Second Amendment rights, stop political prosecutions, and defend the hardworking people of this state from government overreach.
Very truly yours,Kevin KijewskiCandidate for Michigan AG
---

The report raises questions about whether these failed verifications were properly resolved under federal and state law and explicitly notes uncertainty about whether required follow-up occurs. “Michiganders deserve elections that are both accessible and secure,” said Kevin Kijewski. “Ensuring every legal vote counts starts with accurate voter rolls. These 60,000 unresolved non-matches warrant a transparent, independent review – especially with the 2026 elections less than ten months away. I’m asking Attorney General Nessel to take immediate action to provide clarity and reassurance, because proactive accountability builds trust in our system.” In his formal request, Kijewski specifically urged the Attorney General to prioritize the matter and: ✅ Examine records from the Secretary of State, including through subpoenas if necessary, to trace the disposition of these failed verifications.✅ Determine whether any registrations were added to the Qualified Voter File despite non-matches and if such actions complied with the Help America Vote Act (HAVA) and state law.✅ Evaluate any potential impact on voter roll accuracy, including review of voting history for affected records.✅Issue a public summary of findings and recommendations in advance of the 2026 elections. As a candidate committed to upholding the rule of law and protecting the integrity of Michigan’s elections, Kijewski emphasized that this action is about impartial oversight and public assurance. “I will continue to advocate for measures that protects our democratic process,” Kijewski added. “As Michigan’s next Attorney General, my office will prioritize independent reviews to ensure our elections become the most trusted in the nation.” . | | | | | | | | | | | | I’m committed to keeping you informed and welcome your feedback. As Michigan's next Attorney General, I’ll fight to uphold the rule of law, prosecute dangerous criminals, protect our Second Amendment rights, stop political prosecutions, and defend the hardworking people of this state from government overreach. Very truly yours,Kevin KijewskiCandidate for Michigan AG | | | | | "Follow Kevin on X \(formerly Twitter\") | | | | | "Follow Kevin on Facebook" | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Paid for by Kevin Kijewski for Michigan, 115 W Allegan St, Ste 900, Lansing, MI 48933 Want to change how you receive these emails?You can or |

| | For Immediate Release | CC'd The White House, White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy, Attorney General. January 12, 2026 – Grand Rapids, Michigan Updated Statement on Why We Must Take Greenland, Like It or Not. I have heard no compelling argument that would dissuade me from continuing to advise that the United States must take Greenland—whether the world likes it or not. I reiterate what the President has already made clear: we would prefer to make a fair deal to purchase Greenland, a position and interest the United States has held since the 1800s and 1900s. Denmark has repeatedly demonstrated an inability—or unwillingness—to defend the territory, whether through inadequate military capability, manipulation to ensure perpetual U.S. protection, or both. Our Founding Fathers landed boats on the Americas, claimed the land, and we continue to defend it. We acquired Alaska, and we continue to defend it. We do not outsource the defense of our homeland and any territory to another country or arbitrary security alliance—especially when our failure to defend it would create an existential national security threat to that country. The United States does not need NATO to defend the United States. NATO needs the United States to defend NATO. We cannot fulfill that role while Denmark—or any other NATO member—acts in ways that put American sovereignty and national security at risk. Denmark may have had a boat land on Greenland some 500 years ago, but I see zero evidence of Danes lining up to join their military and be stationed there, let alone die for it. If your argument boils down to “more American lives should be risked—or more Americans need to die—in Greenland to protect Denmark, because of some arbitrary treaty,” then you clearly underestimate our resolve and must have skipped the chapter on hostile takeovers in business school. We must secure Greenland. Like it or not. As the President has said, we can do it the easy way or the hard way. Austin ChengeU.S. Army VeteranFormer Republican Candidate for Governor of Michigan For more information, contact:
The Office of Austin Chenge

On Monday, the US Department of Health and Human Services announced it will recommend **fewer** vaccines for American children. There are real-world consequences to these vaccine recommendations. There have been **_nine pediatric_********_deaths_** from flu this season after the US Health and Human Services Secretary, Robert F Kennedy Jr, halted the publicity campaign for the flu vaccine. Let’s be clear: this _will_ endanger children and lay the groundwork for a reemergence of preventable diseases like meningitis. **At Able Dems, we are fighting back against preventable childhood deaths. ✅ **Safety and security training for women candidates** \- we've launched a partnership with a specialized organization to design a program uniquely tailored to our women candidates across America. This program will help them maintain physical and digital/cyber security for themselves as well as their families while on the campaign trail.

^ _This has proven to be deeply needed, as we witnessed tragic political assassinations in Minnesota earlier last year._

✅ **A first-of-its-kind research initiative** confirming what we already know to be true: more women in government leads to better policy for women constituents. We have already collected the essential data from the past 20 years (tracking number of women in all 50 state legislatures x >3,000 (!!!!!) policies pertaining to women throughout those two decades).

✅ **Press/Interview booking in our candidates' local markets** – especially for their district's local newscasts, and in red states with lower representation for Democrats in local media.

✅ **Communications/voter outreach coaching** – This includes pro-bono debate prep and expert coaches who can help our candidates refine messages presented to voters, donors, the press, etc...

✅ **Abortion ban state prioritization** \- we have a list of target races in the states where male supermajorities had the audacity to pass laws limiting women's bodily autonomy in their states. We will be chipping away at those supermajorities in states like TN, SC, OK, TX, and many more by the end of 2026.

_There's a lot more where this came from._ [**Chip in $20.26 to be one of our first funders of 2026 and make this work possible!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123174754/594593505/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_260112receipts_N_JZ_NO_13_NG_N_L2&amount=20.26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjkyMzYxMzVjLWM3ZWYtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMGQzYTExZjkwMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=A5D7WmeOoIjp7CyCG9tFuOR6hXY3siz0_AYjWljMyCE=)

And finally... THE RECEIPTS: As we've mentioned in earlier emails, our win rate for 2025 was 98% (!!!!!!!). The work we are doing is MAKING A DIFFERENCE, and, thanks to Her Bold Move, THIS WEEK there are women across America entering the rooms where decisions are being made for the very first time. We know, thanks to our research, that this will result in positive policies for the women of America.

But here’s the unfortunate reality: Many political orgs are laying off employees, folding programs, or giving up on their women-related priorities altogether. HBM is standing strong and remains one of the only sources of support women candidates can turn to as they bravely run for political office. It's becoming more critical than ever to support brave women, as so much of the support they once depended on is no longer available.

[**Start-of-year gifts help us budget for the upcoming year and plan our programs accordingly. Help us start off 2026 as strong as possible so we can elect more pro-choice, pro-WOMEN women up and down the ballot by rushing $20.26 or more now.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hbm-pac/?refcode=EM_F_260112receipts_N_JZ_NO_13_NG_N_L3&amount=20.26)

Thank you,

Jordan Zaslow

name: Joe Jaworski
subject: Voting is a right, not a threat
disclaimer_text: paid for by Joe Jaworski Campaign
body:
For years, Texas politicians have treated voting like a threat instead of a right. They have made it harder to register, harder to vote... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/jaworskifortexas.com/last-night-14192047?e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | [ ](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=402ddf77da&e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | , For years, Texas GOP and now MAGA politicians have treated voting like a _threat_ instead of a _right_. They’re threatened by your right to vote them out of office. Instead of respecting your power at the polls, they have made it harder to register, harder to vote, and easier to intimidate people at the polls. **That did not happen by accident.** It happened because those in power wanted fewer people participating and no one in statewide office was willing to stop it. The Texas Attorney General has the authority to change that. **As Attorney General, I will stand up for voters who are harassed or intimidated, and I will hold anyone who tries to silence Texans at the ballot box accountable.** Democracy works best when participation is encouraged, not feared. Texans should never feel like exercising their right to vote puts them at risk. [**If you believe Texans deserve an Attorney General who will defend the right to vote instead of undermining it, I’m asking you to help us build the campaign to make that happen.**](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=0c63691e52&e=5d8d80652b) Onward!

**
**

name: Deb Haaland
subject: This is who I am
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
Getting here wasn't easy, Peter
Peter: **[In a moment, I'm going to ask you to make a split donation of $10 between Heartland Patriots and my campaign.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99211?refcode=em20260112-9923&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=1&akid=9923%2E614464%2EEg882H)** But first, will you let me explain who I am, why I'm in this race, and the difference your donation will make?
As the daughter of a Pueblo woman, I was taught the value of hard work from a very young age.
My mother was born and raised in a train boxcar, where the Laguna people built a community far from their home. She proudly served in the U.S. Navy, raised four kids as a military wife, and devoted more than two decades to Indian education at the Bureau of Indian Affairs.
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99211?refcode=em20260112-9923&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9923%2E614464%2EEg882H)
I am blessed to come from generations of resilient and fierce Pueblo women – **and I am the person, mother, and leader I am today because of them.** _But getting here wasn't easy, Peter, and the hard work and resilience I learned proved essential._
Like many New Mexicans, I know what it’s like to struggle. I was a single mom living paycheck to paycheck, relying on government programs like SNAP, WIC, and Medicaid to help keep my child fed and healthy. **Every day, as hard as it was, I had to get back up and keep going.**
But my struggle made me fierce. It fortified my resolve to build a brighter future – not only for my child, _but for every family dreaming of and fighting for a better life._
That's why I decided to run for office in 2018. _And I won._ I became one of the first Native American women elected to Congress, and in 2021, the first Native American cabinet secretary in U.S. history. **Now, I'm running to be the next governor of New Mexico to help move our state toward a future where everyone can thrive.**
**Every step of my journey has been possible because people like you believed in me, Peter. So please:[Can I count on you to chip in $10 or more right now to be split between Heartland Patriots and my campaign help me win, keep New Mexico blue and deliver progress for working families?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99211?refcode=em20260112-9923&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=3&akid=9923%2E614464%2EEg882H)**
If you have stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Deb Haaland and Heartland Patriots will process instantly:
[Contribute $10](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99212?refcode=em20260112-9923&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=4&akid=9923%2E614464%2EEg882H) [Contribute $25](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99212?refcode=em20260112-9923&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=25&t=5&akid=9923%2E614464%2EEg882H) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99212?refcode=em20260112-9923&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=6&akid=9923%2E614464%2EEg882H) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99212?refcode=em20260112-9923&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=7&akid=9923%2E614464%2EEg882H) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99212?refcode=em20260112-9923&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=8&akid=9923%2E614464%2EEg882H) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99212?refcode=em20260112-9923&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=9&akid=9923%2E614464%2EEg882H) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99211?refcode=em20260112-9923&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=10&akid=9923%2E614464%2EEg882H)
---
I know that it takes a village – in life, and in politics – and that's why I'll never stop fighting for our communities.
Thank you for standing with me,
Deb

name: Mary Peltola
subject: My decision
body:
[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260112_1.1Qi-7R&refcode=em20260112-1&t=1001&akid=4524%2E582294%2ExbNTqw ]Mary Peltola

Hello —

My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the
decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska — and to ask for
your support as we begin this journey together.

But if you already understand how important this race and your support
are, and you’re ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please
use this link to split a contribution today:

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260112_1.1Qi-7R&refcode=em20260112-1&t=1002&akid=4524%2E582294%2ExbNTqw ]https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_20260112_1?refcode=em20260112-1-founding-donor

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260112_1.1Qi-7R&amount=5&refcode=em20260112-1&t=1003&akid=4524%2E582294%2ExbNTqw ]Contribute »

Now here’s a bit more on how I came to this decision.

When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the
summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance.

Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity.

Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish
stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our
freezers are harder to find.

So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just crushing Alaska
families.

And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17
bucks for a gallon of milk. They don’t even believe us.

They're focused on their own wallets — not ours.

And when they actually work together on something, it’s usually to help
themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting
all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors
feather their own nest.

It’s why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All
while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted.

I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at
home.

Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our
fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can
actually afford.

Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again.

My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values
have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning.

But if we’re going to secure Alaska’s future, we need another priority:
fighting corruption.

I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our
future.

This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election,
and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we’ll be able to win
this race... and that is together.

So I am asking:

body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123176590/594599615/-939871488?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.12_Mary-Peltola-Split_B1_F1_S1_C1_90OP_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxOWJhOTNiNC1jOGVmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nveLtIKDsu7OVvlRyy5m1DTx-nlopBzQhG555Jf4pJE=) Hello —

My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska — and to ask for your support as we begin this journey together.

But if you already understand how important this race and your support are, and you’re ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please use this link to split a contribution today:

[**_https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_em_q12026_mary-peltola-split/founding-donor_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123176591/594599616/-939871488?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.12_Mary-Peltola-Split_B1_F1_S1_C1_90OP_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxOWJhOTNiNC1jOGVmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nveLtIKDsu7OVvlRyy5m1DTx-nlopBzQhG555Jf4pJE=) | | [ CONTRIBUTE >> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123176592/594599617/-939871488?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.12_Mary-Peltola-Split_B1_F1_S1_C1_90OP_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxOWJhOTNiNC1jOGVmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nveLtIKDsu7OVvlRyy5m1DTx-nlopBzQhG555Jf4pJE=)
---
Now here’s a bit more on how I came to this decision.

When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance.

Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity.

Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find.

So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just _crushing_ Alaska families.

And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. _They don’t even believe us._

They're focused on their own wallets — not ours.

And when they actually work together on something, it’s usually to help themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors feather their own nest.

It’s why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted.

I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at home.

Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can actually afford.

Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again.

My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning.

But if we’re going to secure Alaska’s future, we need another priority: fighting corruption.

I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our future.

**This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election, and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we’ll be able to win this race... and that is together.**

So I am asking:

[**_Please contribute to my campaign for the U.S. Senate and Maggie Hassan today to become a Founding Donor. We are looking for a huge number of day one donors to get our campaign off to a strong start and put us in a position to win. No donation is too small. They will all add up fast._**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123176593/594599618/-939871488?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.12_Mary-Peltola-Split_B1_F1_S1_C1_90OP_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxOWJhOTNiNC1jOGVmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nveLtIKDsu7OVvlRyy5m1DTx-nlopBzQhG555Jf4pJE=)
