name: Christina Hines
subject: We just FILED to run!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christina for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260112_filingannouncement_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260112_filingannouncement_act]
Dear team,
I am so proud to announce that we just FILED 2,000 signatures—double the required amount we needed to get on the ballot to represent Michigan’s 10th District. This was an operation led by volunteers—we had ZERO paid circulators!
This district is the only open toss-up seat here in Michigan and will be key to taking back the U.S. House. The last time this seat was open, Republicans only won by a mere 1,600 votes. That means it’s winnable for Democrats, but it also means Donald Trump, Mike Johnson, and Republicans in DC aren’t going to let it go without a fight.
If you can, will you chip in $20 to make sure we have the resources we need to take on Republican Super PACs and win this seat? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260112_filingannouncement_act&amount=]
CHIP IN $20 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260112_filingannouncement_act&amount=]
Thanks to the hard work of our wonderful team of volunteers, and the thousands of MI-10 residents ready for change, we filed these signatures early and now we can spend every day leading up to the election focused on flipping this seat!
We have the momentum. We have grassroots support on the ground. What we need now are donors who are willing to invest early and make sure we have the resources we need to FLIP THIS SEAT!
National Republicans are watching this race closely. Please chip in today and show them we’re not backing down. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260112_filingannouncement_act&amount=]
CHIP IN $20 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260112_filingannouncement_act&amount=]
Love,
Christina
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260112_filingannouncement_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cbh_email-26q1?refcode=x_260112_filingannouncement_act] Christina is a lifelong advocate for justice and a former prosecutor for survivors of sexual assault, child abuse, and human trafficking.
Now she’s running to bring that same dedication to Congress, working to put the middle class first, protect the American Dream, and ensure every community can thrive. She fights for safe neighborhoods, fair opportunities, and a future where everyone can succeed.
To learn more about Christina, visit ChristinaHinesForCongress.com [christinahinesforcongress.com] .

This was an operation led by volunteers—we had ZERO paid circulators! This district is the only open toss-up seat here in Michigan and will be key to taking back the U.S. House. The last time this seat was open, Republicans only won by a mere 1,600 votes. That means it’s winnable for Democrats, but it also means Donald Trump, Mike Johnson, and Republicans in DC aren’t going to let it go without a fight. If you can, will you chip in $20 to make sure we have the resources we need to take on Republican Super PACs and win this seat? [=] CHIP IN $20 [=] Thanks to the hard work of our wonderful team of volunteers, and the thousands of MI-10 residents ready for change, we filed these signatures early and now we can spend every day leading up to the election focused on flipping this seat! We have the momentum. We have grassroots support on the ground. What we need now are donors who are willing to invest early and make sure we have the resources we need to FLIP THIS SEAT! National Republicans are watching this race closely. Please chip in today and show them we’re not backing down. name: Secretary Patrick J. Murphy
subject: Ethos, Logos, Pathos and the Architecture of Trust
body:
What ancient philosophy can tell us about leadership and modern markets.

# [Ethos, Logos, Pathos and the Architecture of Trust](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=184323573&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg0MzIzNTczLCJpYXQiOjE3NjgyMzExNTIsImV4cCI6MTc3MDgyMzE1MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ipOM9clMMgIqty-_W3UVV_24uF9VUZy5pdSyi_nL7Mk)
### What ancient philosophy can tell us about leadership and modern markets.
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Jan 12
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=184323573&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg0MzIzNTczLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2ODIzMTE1MiwiZXhwIjoxNzcwODIzMTUyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Xg5SUxFfr7XXAr4s34xFLVkFB-4Ajl1v_V3_HhNICX0&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=184323573&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg0MzIzNTczLCJpYXQiOjE3NjgyMzExNTIsImV4cCI6MTc3MDgyMzE1MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ipOM9clMMgIqty-_W3UVV_24uF9VUZy5pdSyi_nL7Mk&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=184323573&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg0MzIzNTczLCJpYXQiOjE3NjgyMzExNTIsImV4cCI6MTc3MDgyMzE1MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ipOM9clMMgIqty-_W3UVV_24uF9VUZy5pdSyi_nL7Mk)

---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/ethos-logos-pathos-and-the-architecture?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

This week, I'll be at the Wharton School's [Future of Finance Forum](https://substack.com/redirect/16976500-4b99-42ed-b937-4655170ed4ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) speaking on a [panel](https://substack.com/redirect/c2b2fe25-4203-4e43-bf79-2be26bc03f65?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about U.S. federal debt and the long-term fiscal outlook. It's a fitting setting for a question that keeps resurfacing across markets and boardrooms alike:
Why does trust erode so quickly in moments of volatility, even when data and capital remain abundant?

The answer isn't found in any single chart or balance sheet. It shows up instead in how markets react before outcomes are known. Increasingly, what's being priced isn't just policy or performance, but credibility itself.
Recent geopolitical analysis points to a consistent pattern. Markets, allies, and institutions are reacting less to isolated events than to perceived coherence, consistency, and credibility over time.
Recent developments in Venezuela offer a clear illustration of this dynamic. Markets moved not in response to newly revealed facts, but to perceived inconsistencies in signaling, credibility, and institutional coherence. Risk was repriced before outcomes were known - not because information was scarce, but because confidence in the underlying system was. Don't forget that Venezuela's biggest oil customer is China, and China is their largest creditor (owed $13-15 billion) of the $170 billion in debt they've accrued.
This pattern is not unique. Many nations are spending way more than they're taking in. The United States, for example, is spending roughly $2 trillion per year more than it collects in revenue.
In many advanced economies, sovereign debt now exceeds 100% of GDP. In our [Hilco analysis](https://substack.com/redirect/bf427676-e9c5-41bc-bc0a-fff4f030c300?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and in Ray Dalio's recent book _Why Countries Go Broke,_ we illustrate how aging populations, remilitarization ($2.7 trillion annually), mounting infrastructure needs, populism, and a lack of fiscal discipline compound these factors. Markets today react less to isolated shocks than to whether institutions appear coherent over time. When signals stop lining up, expectations harden quickly.
Venezuela isn't an outlier. It's a modern case study in how legitimacy gaps propagate faster than formal decisions or policy announcements ever could. The same dynamics appear in currency markets, sovereign debt, and even equity valuations tied to geopolitical exposure. The most durable trust framework I've found for understanding this behavior predates modern finance and geopolitics by more than two millennia.
It comes from Aristotle.

At Wharton, each first-year MBA student is taught Aristotle's framework of _ethos_ , _logos_ , and _pathos_ as rhetorical tools. But Aristotle wasn't solely interested in clever persuasion. He was studying why authority is accepted in the first place: what types of leaders do we still believe in, do they do what they say, are people inspired to follow them, and what causes that belief to fracture.
**Ethos** refers to credibility and character.

**Logos** to logic, coherence, and reasoning that holds up under scrutiny.

**Pathos** to an emotional understanding of how human beings experience risk, consequence, and uncertainty - and speaking to their heads and hearts inspires another to act.
Together, they form an architecture of trust.
When that architecture is sound, it's barely noticed. When it cracks, everything built on top of it starts to unravel.
Across my time in public service and private-sector work, I've seen that outcomes are rarely determined by access to information alone. They hinge on something more basic: whether the people making decisions are trusted, whether their reasoning makes sense when questioned, and whether their actions show an understanding of how real people will feel the consequences.
The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..S0S0zpF9CmXky_i__SXGjrAl0WHjovyz83_obonM-Sc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=184323573)
## **On Supporting the Underdog**
It sounds easy in hindsight, but during my first year in Congress, at age 33, I coauthored legislation to end the Iraq War responsibly with a first-term U.S. Senator. We'd first gotten to know each other playing pick-up basketball in DC, but it was when I was standing shoulder-to-shoulder with him-- watching his moral courage calling for a timeline to end the war responsibly and inspiring his Senate colleagues to support our Iraq Deescalation Act--that showed me he wasn't just a show horse, but a workhorse.
When he later asked me to endorse his run for President, my closest political advisors--including our then-governor of Pennsylvania, the mayor of Philadelphia, and all but one member of the PA congressional delegation-- advised against it. They suggested endorsing the favored candidate who was up by 38 points in the presidential primary.
But it came down to trust - his ethos, pathos, and logos - which is why I then endorsed Senator Barack Obama. I was 100% positive he would win, I saw a path, but most important, I knew he was a leader of character who would put our country first.
While I didn't always agree with him politically, he made me so proud as America's leader by following through on what he said he'd do: taking out bin Laden; providing healthcare to over 20 million Americans who were without (including allowing young Americans to stay on their parents' plan till 26); and helping restart our economy after the devastating financial crisis he inherited on his first day in the White House, producing 75 straight months of job growth-- the longest stretch in American history.
Much like political public servants, financial systems have now become even more interconnected and geopolitical risk stubbornly persistent, so trust becomes both scarcer and more consequential.
## **Pathos: How Human Perception Shapes Markets**
Pathos is often misunderstood as emotion overpowering reason. In practice, it is the mechanism through which people process uncertainty.
Markets may be built on models, but they are run on human judgment. Investors, executives, and consumers respond first to confidence, fear, perceived intent, and narrative coherence well before fundamentals have time to assert themselves. Headlines move prices not because they tell the full story, but because they frame expectations. When trusted turnaround leader Brian Niccol was named Starbucks CEO in September 2024, there's a reason why their stock price increased 24% almost immediately, adding more than $20 billion in value. You can now see the progress in the new 'Starbucks Coffee Company.'
In business, you can have strong balance sheets and sound strategy, but if stakeholders lose confidence in your judgment or intentions, or ability to anticipate the next shock, the margin for error narrows quickly.
## **Doubling Down on American Manufacturing**
When I recently advised a CEO to double down on building another American factory focused on producing critical minerals, he was candid about how difficult raising capital had become. I walked him through how the current Department of Defense is committing $200 billion through its Office of Strategic Capital to critical minerals, while the world's largest bank (JP Morgan) just announced a $1.5 trillion Resiliency and Security Investment initiative in the same sector.
Ignoring pathos does not produce more rational systems. It produces systems that misread their own limits.
## **Ethos: Credibility as Strategic Capital**
Ethos accumulates slowly and depreciates rapidly.
In the Army, we often say you can get 99 'attaboys' but that one 'oh shit' moment could ruin it all. Integrity is often life or death. The same principle applies in business and politics. In geopolitical environments, credibility supports deterrence, holds alliances together, and keeps crises from spiraling. In finance, it influences how markets price risk: the cost of capital, duration of patience, and tolerance for uncertainty.
Several recent geopolitical events illustrate a consistent pattern: when credibility erodes, instability follows, even when material strength remains intact. Actors with fading trust tend to compensate through escalation, not because they want to, but because they have fewer alternatives. Markets behave much the same way. Like allies, they are attentive to gaps between rhetoric and reality. When commitments shift or explanations change, confidence adjusts quickly.
Credibility is not a branding exercise. It is a form of strategic capital--and once it's spent, it is difficult to replenish on demand.
## **Logos: Why Coherence Still Governs Decision-Making**
Logos is not abstract logic divorced from real-world consequences. It is reasoning that holds up under scrutiny and respects the intelligence of the audience.
In both finance and geopolitics, coherence signals competence. Clear reasoning reduces the risk of miscalculation. It helps others understand not just what an institution is doing, but how it's likely to behave next when conditions change. That predictability matters, especially when interests don't neatly align.
Geopolitical analysis consistently shows that volatility tends to spike not when actors disagree, but when their logic becomes difficult to anticipate. Strategies that shift midstream, rationales that change without explanation, or decisions that don't fit prior behavior create uncertainty. Incoherent strategy is often read as weakness regardless of intent.
For business leaders, the lesson is less philosophical than practical. Clarity is not a luxury. It is a stabilizing force. When reasoning is sound and consistently explained, it lowers the temperature and reduces friction, even in moments of disagreement, and gives institutions the credibility they need to operate under pressure.
Similar dynamics are emerging in domains that are harder to see but increasingly impossible to ignore. Cybersecurity - now rivaling the scale of the world's largest economies--currently the world's 3rd economy -- remains persistently mispriced or underinvested in. Not because its potential damage is mysterious, but because its risks are spread across systems, shared by many actors, and difficult to map in advance.
As with geopolitics, volatility accelerates not when threats are unknown, but when their logic and consequences are difficult to anticipate. When leaders cannot explain how risks unfold or how they intend to respond, uncertainty compounds quickly.
In that environment, persuasion has limits. The most effective arguments do not force agreement or obedience. They create enough shared understanding to allow coordination. Alignment, not compliance, is what makes complex systems resilient.
***
Bottom line: Aristotle's architecture of trust is something we can all do better at- especially those in power who need to earn our followership.
These ideas will be explored further this Thursday in Washington, D.C., at the **[Future of Finance Forum](https://substack.com/redirect/c2b2fe25-4203-4e43-bf79-2be26bc03f65?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** where leaders across finance and geopolitics will dig into what trust actually looks like when markets are volatile and the stakes are real.

If you are interested in attending, please email finance-centers@wharton.upenn.edu.
- Patrick

Doubling Down on American Manufacturing When I recently advised a CEO to double down on building another American factory focused on producing critical minerals, he was candid about how difficult raising capital had become. I walked him through how the current Department of Defense is committing $200 billion through its Office of Strategic Capital to critical minerals, while the world's largest bank (JP Morgan) just announced a $1.5 trillion Resiliency and Security Investment initiative in the same sector. Ignoring pathos does not produce more rational systems. It produces systems that misread their own limits. Ethos: Credibility as Strategic Capital Ethos accumulates slowly and depreciates rapidly. In the Army, we often say you can get 99 'attaboys' but that one 'oh shit' moment could ruin it all. Integrity is often life or death. The same principle applies in business and politics. In geopolitical environments, credibility supports deterrence, holds alliances together, and keeps crises from spiraling. In finance, it influences how markets price risk: the cost of capital, duration of patience, and tolerance for uncertainty. Several recent geopolitical events illustrate a consistent pattern: when credibility erodes, instability follows, even when material strength remains intact. Actors with fading trust tend to compensate through escalation, not because they want to, but because they have fewer alternatives. Markets behave much the same way. Like allies, they are attentive to gaps between rhetoric and reality. When commitments shift or explanations change, confidence adjusts quickly. Credibility is not a branding exercise. It is a form of strategic capital--and once it's spent, it is difficult to replenish on demand. Logos: Why Coherence Still Governs Decision-Making Logos is not abstract logic divorced from real-world consequences. It is reasoning that holds up under scrutiny and respects the intelligence of the audience. In both finance and geopolitics, coherence signals competence. Clear reasoning reduces the risk of miscalculation. It helps others understand not just what an institution is doing, but how it's likely to behave next when conditions change. That predictability matters, especially when interests don't neatly align. Geopolitical analysis consistently shows that volatility tends to spike not when actors disagree, but when their logic becomes difficult to anticipate. Strategies that shift midstream, rationales that change without explanation, or decisions that don't fit prior behavior create uncertainty. Incoherent strategy is often read as weakness regardless of intent. For business leaders, the lesson is less philosophical than practical. Clarity is not a luxury. It is a stabilizing force. When reasoning is sound and consistently explained, it lowers the temperature and reduces friction, even in moments of disagreement, and gives institutions the credibility they need to operate under pressure. Similar dynamics are emerging in domains that are harder to see but increasingly impossible to ignore. Cybersecurity - now rivaling the scale of the world's largest economies--currently the world's 3rd economy -- remains persistently mispriced or underinvested in. Not because its potential damage is mysterious, but because its risks are spread across systems, shared by many actors, and difficult to map in advance. As with geopolitics, volatility accelerates not when threats are unknown, but when their logic and consequences are difficult to anticipate. When leaders cannot explain how risks unfold or how they intend to respond, uncertainty compounds quickly. In that environment, persuasion has limits. The most effective arguments do not force agreement or obedience. They create enough shared understanding to allow coordination. Alignment, not compliance, is what makes complex systems resilient. Bottom line: Aristotle's architecture of trust is something we can all do better at- especially those in power who need to earn our followership. These ideas will be explored further this Thursday in Washington, D.C., at the ** where leaders across finance and geopolitics will dig into what trust actually looks like when markets are volatile and the stakes are real. If you are interested in attending, please email finance-centers@wharton.upenn.edu. - Patrick The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. You 're currently a free subscriber to From the Desk of Honorable Patrick J. Murphy. For the full experience, upgrade your subscription. Annual paid subscribers will receive a signed copy of my 2008 memoir, | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Patrick J. Murphy ? ?

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/aee13ab6-8efd-41ae-93b2-e274ca2fcd2c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

**My piece was published by** _**[The Epoch Times](https://substack.com/redirect/aee13ab6-8efd-41ae-93b2-e274ca2fcd2c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_**.**
What's happening in Minnesota didn't come out of nowhere.
It wasn't an accident.
And it wasn't random.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9rYXRoeWJhcm5ldHRlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.dyjr14ZohfCkLmpibj8EefbwRId9OHFs4WWVhPAYoos?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=184324133)
The line readers are responding to most is this:
> _What Americans are witnessing --large-scale welfare and childcare fraud, weak enforcement, political paralysis, and rising public anger--is not random. It is the predictable downstream result of decades of policies made by people who would never have to live with the consequences and who faced few personal costs for getting it wrong._
That sentence captures the argument.
This essay is not about scapegoating communities or collapsing serious policy failures into race. It is about incentives, accountability, and what happens when decision-makers are insulated from the consequences of failure.
You can read the full piece here:
👉 **[Fraud, Failure, and the Cost of Not Demanding Assimilation](https://substack.com/redirect/aee13ab6-8efd-41ae-93b2-e274ca2fcd2c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

name: Ilhan Omar
subject: Solidarity
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=2025%2E791427%2E0CbKKF)
Friend — Last week, Minneapolis was absolutely crushed when state violence took the life of one of our neighbors.
Renee Good was murdered by ICE after weeks of constant harassment and brutality by ICE agents across the city.
But if there’s one word to describe how our community has responded, it’s this: **Solidarity.**
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-jan26-ice.iTCV84&refcode=em260112-1-180&t=2&akid=2025%2E791427%2E0CbKKF)
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-jan26-ice.iTCV84&refcode=em260112-1-180&t=3&akid=2025%2E791427%2E0CbKKF)
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-jan26-ice.iTCV84&refcode=em260112-1-180&t=4&akid=2025%2E791427%2E0CbKKF)
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-jan26-ice.iTCV84&refcode=em260112-1-180&t=5&akid=2025%2E791427%2E0CbKKF)
From vigils and fundraisers, to marches and protests, to neighborhood watches and community organizing, Minneapolis and all of Minnesota has proven that we have each other’s backs. That we keep each other safe, not violent federal agents who don’t give a damn about their fellow Americans.
**This kind of solidarity is what frightens MAGA the most.**
The White House thinks that because they won the election in 2024, they get to do whatever they want. That America is always on their side. _That couldn’t be further from the truth._
The truth is that a majority of Americans of all backgrounds and political leanings are completely fed up with ICE and their agenda of brutality. We are ready to fight back for our democracy. We already are.
And MAGA is clearly scared: Just look at how they trot out JD Vance and Kristi Noem to take the heat for Trump.
It’s our job to make sure that the death of Renee Good, and the plight of everyone who has been affected by ICE, is not forgotten.
In solidarity now and always,
— Ilhan Omar
And P.S., if you want to help send a leader in the movement to Abolish ICE back to Congress, [**consider chipping in to my campaign for reelection.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-jan26-ice.iTCV84&refcode=em260112-1-180&t=6&akid=2025%2E791427%2E0CbKKF) I haven’t backed down from taking on Trump and ICE before, and I certainly won’t now. But that means we have to be ready for anything our opponents throw our way to try to defeat us. [**Your donation helps us build a winning campaign. Please, chip in here:**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-jan26-ice.iTCV84&refcode=em260112-1-180&t=7&akid=2025%2E791427%2E0CbKKF)
_If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your contribution will go through immediately:_
| | **[Donate $10](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/771?c0=io-em-jan26-ice.qAczJi&refcode=em260112-1-180&t=26&akid=2025%2E791427%2E0CbKKF)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/15?c0=io-em-jan26-ice.FXFM51&refcode=em260112-1-180&t=27&akid=2025%2E791427%2E0CbKKF)**
---
****

clean_body: | | | Friend — We have TWO days left before the fundraising deadline takes effect — and once it does, we’re cut off. This is our last chance to raise what we need to redraw fair maps, protect Virginia, and push back against extremist Republicans who are trying to rig the system in their favor. Fair representation is on the line, and the clock isticking. What we raise in the next two days will shape what happens next. The truth is, if we don’t act now, they will steamroll us when it matters most. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. They’re counting on us being outmatched and underfunded. We cannot let Virginia become their next proving ground. This moment is urgent. Please don’t wait. — Elizabeth | Democrat is running for Delegate in Virginia's 22nd District. She came to America as a single mother, becoming a union leader and one of the first Latinas in the Virginia General Assembly. She passed stronger gun safety laws, expanded collective bargaining rights and paid sick leave, and protected reproductive rights. To donate by check, send to: Friends of Elizabeth Guzman PO Box 74, Nokesville VA, 20182 \- \- Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman. [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71827?t=10&akid=23808%2E4449941%2Eh1eVUO)
* * *
Donald Trump is acting like a [**DICTATOR**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71827?t=11&akid=23808%2E4449941%2Eh1eVUO) and Brett Kavanaugh, Amy Coney Barrett, and Neil Gorsuch are empowering him every step of the way.

Over and over again he pushes the boundaries of the law and then [**DEFIES**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71827?t=12&akid=23808%2E4449941%2Eh1eVUO) the rulings of judges simply because he doesn't agree with them. [**It’s totally illegal, and Donald Trump shouldn’t be above the law.**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71827?t=13&akid=23808%2E4449941%2Eh1eVUO)

[**The stakes are high, team. If we don’t fight back against these authoritarian tactics RIGHT NOW, our democracy will be at serious risk of collapse. **](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71827?t=14&akid=23808%2E4449941%2Eh1eVUO)
[ **If you agree with Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson, add your name to our petition now to stand with her and fight back against Donald Trump and his authoritarian power grabs → ** ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71827?t=15&akid=23808%2E4449941%2Eh1eVUO)
[ADD MY NAME >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71827?t=16&akid=23808%2E4449941%2Eh1eVUO)
---
If we fail to act now, we could lose everything.

In this together,

Fight for Reform

name: George Conway
subject: I voted for Trump. Now, I’m running to stop him.
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac]
Peter, I’m George Conway. Yes, that George Conway.
I put everything on the line to defeat Donald Trump.
First, I quit the Republican Party.
Then, I founded the Lincoln Project.
Now, I’m running for Congress.
Peter, I know how to beat MAGA better than anyone. I know what makes these people tick. So I’m officially running for Congress in NY-12.
Will you stand with me and split a donation between my campaign and Real Texas PAC to help me raise $800,000 to launch my Democratic Campaign for Congress? → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to George Conway and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?express_lane=true&refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?express_lane=true&refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?express_lane=true&refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?express_lane=true&refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?express_lane=true&refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=25]
Look, being a former Republican isn’t my whole story.
My mother immigrated from the Philippines as an organic chemist. I followed in her footsteps and majored in biochemistry before I went to Law School to pursue my true passion – Constitutional Law.
Looking back, the Constitution was my first love. At 10, I was obsessed with the Watergate Scandal. ( Little did I know that was child's play compared to what Trump would do ).
I know that sounds boring and nerdy, but the promise of our democracy, freedoms, and the rule of law fascinates me – and for years I thought Republicans upheld those same ideals.
I was so wrong. When I finally got a peek under the hood and saw what these lying, sleazebag, horrible MAGA loyalists were doing to our country, I couldn’t stomach it.
Let me be straight with you: Republicans are failing America, and as someone who’s been fighting Trump for years, I know exactly how to stop them.
It requires not running the same plays, election after election, hoping we’ll see different results. It means being bold and not afraid to fight fire with fire – like I’ve been doing at the cost of my career and reputation.
Look, Democrats’ success last November proved there’s hope. Trump may think he’s a king on a throne, but his wannabe dictatorship is built on a house of cards, and we are this close to toppling it.
If we can take back Congress for Democrats, we can cut Trump’s MAGA agenda off at the knees. To do it, we need a fearless candidate who knows what makes the MAGA machine tick, and who can rewrite the playbook and WIN.
That’s why I’m running, Peter . I’ve seen firsthand the devastation wrought by the selfishness and narcissism of Trump’s MAGA regime. We cannot let this be our future. If you’re with me, help me launch my campaign for Congress by splitting a donation between my campaign and Real Texas PAC right now → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to George Conway and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?express_lane=true&refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?express_lane=true&refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?express_lane=true&refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?express_lane=true&refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?express_lane=true&refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2026-conway?refcode=partner_loc_gc_em_therealtexaspac&amount=25]
This is a fight for the future of our country, and I’m proud to have you with me, Peter.
Forward,
George Conway
Democrat for Congress in NY-12

Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. | | | [](https://trk.email.tedcruz._dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1FLUhvdXNlRmlsZSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249Q3J1el9JbXBlYWNoLUJvYXNiZXJnXzIwMjYwMTEyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09RW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9SW1wZWFjaC1Cb2FzYmVyZyZ1dG1fYWdlbmN5PVBHJmFtb3VudD03NSJ9/8g47wBw-CZFxqFIhQYli6w)

Judge Boasberg is a far-left, highly partisan individual who has proved unfit to serve on our Nation's bench. Boasberg committed a grotesque abuse of power when he allowed the Biden administration to hide its politically motivated spy operation against Republican Senators. He did this by lawlessly forbidding disclosure of the operation... To put it another way, Boasberg helped run cover for Biden's weaponized DOJ, allowing them to spy and collect call data from Republican Senators (including me) without the normally required legal disclosure. His non-disclosure order had no basis in law whatsoever. _Lawless judges, working with weaponized federal law enforcement to harm and harass their political opponents, is outrageous and truly dangerous. And if the consequences for these leftists are not swift and severe, our Nation will be lost._ **[If you agree with me and if you want a United States Senate that takes action against the corrupt and radical left- and brings serious consequences to their doorstep, then please rush an extra donation to my campaign right now>>](https://trk.email.tedcruz._dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1FLUhvdXNlRmlsZSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249Q3J1el9JbXBlYWNoLUJvYXNiZXJnXzIwMjYwMTEyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09RW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9SW1wZWFjaC1Cb2FzYmVyZyZ1dG1fYWdlbmN5PVBHJmFtb3VudD03NSJ9/1fnkFuM_VTC_iNXomXySXQ) ** Thank you for standing with me against the lawless and radical left,
Ted Cruz

name: Mary Peltola
subject: Alaska
body:
|
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123178197/594605361/-951542880?refcode=WUEFHM16_20260112_PeltolaLaunchSplit_PL_v1s2&sourceid=1057593&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGM1OWJmNC1jY2VmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Oxi9w1Un1XvJeCr8g6v-sZJfZJYoIPydPfWaAxCpX80=) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123178198/594605362/-951542880?refcode=WUEFHM16_20260112_PeltolaLaunchSplit_PL_v1s2&sourceid=1057593&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGM1OWJmNC1jY2VmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Oxi9w1Un1XvJeCr8g6v-sZJfZJYoIPydPfWaAxCpX80=) Peter,

My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska — and to ask for your support as we begin this journey together.

**But if you already understand how important this race and your support are, and you're ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please use this link to split a contribution today:** | [CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123178199/594605363/-951542880?refcode=WUEFHM16_20260112_PeltolaLaunchSplit_PL_v1s2&sourceid=1057593&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGM1OWJmNC1jY2VmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Oxi9w1Un1XvJeCr8g6v-sZJfZJYoIPydPfWaAxCpX80=)
---
Now here's a bit more on how I came to this decision.

When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance.

Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity.

Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find.

So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just _crushing_ Alaska families.

And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. They don't even believe us.

They're focused on their own wallets — not ours.

And when they actually work together on something, it's usually to help themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors feather their own nest.

It's why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted.

I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at home.

Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can actually afford.

Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again.

My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning.

But if we're going to secure Alaska's future, we need another priority: fighting corruption.

I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our future.

**This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election, and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we'll be able to win this race... and that is together.**

So I am asking:
[**Please contribute to my campaign for the U.S. Senate and EMILYs List today to become a Founding Donor. We are looking for a huge number of day one donors to get our campaign off to a strong start and put us in a position to win. No donation is too small. They will all add up fast.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123178200/594605364/-951542880?refcode=WUEFHM16_20260112_PeltolaLaunchSplit_PL_v1s2&sourceid=1057593&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGM1OWJmNC1jY2VmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Oxi9w1Un1XvJeCr8g6v-sZJfZJYoIPydPfWaAxCpX80=)

clean_body: | = = Peter, My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska — and to ask for your support as we begin this journey together. But if you already understand how important this race and your support are, and you're ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please use this link to split a contribution today: | = Now here's a bit more on how I came to this decision. When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance. Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity. Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find. So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just crushing Alaska families. And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. They don't even believe us. They're focused on their own wallets — not ours. And when they actually work together on something, it's usually to help themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors feather their own nest. It's why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted. I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at home. Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can actually afford. Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again. My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning. But if we're going to secure Alaska's future, we need another priority: fighting corruption. I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our future. This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election, and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we'll be able to win this race... and that is together. So I am asking: = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your split contribution will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you, Mary = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? Sign up to get less email . We'd hate to see you go, but if you want to stop receiving all email, unsubscribe . Have a question? Feel free to drop us a line at mailto:information@emilyslist.org or simply reply to this email. Contributions or gifts to EMILYs List or endorsed candidates are not tax deductible. EMILYs List P.O. Box 96612 Washington, DC 20077 United States | ---|---

name: Team GIFFORDS
subject: Thank you in advance.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
In case you missed it...
#

Hi there, it’s the team at GIFFORDS. ICYMI, we wanted to make sure you saw this message from Emma, our Executive Director.
We are about to close out our fundraising drive for the 15-year anniversary of Gabby’s shooting, but wanted to give you one last chance to chip in.
[**Thank you in advance for joining the thousands of Americans contributing to GIFFORDS PAC. ❤️**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-q12026.IDrYd1&refcode=em260112-1-b&t=1&akid=11172%2E3935704%2E1zh-z4)

— BEGIN FORWARDED MESSAGE —
From:**GIFFORDS**
To: **dpwillis67@gmail.com**
Subject Line:**Asking for a favor**
Hi friend,
My name is Emma Brown, and I’m the Executive Director at GIFFORDS. I’ve worked closely with Gabby Giffords for years, and I’m honored to help her lead our fight to end the gun violence epidemic in America.
I’m reaching out with an important favor: This week marked 15 years since Gabby was shot in the head by a gunman who killed six people and injured 12 others. As the nation’s eyes were upon her, Gabby embarked on a path to regain her ability to speak and walk, inspiring the country with her resilience.
This 15-year anniversary is an important reminder of the tragedy on January 8, 2011 — but also of the work we have to do to make sure politically motivated violence never happens again.
[**Will you please make a donation to GIFFORDS PAC today? We want to surprise Gabby by having the most amount of grassroots donors chip in as possible. It adds up when everyone is donating whatever they can.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-q12026.IDrYd1&refcode=em260112-1-b&t=2&akid=11172%2E3935704%2E1zh-z4)

name: Mark Kelly
subject: They’re not going to shut me up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by No Dem Left Behind PAC Not Authorized by Any Federal Candidate or Candidate's Committee
body:
| | [ ](https://nodem-lb.com/l/PPHR1geZViOG)
---
Pete Hegseth decided he's going to censure me, and is threatening to demote me and reduce my pension. I got shot at in service to this country. I missed holidays and birthdays. I commanded a space shuttle mission and launched to the International Space Station while my wife recovered from a gunshot wound to the head. My rank and retirement are things that I earned through my service and sacrifice for this country. Generations of servicemembers have done the same. Pete Hegseth wants to send a message to every single one that he will come after them if they say something he doesn’t like. You swear an oath to the Constitution when you join the military — including the First Amendment right to free speech. We swore to defend the foundational principles of this country. I took that oath seriously 40 years ago, and I still do. Pete Hegseth can threaten to reduce my rank and my pension. But I am not going to let him intimidate me, or anyone else. He is not going to stop me from defending our democracy from a president who is hellbent on destroying it. If they think this is going to shut me up, they’ve got another thing coming. **[And if you’re still with me, please: Split a donation between No Dem Left Behind and me so we can fight back.](https://nodem-lb.com/l/rA0RzYUoUjQ5)**
---
[ CHIP IN TODAY! ](https://nodem-lb.com/l/11mNtBMXjUrs)
---
Don’t give up the ship. Mark
---
| [ ](https://nodem-lb.com/l/PnOeeKfFT1zH)
---
| **Mail checks to:** **No Dem Left Behind** 1030 15th St NW #404 Washington, DC 20005
---
|
---

name: Mary Peltola
subject: My decision
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska.
[](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-peltola-founding-donor.Qgtl21&refcode=em20260112-2675&t=1&akid=2675%2E21716%2EAx4VAb)
Hello -
My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska — and to ask for your support as we begin this journey together.
But if you already understand how important this race and your support are, and you’re ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please use this link to split a contribution today:
[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-peltola-founding-donor](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-peltola-founding-donor.Qgtl21&refcode=em20260112-2675&t=2&akid=2675%2E21716%2EAx4VAb)
**[Contribute »](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-peltola-founding-donor.Qgtl21&refcode=em20260112-2675&t=3&akid=2675%2E21716%2EAx4VAb)**
Now here’s a bit more on how I came to this decision.
When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance.
Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity.
Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find.
So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just _crushing_ Alaska families.
And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. _They don’t even believe us._
They're focused on their own wallets — not ours.
And when they actually work together on something, it’s usually to help themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors feather their own nest.
It’s why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted.
I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at home.
Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can actually afford.
Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again.
My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning.
But if we’re going to secure Alaska’s future, we need another priority: fighting corruption.
I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our future.
**This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election, and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we’ll be able to win this race... and that is together.**
So I am asking:
[**Please contribute to my campaign for the U.S. Senate and Congresswoman Sarah McBride today to become a Founding Donor. We are looking for a huge number of day one donors to get our campaign off to a strong start and put us in a position to win. No donation is too small. They will all add up fast.**](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-peltola-founding-donor.Qgtl21&refcode=em20260112-2675&t=4&akid=2675%2E21716%2EAx4VAb)
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-peltola-founding-donor.Qgtl21&amount=5&refcode=em20260112-2675&t=23&akid=2675%2E21716%2EAx4VAb)**
Thank you,
Mary
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20260112-1&t=24&refcode2=2675_21716_Ax4VAb&akid=2675%2E21716%2EAx4VAb)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/receive_fewer_emails/?t=25&akid=2675%2E21716%2EAx4VAb)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/update_your_information/?t=26&akid=2675%2E21716%2EAx4VAb)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=27&akid=2675%2E21716%2EAx4VAb)

Email us: [info@sarahmcbride.com](mailto:info@sarahmcbride.com)
Paid for by McBride for Delaware

name: Eugene Vindman
subject: Trump is weaponizing the Department of War against me
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
|
**Revenge and retaliation — it is what Donald Trump does.**
Derek,
In just a moment, I am going to ask you to split a contribution between my campaign for Congress and Adam Schiff. This is important, especially today. Please give me a chance to explain.
We’ve found out that Donald Trump and Pete Hegseth's Department of War are investigating me over my support for Ukraine after the Russian invasion.
Here's the truth:
Donald Trump tried to get revenge on me and my brother after we reported the phone call that led to his first impeachment.
Then I demanded he release the transcript of the shocking call he had with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
The very next day this "investigation" was referred to the House Ethics Committee. Hours later Roger Stone said I should be tried for "seditious conspiracy."
There are no coincidences.
Revenge and retaliation. It's what Trump does.
This won't stop. Donald Trump will do whatever it takes to get me out of Congress because he knows I am a massive pain in his ass.
So I need your help in my reelection to Congress.
[**If you can split a contribution between my campaign and Adam Schiff today it would mean the world. Our average online donation is around $30. Many give more. Some give less. Today, I need you to give what you can.**](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=abs_em_vindman2026.I29IfV&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_TAN_GEN_AC90_20260112_AM1_V1_S1&t=3&akid=9403%2E5257348%2EgAKUi_)

body:
# [](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/188?t=1&akid=2396%2E135344%2EfDROHc)

$3.**
That is the contribution we are asking you to split between Mary Peltola's campaign for U.S. Senate in Alaska and Elissa Slotkin.
$3 because Mary Peltola can win this race. She has already won statewide in Alaska and her decision to run puts this race in play.
$3 because Nate Silver calls Mary's decision "the biggest impact of any recruiting decision on Dems' overall chance of winning the Senate."
$3 because Mary Peltola needs to get off to a strong start and cannot do this alone.
We know it probably seems like there is no way $3 can make a difference in one of the most competitive races in the country, but as you read this, people everywhere are contributing $3 of their own.
Now they're hoping you'll do the same. What do you say?
[**Can you please split a $3 contribution between Mary Peltola's campaign for U.S. Senate in Alaska and Elissa Slotkin today? She needs to get off to a strong start if she's going to have a chance to win this race.**](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-peltola.TIh7b1&refcode=em260112-ebs-peltola&t=2&akid=2396%2E135344%2EfDROHc)

Hello,
**My name is Eileen Laubacher, and I am the Democrat and retired U.S. Navy Rear Admiral running against Lauren Boebert here in Colorado.**
I was 17-years-old the first time I raised my right hand and took an oath to support and defend the Constitution against all enemies, foreign and domestic.
I took those words, and that responsibility, seriously every day for the next 4 decades. When I was an intelligence officer. When I was deployed to Afghanistan. When I became an Admiral. And as a member of the president’s National Security Council.
But in all those years, I have never felt as much of a threat to our country, our Constitution, and our very democracy as I do right now. I said I would never run for office. But in this moment, I feel an obligation to step up, serve my community, and defend our country once again.
**I am running for Congress to defeat Lauren Boebert because when she was sworn into office, Lauren took a similar oath to defend our Constitution – and she has betrayed it almost every single day since then.**
Adam Frisch only lost to Lauren Boebert by 546 votes. It was the closest race in the entire country. With the right Democrat, we can finally put an end to Lauren Boebert’s embarrassing political career – but I need your help now to do it.
Our campaign has set a goal to raise another $25,000 to defeat Lauren Boebert and take back the House for Democrats, but right now, we’re still very short. **If I am going to send Lauren Boebert packing and flip this seat blue, I need grassroots donors like you to chip in right now to my campaign:**

name: Voter Action Project
subject: Mail-in voting's new obstacle
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
HuffPost: Mail-In Voters Could Face A New Obstacle This Midterm Season [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=011226_1&amount=25]
Team: Changes at the U.S. Postal Service are going to make a known problem even worse, and it could affect thousands of voters across the country in this November’s midterm elections.
Legally speaking, the date stamped on your mail is when it counts as being sent, from your bills to your taxes to your ballot. For decades, that's been stamped the day you gave it to the United States Postal Service (USPS), whether you handed it to your mail carrier, left it in a mailbox, or dropped it at the local post office.
That's no longer the case. Your mail will be stamped the day it hits a regional processing facility , not your local post office. That could be a day, or more, later than when you handed your mail over, thanks to the Trump administration's "cost-cutting" efforts.
How does this impact vote-by-mail? If you live in a state with a mail-in ballot grace period -- something most states still have, even though MAGA is trying to end those -- your ballot still counts if it was postmarked by Election Day and received in the grace period after Election Day. In this case, however, a ballot mailed on Election Day may not be postmarked as Election Day, making your ballot automatically invalid even though you followed the rule of law and the postmark date is out of your control.
We have to make sure every voter understands this change and how it can impact them -- from vote-by-mail, to their bills, their taxes, legal replies to companies, everything. What Americans think is the mailing deadline may not be the deadline anymore and that's a huge deal that's being under-reported by the media.
Team, Every voter needs to know about this massive change. Will you pitch in $25 or whatever you can today and help us spread the word about the USPS changes so we can protect mail-in ballots? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=011226_1&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=011226_1&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=011226_1&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=011226_1&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=011226_1&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=011226_1&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=011226_1&amount=35]
We're worried about every mail-in voter getting their ballot in on time, but especially those most vulnerable to this change, like the elderly, rural Americans who live far from USPS regional facilities, our military service members, and young people who are voting by mail away from home. None of them deserve to be disenfranchised from our elections over DOGE's cost-cutting measures.
Whether you can fuel our work today or not, we appreciate your support and we hope you can spread the word about this USPS change to your friends, family, and colleagues. You never know how a quick mention of something like this news might truly impact someone else's life.
Thank you,
The Voter Action Project Team
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.

clean_body: Voter Action Project [] HuffPost: Mail-In Voters Could Face A New Obstacle This Midterm Season [] Team: Changes at the U.S. Postal Service are going to make a known problem even worse, and it could affect thousands of voters across the country in this November’s midterm elections. Legally speaking, the date stamped on your mail is when it counts as being sent, from your bills to your taxes to your ballot. For decades, that's been stamped the day you gave it to the United States Postal Service (USPS), whether you handed it to your mail carrier, left it in a mailbox, or dropped it at the local post office. That's no longer the case. Your mail will be stamped the day it hits a regional processing facility , not your local post office. That could be a day, or more, later than when you handed your mail over, thanks to the Trump administration's "cost-cutting" efforts. How does this impact vote-by-mail? If you live in a state with a mail-in ballot grace period -- something most states still have, even though MAGA is trying to end those -- your ballot still counts if it was postmarked by Election Day and received in the grace period after Election Day. In this case, however, a ballot mailed on Election Day may not be postmarked as Election Day, making your ballot automatically invalid even though you followed the rule of law and the postmark date is out of your control. We have to make sure every voter understands this change and how it can impact them -- from vote-by-mail, to their bills, their taxes, legal replies to companies, everything. What Americans think is the mailing deadline may not be the deadline anymore and that's a huge deal that's being under-reported by the media. Team, Every voter needs to know about this massive change. Will you pitch in $25 or whatever you can today and help us spread the word about the USPS changes so we can protect mail-in ballots? >> [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [] OTHER AMOUNT [] We're worried about every mail-in voter getting their ballot in on time, but especially those most vulnerable to this change, like the elderly, rural Americans who live far from USPS regional facilities, our military service members, and young people who are voting by mail away from home. None of them deserve to be disenfranchised from our elections over DOGE's cost-cutting measures. Whether you can fuel our work today or not, we appreciate your support and we hope you can spread the word about this USPS change to your friends, family, and colleagues. You never know how a quick mention of something like this news might truly impact someone else's life. Thank you, The Voter Action Project Team Voter Action Project [] [] [] [] [] Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? Peter,
My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska -- and to ask for your support as we begin this journey together.
But if you already understand how important this race and your support are, and you're ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please use this link to split a contribution today:
[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdtl-peltola/founding-donor](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hsU2y6pPuYHtjqBVMm0xvlcXFZwP7xmD3Ppr072sa7Fljfo6ILqV27TGp1fdfnGIMTX_ddGEycwQ0YVHwJ5p8CBo5DE-E474nct7L0rVsy-QAjfZhQVpF-CGCs2OBYFMTVX3shA54bd3SQcOCGxtDaOINehda1RYB7wg0-rh79eFPbdbZYJWCVVtCuoxHpT86LsgDCC9GUjCjvKOVT_qvYhK6pk3wdpPEAMfJUDX4lI=&c=bIoQWXUKbNScA9b24ZyCa4N9lZVkje_isn714XNoKzM-mKTnFko31Q==&ch=u6N6hPSdxqJgW2qhVT2xquG3mhnzbag2PHVIzuSmpVYQX4z0M6b5ag==)
---
| [CONTRIBUTE](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hsU2y6pPuYHtjqBVMm0xvlcXFZwP7xmD3Ppr072sa7Fljfo6ILqV27TGp1fdfnGIMTX_ddGEycwQ0YVHwJ5p8CBo5DE-E474nct7L0rVsy-QAjfZhQVpF-CGCs2OBYFMTVX3shA54bd3SQcOCGxtDaOINehda1RYB7wg0-rh79eFPbdbZYJWCVVtCuoxHpT86LsgDCC9GUjCjvKOVT_qvTQ7sDWoWEzoibSz1cDabT8=&c=bIoQWXUKbNScA9b24ZyCa4N9lZVkje_isn714XNoKzM-mKTnFko31Q==&ch=u6N6hPSdxqJgW2qhVT2xquG3mhnzbag2PHVIzuSmpVYQX4z0M6b5ag==)
---
Now here's a bit more on how I came to this decision.
When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance.
Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity.
Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find.
So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just crushing Alaska families.
And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. They don't even believe us.
They're focused on their own wallets -- not ours.
And when they actually work together on something, it's usually to help themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors feather their own nest.
It's why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted.
I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at home.
Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can actually afford.
Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again.
My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning.
But if we're going to secure Alaska's future, we need another priority: fighting corruption.
I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our future.
This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election, and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we'll be able to win this race... and that is together.
So I am asking:
[Please contribute to my campaign for the U.S. Senate and Colorado Draws the Line today to become a Founding Donor. We are looking for a huge number of day one donors to get our campaign off to a strong start and put us in a position to win. No donation is too small. They will all add up fast. ](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hsU2y6pPuYHtjqBVMm0xvlcXFZwP7xmD3Ppr072sa7Fljfo6ILqV27TGp1fdfnGIMTX_ddGEycwQ0YVHwJ5p8CBo5DE-E474nct7L0rVsy-QAjfZhQVpF-CGCs2OBYFMTVX3shA54bd3SQcOCGxtDaOINehda1RYB7wg0-rh79eFPbdbZYJWCVVtCuoxHpT86LsgDCC9GUjCjvKOVT_qvf5qL31lAGo1EsWSNxmtrDc=&c=bIoQWXUKbNScA9b24ZyCa4N9lZVkje_isn714XNoKzM-mKTnFko31Q==&ch=u6N6hPSdxqJgW2qhVT2xquG3mhnzbag2PHVIzuSmpVYQX4z0M6b5ag==)

body:
Mary Peltola’s decision puts the Senate in play.

**This is the race that may determine control of the Senate.**
Good morning - I am writing to ask if there is anything I can say to convince you to contribute to Mary Peltola's campaign for United States Senate in Alaska.
If you understand how exciting it is that Mary decided to run for the Senate, how it puts control of the Senate in play, and are ready to help her get her campaign off to the fast start she needs — use this link to split a donation between her campaign and my Fight for the People PAC today.
[**https://act.kamalaharris.com/go/peltola-senate**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-peltola?refcode=em260112-a1&refcode2=893_2563979_3FkDhe&akid=893%2E2563979%2E3FkDhe)
Four years ago, Mary Peltola made history by winning a statewide election and becoming the first woman to represent Alaska in the House of Representatives AND the first Alaska Native member of Congress.
For months, she considered whether or not to run for Alaska's Senate seat this cycle, and today she announced her decision: She's in.
Mary's decision puts this race and Democratic control of the Senate in play. But in a short race, it's critical she gets off to a strong start. So I am asking:
$5 to help Mary Peltola flip Alaska Senate seat in the next election.
$5 because if she wins it means Democrats have a great chance to take back the Senate and put a real check on Trump's presidency.
$5 because you will love Senator Mary Peltola if she wins.
But the response today is critical:
[**Please use this link to contribute $5 or whatever you can afford between Mary Peltola's campaign for U.S. Senate and my PAC. It is so important Mary gets her campaign off to a strong start.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-peltola?refcode=em260112-a1&refcode2=893_2563979_3FkDhe&akid=893%2E2563979%2E3FkDhe)

name: Mary Peltola
subject: I am running for U.S. Senate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39516?t=1008&amount=25&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Mary Peltola Fishing

Peter,

My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the
decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska — and to ask for
your support as we begin this journey together.

But if you already understand how important this race and your support
are, and you’re ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please
use this link to split a contribution today:

[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39516?t=1009&amount=25&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Contribute

Now here’s a bit more on how I came to this decision.

When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the
summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance.

Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity.

Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish
stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our
freezers are harder to find.

So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just crushing Alaska
families.

And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17
bucks for a gallon of milk. They don’t even believe us.

They're focused on their own wallets — not ours.

And when they actually work together on something, it’s usually to help
themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting
all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors
feather their own nest.

It’s why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All
while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted.

I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at
home.

Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our
fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can
actually afford.

Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again.

My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values
have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning.

But if we’re going to secure Alaska’s future, we need another priority:
fighting corruption.

I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our
future.

This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election,
and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we’ll be able to win
this race...and that is together.

So I am asking:

[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39516?t=1010&amount=25&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Please contribute to my campaign for the U.S. Senate and Senator Angela
Alsobrooks today to become a Founding Donor. We are looking for a huge
number of day one donors to get our campaign off to a strong start and put
us in a position to win. No donation is too small. They will all add up
fast.

[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39516?t=1011&amount=10&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Chip in $10 [ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39516?t=1012&amount=25&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Chip in $25
[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39516?t=1013&amount=50&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Chip in $50 [ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39516?t=1014&amount=100&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Chip in $100
[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39516?t=1015&amount=200&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Chip in $200 [ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39516?t=1007&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Custom Amount

Thank you,

Mary

[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/89?t=1001&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ][IMG]

An important update from Team Alsobrooks:

Angela is so thankful for this grassroots team’s support and is eager to
get to work for Marylanders in the Senate. That’s why you’ll be receiving
fewer fundraising emails from us moving forward, in an effort to not clog
up your inbox for the next six years.

Instead, we’d rather send you emails you want to read – like updates on
Angela’s work in the Senate, or rundowns of key issues and news stories
from Washington.

But to make this possible, we’re relying on supporters like you to
consider making a small, monthly recurring contribution of $3 or even $5
to keep our operations running while Angela is busy fighting for Maryland
families.

[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/33703?t=1002&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Make a recurring contribution to Angela Alsobrooks' campaign here.
We believe that emails are the best way for our organization to stay in
touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails,
[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=1003&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]click here.
[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4693?t=1004&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Chip in a one-time donation
[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4694?t=1005&akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3 ]Chip in a monthly recurring donation!

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=3428%2E52803%2ExeIHC3

Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
Alsobrooks for Senate
PO Box 6058
Largo, MD 20792

name: TN House Democratic Caucus
subject: Tomorrow is the Deadline
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_
body:
We'll keep this short because we know you’ve heard a lot from us recently.
| | | | Can't see this email? [View in Your Browser](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESGTJD0PTKGRF5XPFR8WFED_0)
---
| | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESGTJD0PTKGRF5XPFR8WFED_1)
---
| | | | Dear Peter, We'll keep this short because we know you’ve heard a lot from us recently.

Tomorrow, January 13th, our ability to accept contributions comes to an end. At noon, campaign finance restrictions take effect and we are prohibited from financial contributions until the legislature adjourns several months from now.

What we raise in the next 24 hours will directly impact our ability to fight for our Democratic priorities and hold Bill Lee and the Republican establishment accountable.
---
| | [ MAKE YOUR PRE-SESSION CONTRIBUTION ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESGTJD0PTKGRF5XPFR8WFED_2)
---
| We are so grateful for the generosity of supporters like you who have stepped up over the past week. If you haven't yet had a chance to contribute, we hope you'll consider doing so today.

[This isn't about meeting some arbitrary goal. It's about having the resources to continue doing the work and consistently communicating our message. Our members are heading into session ready to fight. Your support ensures they have what they need to be effective.](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESGTJD0PTKGRF5XPFR8WFED_3)

**The deadline is tomorrow. Please give today if you can.**
---
| | [ MAKE YOUR PRE-SESSION CONTRIBUTION ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESGTJD0PTKGRF5XPFR8WFED_4)
---
| From all of us at the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, thank you. We are honored by your trust and support.

With gratitude, **Tennessee House Democratic Caucus**
---
| | | |
---
| _Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_
---
| [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESGTJD0PTKGRF5XPFR8WFED_5) [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESGTJD0PTKGRF5XPFR8WFED_6) [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESGTJD0PTKGRF5XPFR8WFED_7)
---
| No longer want to receive these emails? [Unsubscribe](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESGTJD0PTKGRF5XPFR8WFED_8).
TN House Democratic Caucus PO Box 198082 Nashville, TN 37219
---

Remember: 2026 is an election year, so I’ll be on the ballot. Banner campaign years like next year are never cheap, but our race is going to be especially tough.
We are one of Republicans’ top targets this year because this seat has flipped parties between every governor for the last twenty years. I flipped it from a Republican — _and now Donald Trump’s allies want to flip it right back._
Republicans are cashing in every favor they can to win in Maryland. They’re calling up old candidates, running focus groups, doing opposition research, and preparing to funnel millions into whoever they think has the best shot at flipping this seat back to red.
Our progress is on the line in Maryland this year. And whether we have the resources to fight back depends on what we achieve before our January 14th deadline.
[We can’t afford to play catch up while the Republicans are giving this race their all. Can I count on you to split a donation of $5 or anything you can between the Democratic Party of Georgia and me today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123184803/594624770/1210879453?amount=5&refcode=partner_ms_wes_em_GeorgiaDems&source=DPG_EM_FR_011226_B1_WESMOORESPLIT_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1OTM5ZTliYy1kNWVmLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wNo5Zxqafow4gQ1W3TlUZMmTYxFQjbaIfuiVARlvoFM=)
