name: Jackson Lahmeyer
subject: The Islamification Of Oklahoma
body:
The Islamification of Oklahoma... I want to make you aware of an important issue affecting our community and encourage your participation. Tonight,
| | |
---
**The Islamification of Oklahoma...** I want to make you aware of an _important issue_ affecting our community and encourage your participation. Tonight, the **Broken Arrow City Council** will hear matters related to a proposed **Islamic mosque** development. This meeting matters because of what is being proposed to be built, how it impacts our area, and whether leaders are asking the right questions before approving major cultural changes. Across Oklahoma and the nation, we are seeing a steady expansion of Islam. While America guarantees freedom of religion, it also requires local governments to carefully evaluate land use, traffic impact, transparency, and long-term community effects. These conversations must happen and it must be noted that Islam is much more than a mere religion. Islam is an oppressive and regressive political system that is incompatible with Western Civilization. This is not about hatred or fear. It is about wisdom, accountability, and protecting the character of our community. Citizens have every right to ask: How will this development affect surrounding neighborhoods? Who is funding it, and with what oversight? Will it comply fully with local laws and community standards? Healthy communities are built when citizens show up, speak calmly, and insist on transparency. I encourage you—if you are able—to attend the **Broken Arrow City Council** meeting tonight or to stay informed and engaged in the process. We can be firm without being hateful. Courageous without being cruel. And faithful while remaining respectful. Let’s pray for our city leaders, for wisdom in decision-making, and for peace rooted in truth. **Date & Time**:
Monday, January 12, 2026 at 6:00 p.m. **Location** :
The meeting will be held at the NSU-Broken Arrow Administrative Services Building (Northeastern State University-BA campus) — not in the usual City Hall chambers. Thank you, Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer
---
|
---
It is FINALLY here! I have been very busy signing all the pre-orders for my newest book entitled '[DIVIDED: BREAKING FREE FROM THE DOUBLE LIFE](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=27443925.14086.1.35bcc0aa15255d0ed3b97630d7fc8d80).' **Pastor Paula White-Cain** wrote an _incredible_ Foreword and **Rabbi Jonathan Cahn** gave an _amazing_ Endorsement. In this, I open up about _my own personal struggles_ as a follower of Jesus and a Pastor. I share things that I've never shared publicly in hopes that it will speak to people who might be ashamed with the different struggles they have even as Christians and how to have victory. There are two of us in all of us but we're not destined to live **DIVIDED** forever. You can visit: [https://clr.cm/qPKQ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=27443925.14087.1.00f4719e38a10c15605bee85bc271817)to get your SIGNED copy and start reading immediately with the **FREE e-book** that's included. You can also get a copy on [Amazon](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=27443925.14088.1.ae832b5c5283a63364f4da3d16ca0e5e), [Barnes & Noble](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=27443925.14089.1.ab9c6352c3260ee34b7ab2bdb12d1cca), and all other book retailers as well. I believe this book will help many people overcome the double life that far too many of us live. Also, join me on NEWSMAX tonight at 8:45pm EST with David Harris Jr. Thank you -Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer

Every one of us lives with tension - the tug-of-war between the two of us living in all of us. In DIVIDED, Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer gets real, raw, and deeply relatable as he opens up about his own struggles with pride, anger, and failure while chasing God's purpose. Through humor, honesty, and hard-learned wisdom, Lahmeyer invites readers to drop the mask, confront the "dark me" that keeps surfacing, and experience the transforming power of grace. This is not a polished preacher's pep talk-it's a gut-level, heart-to-heart conversation about what it means to follow Jesus when life gets messy. Drawing from the lives of biblical characters like Peter, Jacob and Paul, Lahmeyer shows that even the greatest heroes of faith had moments of weakness, doubt, and defeat. He unpacks the difference between remorse and repentance, explores how belief shapes behavior, and reveals how identity-not effort-is the key to lasting transformation. With vivid stories, relatable honesty, and the truth of Scripture, he reminds us that God isn't looking for the perfect version of you-He's after the real you. Because the moment you get real with God is the moment He starts making you new. If you've ever felt like you're living two lives-the public you that looks strong and the private you that's barely hanging on-this book is for you. DIVIDED will help you see that failure isn't final, temptation isn't sin, and your past doesn't disqualify your purpose. You'll laugh, you'll be challenged, and you'll find hope in the God who meets you where you are but never leaves you there. Honest, gritty, and full of grace, this book will inspire you to feed the right wolf, renew your mind, and finally live as the "you" God designed you to be.
---
---
|

I want to make you aware of an important issue affecting our community and encourage your participation. Tonight, the Broken Arrow City Council will hear matters related to a proposed Islamic mosque development. This meeting matters because of what is being proposed to be built, how it impacts our area, and whether leaders are asking the right questions before approving major cultural changes. Across Oklahoma and the nation, we are seeing a steady expansion of Islam. While America guarantees freedom of religion, it also requires local governments to carefully evaluate land use, traffic impact, transparency, and long-term community effects. These conversations must happen and it must be noted that Islam is much more than a mere religion. Islam is an oppressive and regressive political system that is incompatible with Western Civilization. This is not about hatred or fear. It is about wisdom, accountability, and protecting the character of our community. Citizens have every right to ask: How will this development affect surrounding neighborhoods? Who is funding it, and with what oversight? Will it comply fully with local laws and community standards? Healthy communities are built when citizens show up, speak calmly, and insist on transparency. I encourage you—if you are able—to attend the Broken Arrow City Council meeting tonight or to stay informed and engaged in the process. We can be firm without being hateful. Courageous without being cruel. And faithful while remaining respectful. Let’s pray for our city leaders, for wisdom in decision-making, and for peace rooted in truth. name: Mary Peltola
subject: My decision
body:
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260112-1.L-wbRv&refcode=em-20260112-1&t=1&akid=1199%2E1086043%2ElU16NV)
Peter —
My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska — and to ask for your support as we begin this journey together.
But if you already understand how important this race and your support are, and you’re ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please use this link to split a contribution today:
**[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20260112-1?refcode=em-20260112-1-founding-donor](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260112-1.L-wbRv&refcode=em-20260112-1&t=2&akid=1199%2E1086043%2ElU16NV)**
**[Contribute ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260112-1.L-wbRv&refcode=em-20260112-1&t=3&akid=1199%2E1086043%2ElU16NV)**
Now here’s a bit more on how I came to this decision.
When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance.
Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity.
Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find.
So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just crushing Alaska families.
And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. _They don’t even believe us._
They're focused on their own wallets — not ours.
And when they actually work together on something, it’s usually to help themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors feather their own nest.
It’s why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted.
I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at home.
Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can actually afford.
Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again.
My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning.
But if we’re going to secure Alaska’s future, we need another priority: fighting corruption.
I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our future.
**This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election, and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we’ll be able to win this race... and that is together.**
So I am asking:
**[Please contribute to my campaign for the U.S. Senate and Wes Moore today to become a Founding Donor. We are looking for a huge number of day one donors to get our campaign off to a strong start and put us in a position to win. No donation is too small. They will all add up fast.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260112-1.L-wbRv&refcode=em-20260112-1&t=4&akid=1199%2E1086043%2ElU16NV)**

Thank you,
Mary

name: Mary Peltola
subject: My decision:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | [](https://go.dscc.org/go/22453?amount=5&t=4&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=22355%2E9716520%2EK0OWf6)
Hello -

My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska – and to ask for your support as we begin this journey together.

But if you already understand how important this race and your support are, and you're ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please use this link to split a contribution today to me and the DSCC to help us win everywhere:

[**_www.DSCC.org/Peltola-founding-donor_**](https://go.dscc.org/go/22453?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=22355%2E9716520%2EK0OWf6)

| | [CONTRIBUTE](https://go.dscc.org/go/22453?amount=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=22355%2E9716520%2EK0OWf6)
---

Now here's a bit more on how I came to this decision.

When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance.

Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity.

Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find.

So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just _crushing_ Alaska families.

And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. _They don't even believe us._

They're focused on their own wallets – not ours.

And when they actually work together on something, it's usually to help themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors feather their own nest.

It's why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted.

I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at home.

Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can actually afford.

Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again.

My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning.

But if we're going to secure Alaska's future, we need another priority: fighting corruption.

I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our future.

**This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election, and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we'll be able to win this race ... and that is together.**

So I am asking:

[**_Please contribute to my campaign for the U.S. Senate and the DSCC today to become a Founding Donor and help Democrats win. We are looking for a huge number of day one donors to get our campaign off to a strong start and put us in a position to win. No donation is too small. They will all add up fast._**](https://go.dscc.org/go/22453?amount=5&t=7&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=22355%2E9716520%2EK0OWf6)

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately and be split between Alaskans for Mary and the DSCC.

| [Donate $5](https://go.dscc.org/go/22453?amount=5&t=8&amount=5&express_lane=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=22355%2E9716520%2EK0OWf6)
---
| [Donate $10](https://go.dscc.org/go/22453?amount=5&t=9&amount=10&express_lane=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=22355%2E9716520%2EK0OWf6)
---
| [Donate $20](https://go.dscc.org/go/22453?amount=5&t=10&amount=20&express_lane=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=22355%2E9716520%2EK0OWf6)
---
| [Other Amount](https://go.dscc.org/go/22453?amount=5&t=11&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20260112-MaryPeltola-PeltolaLaunchTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=22355%2E9716520%2EK0OWf6)
---

Thank you,

Mary

name: Ro Khanna
subject: I backed a billionaire tax — now they want me out
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

FIRST: I stood up for working people and said the wealthiest few Americans
should pay a modest 1% tax to help fund healthcare — especially after
Republicans gutted Medicaid and are still trying to let Affordable Care
Act subsidies expire.

And Bernie Sanders agrees with me:

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260112-rk.u7D68K&refcode=em20260112-37363&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=37363%2E1203867%2ESI3x-M ]Tweet from Bernie Sanders saying “Yes: We need a wealth tax on
billionaires” and a statement agreeing with Rep. Ro Khanna’s proposed
billionaire tax.

NOW: they are openly threatening to bankroll a primary challenger against
me because of it.

Let me be clear: I will not back down from taxing the ultra-wealthy to
protect healthcare for millions of Americans.

But the attacks are real. And they’re escalating.

The ultra-wealthy and their allies are furious that I’ve said the quiet
part out loud: in the richest country in the world, we shouldn’t be
cutting healthcare for working families while billionaires pay a lower
effective tax rate than teachers and firefighters.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260112-rk.u7D68K&refcode=em20260112-37363&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=37363%2E1203867%2ESI3x-M ]Before I explain more, will you rush a split $5 to my
campaign and Justice Democrats to help us show those bankrolling a primary
against me that threatening us will only strengthen our movement?

I don’t take PAC money.
I don’t take lobbyist money.
I don’t take corporate money.

My campaign is powered by people like you. And that’s exactly why they
want to push progressives like me and my fellow Justice Democrats out of
Congress.

This fight isn’t just about me. It’s about whether someone can stand up to
the wealthiest few in America while being a representative from Silicon
Valley.

If I lose this seat, it sends a message that they can silence anyone who
fights for the working class.

If we win, it proves something powerful: that people power can beat big
money – even when they come for us.

I really need your back right now for the fight that is about to come.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260112-rk.u7D68K&refcode=em20260112-37363&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1004&akid=37363%2E1203867%2ESI3x-M ]Chip in whatever you can today and help us show these ultra-wealthy and
their allies that my seat isn’t for sale.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260112-rk.u7D68K&refcode=em20260112-37363&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1023&akid=37363%2E1203867%2ESI3x-M ]Contribute $5

Thank you for standing with me.

In solidarity,

Ro

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20260112-37363-footer&t=1024&refcode2=37363_1203867_SI3x-M&akid=37363%2E1203867%2ESI3x-M

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1025&akid=37363%2E1203867%2ESI3x-M

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster, PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find.
So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just _crushing_ Alaska families.
And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying as much as 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. _They don’t even believe us._
They're focused on their own wallets — not ours.

clean_body: | Derek — My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska — and to ask for your support as we begin this journey together. But if you already understand how important this race and your support are, and you’re ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please use this link to split a contribution between our campaign and the DNC today: Now here’s a bit more on how I came to this decision. When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance. Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity. Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find. So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just crushing Alaska families. And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying as much as 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. They don’t even believe us. They're focused on their own wallets — not ours. And when they actually work together on something, it’s usually to help themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors feather their own nest. It’s why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted. I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at home. Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can actually afford. Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again. My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning. But if we’re going to secure Alaska’s future, we need another priority: fighting corruption. I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our future. This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election, and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we’ll be able to win this race... and that is together. So I am asking: If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your split contribution between Mary Peltola and the Democratic National Committee instantly: | | ** | | | | | | | ** Thank you, Mary If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

name: Barlow For Wyo
subject: Barlow For Wyoming Newsletter
body:
2026 Kicks Off With Strong Momentum
| | | | | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESJ619X34VJE40SY0BGK5RX_0)
---
| **Welcome to the Barlow for Wyoming Newsletter. Your hub for latest news, information and upcoming events.**
---
|
---
| | |
---
| BUILDING MOMENTUM
---
| I’m grateful for the trust Wyoming people have placed in this campaign. Because of that support, we’re heading into 2026 with real momentum. We closed 2025 with more than $500,000 raised, 460 Wyoming donors, hundreds of volunteers, and engagement in all 23 counties. I entered this race to keep serving Wyoming—not to chase headlines or play political games. This campaign is being built by Wyoming people who believe in local control, practical solutions, and leadership that listens. I’ll keep working every day to earn that trust and stay focused on Wyoming’s future.
---
| **Ride with Barlow for Wyoming**
---
| Eric's campaign is powered by folks who believe in Wyoming’s values of hard work, integrity, and community. Your contribution keeps us moving forward to build a stronger future for our state. **👉 Join the ride — chip in today!**
---
| | [ DONATE ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESJ619X34VJE40SY0BGK5RX_1)
---
|
---
| **On The Trail**
---
| |
---
| Barlow Raises Over $500k, Brings Historic Fundraising Momentum Into 2026 Eric Barlow’s campaign for Governor of Wyoming is closing out the year with unprecedented momentum, reporting just over half a million dollars raised. The amount places the campaign on pace for one of the strongest fundraising efforts of any Wyoming gubernatorial
race in Wyoming’s history. “I entered this race to continue serving Wyoming, not to climb the political ladder or play wait-and-see politics,” Barlow added. “As we head into the new year, I’m grateful for the trust people have placed in this campaign, and I’ll keep working every day to earn it.” Since launching his campaign, Barlow has received broad, grassroots support from Wyomingites across all 23 counties. His campaign has signed up hundreds of volunteers, has over 460 Wyoming donors, and has more than $390,000 in cash on hand to carry forward into 2026. “This campaign is being built by Wyoming people who believe in Wyoming solutions,” Barlow said. “This level of support is humbling, and it tells me that folks are ready for leadership that listens, respects local communities, and puts the long-term future of this
state first.” “Wyoming people know Eric Barlow, and they trust his steady leadership,” said Diana Enzi, co-chair of the Barlow for Wyoming campaign committee. “This early support reflects real confidence in his commitment to practical solutions for Wyoming’s future.” The strong fundraising comes alongside growing statewide engagement. Over the past five months, the campaign has expanded its footprint across Wyoming, with supporters hosting events, volunteering, and helping carry Barlow’s practical conservative message into their own communities. Campaign leadership attributes the fundraising success to a focus on direct voter engagement and a campaign grounded in Wyoming values rather than national political trends. “This unprecedented early support reflects what we know about our Wyoming neighbors,” said Tony Ross, co-chair of the Barlow for Wyoming campaign committee. “They are responding to a campaign built on experience, service and respect, and they’re ready for steady, capable leadership.” Looking ahead, the campaign plans to build on its momentum with expanded outreach, additional community events, and continued engagement with voters across Wyoming. January will include events in Campbell County, the Big Horn Basin, and both southwest and northwest Wyoming. February and early March will focus on the 2026 legislative budget session, during which Barlow will host events across the southeastern part of the state.
---
|
---
| |
---
| **ECONOMY** In 2025, I traveled to all 23 Wyoming counties. Different towns, same conversation: workforce. From major infrastructure projects to the industries that power them–natural gas, oil, coal, and uranium–to growing communities and homeowners who depend on skilled trades close to home, Wyoming’s challenge isn’t vision; it’s people-power. Building and keeping a skilled workforce that earns a livable wage isn’t optional—it’s essential for Wyoming today and for the next generation. That means supporting the work already happening on the ground with programs like RIDE and Profile of a Graduate; Career and Technical Education efforts such as Wyoming SkillsUSA; our community college certificate programs; UW WORTH and Ranch Management programs; and initiatives like the newly introduced Build Wyoming that connect talent to opportunity. Wyoming works best when we prepare people for real jobs, real careers, and real lives, right here at home.
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
| PRO LIFE STANCE I’ve never wavered in my pro-life convictions. My legislative record reflects a clear commitment to protecting life and supporting families—by strengthening maternal health, backing community-based care, and ensuring women and children have real support close to home. Being pro-life means building a culture that values life and strengthens Wyoming families.
---
|
---
| |
---
| WY SUPREME COURT RULING ON ABORTION Last week's Wyoming Supreme Court ruling on abortion underscores the seriousness of the constitutional questions facing our state and the shared duty to address them with care and respect. Wyoming residents hold sincere, deeply felt views on this issue, and those voices should be heard through a transparent, lawful process. As Governor, my commitment will be to steady leadership—listening to Wyoming people, respecting the courts, and working to ensure access to healthcare while upholding the principles of constitutional governance. In moments like this, Wyoming is best served by leaders who lower the temperature, respect one another, and focus on the well-being of our people. As Governor, I look forward to advancing a pro-life agenda with the respect and compassion that we all expect of our leaders.
---
|
---
| **Get In The Saddle**
---
| | |
---
| | | | Wyoming’s strength has always come from neighbors pitching in. Join the Barlow For Wyoming team of volunteers who are helping share his message across our state. **🤠 Ride with us — sign up to volunteer!**
---
| | [ VOLUNTEER ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESJ619X34VJE40SY0BGK5RX_2)
---
| | | | **Follow Us Online**
---
| [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESJ619X34VJE40SY0BGK5RX_3) [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESJ619X34VJE40SY0BGK5RX_4) [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESJ619X34VJE40SY0BGK5RX_5) [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESJ619X34VJE40SY0BGK5RX_6)
---
| No longer want to receive these emails? [Unsubscribe](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KESJ619X34VJE40SY0BGK5RX_7)
Barlow For Wyo P.O. Box 822 Green River, WY 82935
---

subject: Trump’s Fed coup will crash economy to depths of 1929
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
| | | | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat taking on Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th congressional district. Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However [if you'd like to unsubscribe,](https://visitor.constantcontact.com/do?p=un&m=001XvBc3bsHgRWXLyJfHoB0-w%3D&ch=005337f2-e4bf-11f0-bd14-0242b661c010&ca=49961339-36ae-4efc-a9b5-cb2817c5f9f2) you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email.
---
---
| | [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001iAygO44cktnT_RKyKLsM5X8evtRb7cP_9RetTb6NL8lXI8znUkyk1FC6e2ovoauzZ2fOlsh0IbeV8HHl20fqyziWMrKd-m8bFdJ2m5rbfYl00X22MrPG5C0EkLQ7hXWA27sNV1wfm6F-Cs3xY4OLU891l0O9lttyoZvPtvDAjRv2wc_XsneyPoKpUyxVhGFKFp25uLJlEu_Z435Y9STbP2qxgOTMBmir&c=ZBJ6mjg_e1xLjLmizPjgsYzEuFDiAsn_sRfrSKelYsbh2Ox74A2YUg==&ch=F168Spnvf3OGS1r8bq0epxI_jxXw6ZzbYB_W6oP7usYzLMVk1RJ3OQ==)

| Peter,
Last night, Donald Trump triggered a massive constitutional crisis that threatens to destroy our economy and lead to untold suffering and pain for working-class Americans.
First, a report leaked that Trump's Department of Justice was opening an investigation against Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell over "lies to Congress about the scope" of a renovation of the Fed headquarters in D.C.
Powell then released a video on social media acknowledging the investigation and warned that Trump was weaponizing the DOJ against him in order take full control of the central bank:
---
| | [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001iAygO44cktnT_RKyKLsM5X8evtRb7cP_9RetTb6NL8lXI8znUkyk1FC6e2ovoauzZ2fOlsh0IbeV8HHl20fqyziWMrKd-m8bFdJ2m5rbfYl00X22MrPG5C0EkLQ7hXWA27sNV1wfm6F-Cs3xY4OLU891l0O9lttyoZvPtvDAjRv2wc_XsneyPoKpUyxVhGFKFp25uLJlEu-KDnb3aXjPLOyr0JvbU9fx&c=ZBJ6mjg_e1xLjLmizPjgsYzEuFDiAsn_sRfrSKelYsbh2Ox74A2YUg==&ch=F168Spnvf3OGS1r8bq0epxI_jxXw6ZzbYB_W6oP7usYzLMVk1RJ3OQ==)
---
| This a full blown coup.
The Fed has been independent for 100 years, which is essential so that it can make decisions based on what is best for the economy, not for political reasons.
The Fed acts like a thermostat for the economy, raising and lowering interest rates to prevent prices from getting too high or the economy from crashing.
For months, Trump has been attacking Powell, begging him to lower interest rates significantly. Powell has resisted in fear of triggering skyrocketing prices and crashing the economy.
Now, Trump is weaponizing the DOJ in an attempt to seize control of the Fed and set monetary policy himself at a time when the economy is already precarious due to his mismanagement.
This criminal act could unleash hell for working-class Americans. If Trump takes control of the central bank and begins lowering interest rates despite warnings from experts, it could trigger a massive rise in prices and send our economy crashing at a time when many people are already struggling to afford the essentials.
Republicans have been attacking the Federal Reserve for decades in an attempt to cede control of our economy over to billionaires and corporations. My opponent Lauren Boebert, has even cosponsored a bill that would abolish it altogether.
[This is what happens when MAGA extremists led by a convicted felon take over our government; lawlessness, a cratering economy, and working-people taking the brunt of their abuse. We need to fight back. Will you rush a donation of $25, $15, or whatever you can spare to help me defeat MAGA extremist Lauren Boebert and restore justice and fix our economy?](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001iAygO44cktnT_RKyKLsM5X8evtRb7cP_9RetTb6NL8lXI8znUkyk1FC6e2ovoauzMrsz1X896-JH057FyeFuEKMzaFZVFMcn40ICNi5Yovb7pEDC7TB7oxW-tyLrVGMXfrA1vekXoY28NDYmOrvRTqPHPToqhTK5ILb3pDTX0b-JHlLOybdyvhpt403jH8vMRF072FzOpUqJf7C2_jEwOMkPKIsfV_4p8gPigqusDEA=&c=ZBJ6mjg_e1xLjLmizPjgsYzEuFDiAsn_sRfrSKelYsbh2Ox74A2YUg==&ch=F168Spnvf3OGS1r8bq0epxI_jxXw6ZzbYB_W6oP7usYzLMVk1RJ3OQ==)
---

However you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email. | | == | Peter, Last night, Donald Trump triggered a massive constitutional crisis that threatens to destroy our economy and lead to untold suffering and pain for working-class Americans. First, a report leaked that Trump's Department of Justice was opening an investigation against Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell over "lies to Congress about the scope" of a renovation of the Fed headquarters in D.C. Powell then released a video on social media acknowledging the investigation and warned that Trump was weaponizing the DOJ against him in order take full control of the central bank: | | == | This a full blown coup. The Fed has been independent for 100 years, which is essential so that it can make decisions based on what is best for the economy, not for political reasons. The Fed acts like a thermostat for the economy, raising and lowering interest rates to prevent prices from getting too high or the economy from crashing. For months, Trump has been attacking Powell, begging him to lower interest rates significantly. Powell has resisted in fear of triggering skyrocketing prices and crashing the economy. Now, Trump is weaponizing the DOJ in an attempt to seize control of the Fed and set monetary policy himself at a time when the economy is already precarious due to his mismanagement. This criminal act could unleash hell for working-class Americans. If Trump takes control of the central bank and begins lowering interest rates despite warnings from experts, it could trigger a massive rise in prices and send our economy crashing at a time when many people are already struggling to afford the essentials. Republicans have been attacking the Federal Reserve for decades in an attempt to cede control of our economy over to billionaires and corporations. body:
There comes a point in every man’s life...
| Dear Peter,
---
There comes a point in every man’s life when you wonder how history will remember you.
---
No matter what happens in my RIGGED trial, I know with absolute confidence that history will say: **_“Rudy W. Giuliani was a fighter.”_**
---

__
---
**I fought the Mafia, I fought violent criminals, I fought corruption, I fought the Deep State agents who tried to frame and destroy my client – President Trump.**
---
_Now, at 81 years old, rather than fighting for someone else’s freedom and justice, I find myself fighting for my** _own_**._
---
**That’s why I officially filed a NEW _Defense_ _Trust_ in the conservative-friendly state of Florida that will give me _more power_ to fight the Left’s FINAL attempt to imprison me for LIFE.**
---
_Peter, I am going to be brutally honest with you: right now, I lack the necessary means to fully fund my Defense Trust. Trust me, admitting that is not my strong suit._
---
But to beat the bloodthirsty vengeful Democrats, I’m going to need every cent I can get.
---
_I’m going up against the machine that let James Comey and Letitia James off the hook – the machine that had me and the President ARRESTED in the most violent jail in America._
---
With your help, history will forever remember that we took on the machine – and _WON_!
---
_But, if you can’t give right now, then please just carry on with your day._
---

name: Hoosiers for Haggard
subject: Officially Filed!
body:
We have officially filed to run for Congress and have a whole new website for you to check out!

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/aebcb2c40b86/officially-filed?e=72dace1453)
---
|
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
| |
# It’s Official!
---
|
---
| | As many of you may have heard, the Haggard for Congress campaign made our big announcement that we are officially filed to run and win in the 4th Congressional district in 5 short months! As the first candidate filed, I wanted to thank you all for the support I have been receiving the last few days and I look forward to what’s to come into May!

---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
|
---
|
---
| | I also had the greatest honor to join national conservative grassroots activist Scott Pressler in Plainfield Thursday night! The room was packed and I made sure to dedicate my speech to making sure 2026 is the year we get people out knocking on doors, campaigning, and supporting real, conservative candidates. Here is a great video also our team put together highlighting the work we need to do in 2026!
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
| [My speech with Scott!](https://hoosiersforhaggard.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9e7cbafb97c2110b065cece3&id=c25892a281&e=72dace1453)
---
|
---
| | I finally had a great time this week speaking to a room of conservative activists in Lafayette at the Citizens In Action meeting. I was proud to give updates on upcoming legislation, talk about our race, and the need to make sure we keep Tippecanoe County red in 2026 and beyond!
---
|
---
|
---
| | I will continue highlighting the endorsements I continue to receive throughout the district from local leaders and groups that put their faith in me to represent them in Congress. Our momentum is just getting started and I can’t wait to continue to share with you who is already on board. If you ever want to get involved or endorse, please reach out!
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
| |
---
|
---
| |
# Finally please check out our new and improved website below!
---
| [Check Out Our NEW Website Here!](https://hoosiersforhaggard.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9e7cbafb97c2110b065cece3&id=af719e5fc7&e=72dace1453)

subject: Your gift saves lives and shares the gospel
body:
For nearly two decades, PreBorn! has been standing in the gap for mothers and babies across the nation.

| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zq4n10buv5nbeh3dv964ci7hp9m6mugap6rocs88_rp22sh2s8i6eor0s9imaoj2cph2bs)
---
Hi Advocate for Life, For nearly two decades, **PreBorn!** has been standing in the gap for mothers and babies across the nation. We do two simple but powerful and life-changing things: We help women in crisis **see their babies and hear their heartbeats through free ultrasounds** , and we offer **gospel-centered, compassionate care** at our Network Clinics. Those two things change everything and we can’t do them without people like you. When a mother hears her child’s heartbeat for the first time, fear gives way to faith.

A tiny miracle begins the story of a mighty future.

Hope breaks through the darkness. Your **$28 gift provides one life-saving ultrasound** and shares the love of Christ with a woman in need. As our thank-you for your gift, you’ll receive the [**_Tiny Miracles, Mighty Futures 2026 Calendar_**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zbh2ip4bq3ifeuepr0ps5n8eqb5jvs7uesg85k48_rp22sh2s8i6eor0s9imaoj2cph2bs) __ filled with **scripture and a monthly prayer guide** to help you lift up mothers, babies, and the ministry of PreBorn! all year long in prayer.

Calendars are limited during this busy year-end season, so give now to **save a baby, share the gospel, and celebrate the gift of life**.

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Where the Democratic Party goes from here:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
Warren for Senate: https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/370?source=em-header&t=1001&akid=2751%2E4032634%2E5taPYQ

This is a longer message from me, but an important one. It’s about the
future of the Democratic Party and how our success depends on a choice we
make right now:

Are we going to carefully craft our policies to ensure that the rich keep
right on getting richer, or can we build a party that ferociously and
unapologetically serves the needs of working people?

The first step in rebuilding trust with the American people is to admit
that we have to choose.

Because this is a dangerous moment for America and for the world.

A global contest is escalating between democratic institutions governed by
the rule of law and lawless dictators who seek to enrich themselves and
their cronies.

Here at home, President Trump’s tariffs are driving up costs for families.
Millions of Americans have lost their health insurance so that Republicans
could fund tax breaks for rich people. And Donald Trump’s view of the
First Amendment is that he gets to say whatever he wants, AND he gets to
use the power of government to silence, extort, bankrupt, or even
prosecute anyone who criticizes him.

None of this would be happening if Democrats hadn’t been wiped out in
2024.

According to some self-described experts, we lost because we were “too
progressive.”

But for a lot of powerful people — wealthy people from Wall Street,
Silicon Valley, and Washington — “too progressive” is code used to
undermine any economic agenda that favors working people. We’re being told
that in order to respond to the 2024 losses, the Democrats need to water
down our economic agenda, claiming that a less progressive Democratic
Party will win more elections.

They are wrong.

Revising our economic agenda to tiptoe around that conclusion might appeal
to the wealthy, but it definitely will not help Democrats win elections.

A Democratic Party that worries more about offending big donors than
delivering for working people is a party that’s doomed to fail.

Let’s just look at the basic math. By definition, the top 0.1% of the
economic ladder doesn’t have a lot of votes. So when the question is
raised whether Democrats should build our tent by sucking up to the rich,
it’s sure not about attracting their votes. It’s about attracting their
money.

Most of these people are not interested in changing an economic game that
is already rigged in their favor. In exchange for their financial support,
they insist that the Democratic Party turn its economic agenda in a
direction that mostly benefits the wealthy and further undermines the
economic stability of tens of millions of families across this country.

They can fund super PACs to prop up political campaigns for their chosen
candidates. They can fund their own lobbying efforts. They can build or
simply buy whole media empires in order to bend the news to their liking.
And, as we’re seeing right now with AI and crypto, they can try to crush
anyone who gets in the way of their business interests.

Over the past generation, the wealthy have avoided accountability again
and again. Regular Americans must play by every rule or face real
consequences. You don’t need to read every news article about Jeffrey
Epstein and his good buddies like Larry Summers and Donald Trump to
understand how consistently rich and powerful insiders protect each other,
regardless of politics and regardless of how obscene the situation has
become.

So how does this affect winning elections?

After the 2024 election, pundits sliced and diced demographic groups —
across race, age, religion, and geography — to show how Democrats need to
grow our coalition to win again. Yes, we need support from rural voters,
men, and voters without a college degree. And yes, in 2025 we won back
some of those folks back, partly because Democratic candidates from every
wing of the party ran against Trump’s betrayal of working people on
affordability issues.

But in the long run, to build a strong Democratic party with a sturdy big
tent, it’s not enough to simply attack Trump. Democrats need to earn trust
— long-term, durable trust — across the electorate.
Trust that we actually understand what’s broken, and trust that we have
the courage to take on our rigged system and fix it — even when that means
taking on the wealthy and well-connected.
Democrats weren’t always just the default option when the other guys were
worse.

We were once trusted by working people to fight for their interests. And
we delivered — even against tough Republican opposition. Social Security,
strong unions, the 40-hour workweek, overtime pay, Medicare, Medicaid,
homeownership for veterans and first-time homebuyers, the Affordable Care
Act. Over and over, we showed that we could fight and we could deliver.

There is a temptation — in this moment of national crisis — to sand down
our edges to avoid offending anyone, especially the rich and powerful who
might finance our candidates.

Yes, because of our broken campaign finance laws, Democrats need to raise
a lot of money. But money is not the only ingredient for a successful
election. When Democrats water down their economic platform to appeal to
wealthy donors, whether the transaction is explicit or subtle, we squander
trust with working people and the money just isn’t worth it.

Democrats need a big tent. But there are two visions for what a big tent
means. One vision says that we should shape our agenda and temper our
rhetoric to flatter any fabulously rich person looking for a political
party that will entrench their own economic interests. The other vision
says we must acknowledge the economic failures of the current rigged
system, aggressively challenge the status quo, and chart a clear path for
big, structural change.

If we are going to pick up the broken pieces from the 2024 election and
build a durable Big Tent, we must acknowledge a hard truth: The Democratic
Party cannot pursue both visions at the same time. Either we politely
nibble around the edges of change, or we throw ourselves into the fight.

If Democrats want to win elections, they need to read the room — or I
should say, they need to read literally any room anywhere in America that
isn’t filled with big donors.

What does it mean to focus our agenda on an aggressive economic vision? At
its core, the goal is simple and easy to measure.

It means boosting pay and making life more affordable for working people.

Building more affordable homes and cracking down on corporate landlords.

Increasing the size of Social Security checks.

Providing universal child care.

Passing price gouging laws with real teeth.

Guaranteeing the right to repair your own cars, machines, and business
equipment.

Strengthening unions.

Building universal health care.

Taxing the wealthy and giant corporations.

I could go on and on — we are not short on good ideas.

Zohran Mamdani came from out of nowhere and took down a political dynasty.
How? He ran a campaign tightly focused on the cost of living with an easy
to understand platform – free buses, freeze the rent, and deliver no-cost
childcare. Mikie Sherrill also focused on cost of living with an
easy-to-understand platform, including affordable childcare and a bold
promise to freeze utility rates on day one. And she won by 14 points.

Ideas are great, but voters also need to believe that we have the guts to
enact an affordability agenda, even over the objections of other
Democrats.

We win when we show we’re willing to actually fight. That means that even
when we fail, we leave everything on the field.

We win when we run on the big changes it will take to build an economy for
everyone.

We win when we call out corruption and bad actors.

We win when we stand against the avalanche of corporate money trying to
bury our democracy.

We win when we stop members of Congress from buying and selling individual
stocks and cryptocurrencies while they are writing laws that affect those
assets.

It doesn’t take a political genius to conclude that in a democracy, when
the choice is between “make the rich richer” and “help everybody else,”
winning elections is about choosing “everybody else.”

Ideas are not better because they come from a rich person offering to open
his wallet and advance his own financial interests — and our leaders
should stop acting like they are.

I am deeply worried about the survival of our democracy. Democrats need to
win elections across this country, and I will do everything I can to help.
That’s why my campaign just donated over $400,000 to 23 state parties that
will be fighting for some of the most competitive Senate, House, and
Governor seats in November.

We must, must, must win up-and-down the ballot in these critical midterm
elections, and I’ll keep doing my part. And every chance I get, I will
also stand up and say that Democrats cannot build a durable, governing
majority that actually makes life better for people and rebuilds trust in
the Democratic Party by watering down our economic vision.

[ https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3315?t=1006&akid=2751%2E4032634%2E5taPYQ ]If you agree with this vision, and if you’re able to, please pitch in
to support our campaign today. With your support, we’re fighting for
Democrats to win big in 2026, 2028, and beyond by campaigning on big ideas
that help working people.

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

clean_body: Warren for Senate: This is a longer message from me, but an important one. It’s about the future of the Democratic Party and how our success depends on a choice we make right now: Are we going to carefully craft our policies to ensure that the rich keep right on getting richer, or can we build a party that ferociously and unapologetically serves the needs of working people? The first step in rebuilding trust with the American people is to admit that we have to choose. Because this is a dangerous moment for America and for the world. A global contest is escalating between democratic institutions governed by the rule of law and lawless dictators who seek to enrich themselves and their cronies. Here at home, President Trump’s tariffs are driving up costs for families. Millions of Americans have lost their health insurance so that Republicans could fund tax breaks for rich people. And Donald Trump’s view of the First Amendment is that he gets to say whatever he wants, AND he gets to use the power of government to silence, extort, bankrupt, or even prosecute anyone who criticizes him. None of this would be happening if Democrats hadn’t been wiped out in 2024. According to some self-described experts, we lost because we were “too progressive.” But for a lot of powerful people — wealthy people from Wall Street, Silicon Valley, and Washington — “too progressive” is code used to undermine any economic agenda that favors working people. We’re being told that in order to respond to the 2024 losses, the Democrats need to water down our economic agenda, claiming that a less progressive Democratic Party will win more elections. They are wrong. Revising our economic agenda to tiptoe around that conclusion might appeal to the wealthy, but it definitely will not help Democrats win elections. A Democratic Party that worries more about offending big donors than delivering for working people is a party that’s doomed to fail. Let’s just look at the basic math. By definition, the top 0.1% of the economic ladder doesn’t have a lot of votes. So when the question is raised whether Democrats should build our tent by sucking up to the rich, it’s sure not about attracting their votes. It’s about attracting their money. Most of these people are not interested in changing an economic game that is already rigged in their favor. In exchange for their financial support, they insist that the Democratic Party turn its economic agenda in a direction that mostly benefits the wealthy and further undermines the economic stability of tens of millions of families across this country. They can fund super PACs to prop up political campaigns for their chosen candidates. They can fund their own lobbying efforts. They can build or simply buy whole media empires in order to bend the news to their liking. And, as we’re seeing right now with AI and crypto, they can try to crush anyone who gets in the way of their business interests. Over the past generation, the wealthy have avoided accountability again and again. Regular Americans must play by every rule or face real consequences. You don’t need to read every news article about Jeffrey Epstein and his good buddies like Larry Summers and Donald Trump to understand how consistently rich and powerful insiders protect each other, regardless of politics and regardless of how obscene the situation has become. So how does this affect winning elections? After the 2024 election, pundits sliced and diced demographic groups — across race, age, religion, and geography — to show how Democrats need to grow our coalition to win again. Yes, we need support from rural voters, men, and voters without a college degree. And yes, in 2025 we won back some of those folks back, partly because Democratic candidates from every wing of the party ran against Trump’s betrayal of working people on affordability issues. But in the long run, to build a strong Democratic party with a sturdy big tent, it’s not enough to simply attack Trump. Democrats need to earn trust — long-term, durable trust — across the electorate. Trust that we actually understand what’s broken, and trust that we have the courage to take on our rigged system and fix it — even when that means taking on the wealthy and well-connected. Democrats weren’t always just the default option when the other guys were worse. We were once trusted by working people to fight for their interests. And we delivered — even against tough Republican opposition. Social Security, strong unions, the 40-hour workweek, overtime pay, Medicare, Medicaid, homeownership for veterans and first-time homebuyers, the Affordable Care Act. Over and over, we showed that we could fight and we could deliver. There is a temptation — in this moment of national crisis — to sand down our edges to avoid offending anyone, especially the rich and powerful who might finance our candidates. Yes, because of our broken campaign finance laws, Democrats need to raise a lot of money. But money is not the only ingredient for a successful election. When Democrats water down their economic platform to appeal to wealthy donors, whether the transaction is explicit or subtle, we squander trust with working people and the money just isn’t worth it. Democrats need a big tent. But there are two visions for what a big tent means. One vision says that we should shape our agenda and temper our rhetoric to flatter any fabulously rich person looking for a political party that will entrench their own economic interests. The other vision says we must acknowledge the economic failures of the current rigged system, aggressively challenge the status quo, and chart a clear path for big, structural change. If we are going to pick up the broken pieces from the 2024 election and build a durable Big Tent, we must acknowledge a hard truth: The Democratic Party cannot pursue both visions at the same time. Either we politely nibble around the edges of change, or we throw ourselves into the fight. If Democrats want to win elections, they need to read the room — or I should say, they need to read literally any room anywhere in America that isn’t filled with big donors. What does it mean to focus our agenda on an aggressive economic vision? At its core, the goal is simple and easy to measure. It means boosting pay and making life more affordable for working people. Building more affordable homes and cracking down on corporate landlords. Increasing the size of Social Security checks. Providing universal child care. Passing price gouging laws with real teeth. Guaranteeing the right to repair your own cars, machines, and business equipment. Strengthening unions. Building universal health care. Taxing the wealthy and giant corporations. I could go on and on — we are not short on good ideas. Zohran Mamdani came from out of nowhere and took down a political dynasty. How? He ran a campaign tightly focused on the cost of living with an easy to understand platform – free buses, freeze the rent, and deliver no-cost childcare. Mikie Sherrill also focused on cost of living with an easy-to-understand platform, including affordable childcare and a bold promise to freeze utility rates on day one. And she won by 14 points. Ideas are great, but voters also need to believe that we have the guts to enact an affordability agenda, even over the objections of other Democrats. We win when we show we’re willing to actually fight. That means that even when we fail, we leave everything on the field. We win when we run on the big changes it will take to build an economy for everyone. We win when we call out corruption and bad actors. We win when we stand against the avalanche of corporate money trying to bury our democracy. We win when we stop members of Congress from buying and selling individual stocks and cryptocurrencies while they are writing laws that affect those assets. It doesn’t take a political genius to conclude that in a democracy, when the choice is between “make the rich richer” and “help everybody else,” winning elections is about choosing “everybody else.” Ideas are not better because they come from a rich person offering to open his wallet and advance his own financial interests — and our leaders should stop acting like they are. I am deeply worried about the survival of our democracy. Democrats need to win elections across this country, and I will do everything I can to help. That’s why my campaign just donated over $400,000 to 23 state parties that will be fighting for some of the most competitive Senate, House, and Governor seats in November. We must, must, must win up-and-down the ballot in these critical midterm elections, and I’ll keep doing my part. And every chance I get, I will also stand up and say that Democrats cannot build a durable, governing majority that actually makes life better for people and rebuilds trust in the Democratic Party by watering down our economic vision. [ ]If you agree with this vision, and if you’re able to, please pitch in to support our campaign today. With your support, we’re fighting for Democrats to win big in 2026, 2028, and beyond by campaigning on big ideas that help working people. | Peter,
If you're a sports fan, you are probably familiar with the saying "the best defense is a good offense."
Well, that's exactly why Defend Our Courts is prepping a major offensive in the Wisconsin state Supreme Court election this April.
Here's the state of play:

* The Wisconsin Supreme Court currently has a 4-3 liberal majority thanks to Wisconsin Dems absolutely embarrassing Elon Musk last year.

* One conservative justice was up for reelection this Spring, but decided not to run. The seat is now up for grabs in April.

* If Dems win this race, they will expand their majority to 5-2.

* Whoever wins will be elected to a 10 year term. After last year's WI court win, that means Democrats have an opportunity to guarantee a majority on the court until at least 2030.

---

This would be catastrophic to Trump and MAGA, who have been plotting to seize control of state Supreme Courts, the courts with final say on election laws/results, in key swing states across the country.
Last year, we helped lead the defense of PA's Supreme Court from a MAGA takeover. In Philadelphia, where we concentrated much of our voter outreach along with digital and TV ads, turnout surged by 34%. That was the largest increase in the state and Democrats blew MAGA out by 30+ points.
Now we're taking our winning strategy to Wisconsin to help Democrats seize on this once-in-a-generation opportunity.
But MAGA knows what's at stake here too, and they are gonna throw millions of dollars at this election to try and beat us. We only have 4 months to prepare, and that's not a lot of time in political campaigns. We need you on our side NOW:

After last year's WI court win, that means Democrats have an opportunity to guarantee a majority on the court until at least 2030. | == This would be catastrophic to Trump and MAGA, who have been plotting to seize control of state Supreme Courts, the courts with final say on election laws/results, in key swing states across the country. Last year, we helped lead the defense of PA's Supreme Court from a MAGA takeover. In Philadelphia, where we concentrated much of our voter outreach along with digital and TV ads, turnout surged by 34%. That was the largest increase in the state and Democrats blew MAGA out by 30+ points. Now we're taking our winning strategy to Wisconsin to help Democrats seize on this once-in-a-generation opportunity. But MAGA knows what's at stake here too, and they are gonna throw millions of dollars at this election to try and beat us. We only have 4 months to prepare, and that's not a lot of time in political campaigns. We need you on our side NOW: == | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | | | | == | Let's take the fight to MAGA and Trump again. This time in the key battleground state of Wisconsin. Team Defend our Courts | | | | | | | | | | == | | | | | == | | | | Mail checks to: | | | | Defend Our CourtsP.O. Box 45569 Philadelphia, PA 19149 US | | | | | PAID FOR BY DEFEND OUR COURTS, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE.

As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee’s name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account are not tax-deductible for federal income tax purposes.

name: Ashley F.
subject: Judge Chris Taylor’s birthday is tomorrow! Will you sign her card?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Taylor for Justice
body:
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20260112-1010&t=1&akid=1011%2E294397%2Ev0-Rdh)
Hi, it’s Ashley.
Judge Chris Taylor’s birthday is tomorrow, and I want to let you in on a surprise we put together.
We made a **virtual birthday card** to fill with signatures and messages to share with Chris on her special day! 🎂💌
[**I’m hoping that if you have a minute, you’ll add your name before we send the card over to her tomorrow afternoon.**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/signup/2026_birthday_card/?source=em20260112-1010&akid=1011%2E294397%2Ev0-Rdh)
**[Sign the Card ››](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/signup/2026_birthday_card/?source=em20260112-1010&akid=1011%2E294397%2Ev0-Rdh)**
As you can probably imagine, running a statewide campaign takes a lot out of you.
There are many long nights and endless travel, but if you know Chris, you know how dedicated she is to protecting our rights and ensuring everyone gets a fair shot in our judicial system.
**That’s why we want to do something that’ll show Chris how many people are cheering her on.**
[**Will you sign Judge Chris Taylor’s birthday card before tomorrow? You can even add a personal note if you’d like.**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/signup/2026_birthday_card/?source=em20260112-1010&akid=1011%2E294397%2Ev0-Rdh)
Thank you! I know this will mean a lot to her.
With gratitude,
Ashley
Campaign Manager
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_jct_footer?refcode=em20260112-1010&t=3&refcode2=1011_294397_v0-Rdh&akid=1011%2E294397%2Ev0-Rdh)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=4&akid=1011%2E294397%2Ev0-Rdh)

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=1011%2E294397%2Ev0-Rdh)

Email us: [info@chrisforjustice.com](info@chrisforjustice.com)
Paid for by Chris Taylor for Justice
PO Box 1921
Madison, WI 53701

Fellow Patriot,
---
Charlie Kirk built a movement for truth -- but his final message pointed to something eternal.

It wasn't about politics... or elections... or even about the culture war.

His final book was deeply personal, and unlike anything Charlie had written before...

**It was about the Sabbath. **

About sacred rest. Reconnecting with God.

---
Charlie wrote this book because he knew what America was missing... and what he nearly lost himself.

He found that in a world addicted to busyness, noise, and scrolling...

_**The most radical act of resistance is to STOP. **_

To rest. To reflect. To return to God.

This book is his blueprint for how we do that -- as individuals, as families, and as a nation.

🔥 You'll discover how Charlie learned to honor the Sabbath in a culture that never stops

🔥 How setting aside just one day a week gave him clarity, peace, and strength

🔥 Why he believed this message could spark a spiritual revival across America

I know he would've wanted you to have this book.

**When you give today to Turning Point USA, you 're not just supporting the movement Charlie built -- you're helping pass on the message he felt most urgently in his final months. **

**[And we’ll ship you a copy of Charlie’s final book straight to your door.](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=efa44c9658504724a14c64c14a8cd012&_e=SqzmHWrBI09-Z_Ie8UJ3Os7-jd_7gipjJ10Z0VXWXGSyeKBKpDCK9XEljRvKfn1jk_D-qAnpUl0l0I000zSbvUPKYdTwJXoQyv3dNMdhisBClY2Otp6m5t3MWlzFu7OtL0NY8o6euTJlhJp1FA3ItqDmkV6ONBSIWPoD77EV6CP2Q6I5R1CLi3aDu6-aKRcYZN5kXSR_zuYJs8_ywJbsgF3e8x-iza1ChkTIJayknHjsCtufbWoUWqVyvy2DXQT6skvvg364fs0tvd2tEDgNglpN0fPDncpAMg1vewNkFHtpn6khuDC-yX1X0Ydvdz-B-fUEJfFruZaKi-MCn0hSOE0SSXeJ3ZQCCpvH4BGvlQDu3FcUmlOckeGVyGcxP9eh2-jM7nOFPWWY6TC86XFgVNivQAmCNFokl9EWcnvVOhTgkfF_aB5zImRxHuOv0ptsL6vcpnOadtoSXf-uwDC-PDIwPM2815mL8yTqw3gVPbhO7MiJDG2rzJ11KRvlLMZx6cSF8P0u4RknwiK0zcoxvvy57AnJwdtopTVgwvXzWFFzSm2DQYhngIFUpTo50d0C1txQxp0VrHh1C_b7VL8s4Ki1YN-V6E6xt5j-L7fFWvlk3byXyC9GjC9OYlcFged3EVoHdzaN9jjK2MYYTEBm98YYhwA3uT9xwUgVDuk1T7EFN3s4DmIAbiCiHayBJyde)**

Fellow Patriot, thank you, in advance, for helping reach the next generation with truth, faith, and moral courage -- _the very things Charlie gave his life for. _

**For Charlie, **

**Justin Streiff **

clean_body: A message Charlie lived — not just preached Charlie Kirk’s final book on Sabbath rest—make a gift to TPUSA and we’ll send you a copy. | | Fellow Patriot, Charlie Kirk built a movement for truth -- but his final message pointed to something eternal. It wasn't about politics... or elections... or even about the culture war. His final book was deeply personal, and unlike anything Charlie had written before... It was about the Sabbath. About sacred rest. Reconnecting with God. Charlie wrote this book because he knew what America was missing... and what he nearly lost himself. He found that in a world addicted to busyness, noise, and scrolling... The most radical act of resistance is to STOP. To rest. To reflect. To return to God. This book is his blueprint for how we do that -- as individuals, as families, and as a nation. 🔥 You'll discover how Charlie learned to honor the Sabbath in a culture that never stops 🔥 How setting aside just one day a week gave him clarity, peace, and strength 🔥 Why he believed this message could spark a spiritual revival across America I know he would've wanted you to have this book. When you give today to Turning Point USA, you 're not just supporting the movement Charlie built -- you're helping pass on the message he felt most urgently in his final months. Fellow Patriot, thank you, in advance, for helping reach the next generation with truth, faith, and moral courage -- the very things Charlie gave his life for. And as you do, may the message of this book lead you -- just as it led Charlie -- to a deeper trust in God, a stronger love for your family, and a renewed peace in your own soul. For Charlie, Justin Streiff Chief Operating Officer Turning Point USA P.S. Charlie Kirk's final book, Stop, in the Name of God, is a heartfelt call to slow down, remember what's eternal, and rest in God. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

[](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-peltola-em-jan26.H2xyJt&refcode=em260112-1-160&refcode2=4061.734936.KCGRHM&t=1&akid=4061%2E734936%2EKCGRHM)
---
Peter -
My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska — and to ask for your support as we begin this journey together.
But if you already understand how important this race and your support are, and you’re ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please use this link to split a contribution today:
[**https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/Founding-Donor**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-peltola-em-jan26.H2xyJt&refcode=em260112-1-160&t=2&akid=4061%2E734936%2EKCGRHM)
[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-peltola-em-jan26.H2xyJt&refcode=em260112-1-160&t=3&akid=4061%2E734936%2EKCGRHM)
Now here’s a bit more on how I came to this decision.
When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance.
Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity.
Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find.
So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just _crushing_ Alaska families.
And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. _They don’t even believe us._
They're focused on their own wallets — not ours.
And when they actually work together on something, it’s usually to help themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors feather their own nest.
It’s why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted.
I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at home.
Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can actually afford.
Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again.
My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning.
But if we’re going to secure Alaska’s future, we need another priority: fighting corruption.
I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our future.
**This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election, and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we’ll be able to win this race... and that is together.**
So I am asking:
[**Please contribute to my campaign for the U.S. Senate and Ruben Gallego today to become a Founding Donor. We are looking for a huge number of day one donors to get our campaign off to a strong start and put us in a position to win. No donation is too small. They will all add up fast.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-peltola-em-jan26.H2xyJt&refcode=em260112-1-160&t=4&akid=4061%2E734936%2EKCGRHM)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._
| | **[Donate $5](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1061?c0=ruben-peltola-em-jan26.yAGR_H&refcode=em260112-1-160&t=23&akid=4061%2E734936%2EKCGRHM)**

Thank you,
Mary

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]
