name: Frank S. Wright - Corporal, U.S. Marine Corps (Ret.)
subject: I was wounded twice, but many never made it home.
body:
I carry the scars — and the duty to remember.
I still remember seeing the flag raised on Iwo Jima...

| | | [](http://links.americanlibertyalert.com/u/click?_t=3c9a9e018cb54768aae32f693c7987c9&_m=3c011379caca430589d1ab8779be0a0c&_e=j3p6HqIUMM3XJvcLupMyggvjRMPLj7gWNe9ae3_VeixpD9FtwIfVq2o2lOerbIZMyANlrqnGvnozh-wljSFT0pL0dtTvItrahjUWocKt2qdPkNJsP8SgVMFi-ZYC0s6CDWpCGoFAfBjP-kepFJ6juzfByo9MBTN5aTQKm1xRWbAeDKD5gOaAzR8yeojlAJssjAqQeoLBlzu7uG9F0VLbZVia6UQbrhvE-oLAAQ-4GtmDY5dWRyJjSWlad1c3CZFUp-nG69YuPqqz8k-Gev4Ss07gIvLSzeQjLsGKJ3DHprM2wmsX9BllS5DdyJWkf_O270xL0t51NhSD9shHf2k1qxchctG1Kar1LIhmcKbmDyxXtO_fOgKC7-fJAP3OBT90R11mKZtxg7EbDxcWjXUcURQZ4Qyiuz1EOyOH5kjQjPSPzhFTYl6ag51f_DMZztHJc-avttRd2NhvzxH0cr_9pd8jaA4hRDZJMt8lsM6R-8pre1O0vhwgRvwiWWVtdZ4Ai5zsc8HrckMyaEqQSp9f-NeHibo9gV1271iP-po5H7n-2haxWNMjgICSOgt6C6CRSL-yaK3KY2kLtaNVZQfLO7YJNz8WpeWyJ2I66PbGNwXnQUdfjLPWQGbF4YrPGrW-R3tBfQjDUDZEi7Q78sbi3ZhziRWiXume9bnIvOfzkLc%3D)
---
Fellow Patriot,
---
My name is **Corporal Frank S. Wright** , United States Marine Corps, retired. I'm a hundred years old now -- one of the last living Marine Raiders of World War II.In January 1942, just weeks after the attack on Pearl Harbor, I quit high school in Arkansas and walked into the enlistment office. I was sixteen -- too young, too skinny, and too light to qualify. The sergeant told me to come back when I was heavier.So I went home, ate all the bananas I could find, and stuffed five pounds' worth into my pockets before stepping on that scale again.I passed. I lied about my age. And I became a Marine.
---
[](http://links.americanlibertyalert.com/u/click?_t=3c9a9e018cb54768aae32f693c7987c9&_m=3c011379caca430589d1ab8779be0a0c&_e=j3p6HqIUMM3XJvcLupMyggvjRMPLj7gWNe9ae3_VeixpD9FtwIfVq2o2lOerbIZMyANlrqnGvnozh-wljSFT0pL0dtTvItrahjUWocKt2qdPkNJsP8SgVMFi-ZYC0s6CDWpCGoFAfBjP-kepFJ6juzfByo9MBTN5aTQKm1xRWbAeDKD5gOaAzR8yeojlAJssjAqQeoLBlzu7uG9F0VLbZVia6UQbrhvE-oLAAQ-4GtlUnPdrCxyzWnwdr-l6NO-4kmlOHnpHpuj5pKLmiW2qayaO_UOoFI7JJXQfhhcChbNEEKDsVu3zaTDO0XUk63194uBVZxbZg0wwHOVQKpIkVRcfsBjKfqKE222ei5zWwlIGe0PWyogIUrcvzIOVLSrcxx4zUdz2pWjWXjxYiR-hyvkBg8kLECscPNz9FdEQffVuq2v8pVCRxTpxEIs_wtqelO8LssyOK-5vqQe2zybhsueY7xa57SyRxv6KFvTbXj1-HzkmpgD8apFQIVfhy-20SpUggRMSDTtO903HhSOdUAGVOd4Y8sJHeowJee0Zkca7ATHm97oSLNPuHMmdR3aTdV_u-6NtWQD7FervCy32WvkJBqvbbbTy0HsFM778McYvJq3XtB1bcpDT1E8HxKCXzNKCn-yF6SOiss4e5PGBQ0fl7K2njMY2NMeuOU-s4jA%3D)
---
After boot camp, I volunteered to join the **4th Marine Raiders** , America's first special operations unit.They asked me why I wanted to join. I said, _" Because I want to fight."_Our commanding officer was **James Roosevelt** , the President's son -- a good man, the kind who never gave an order he wouldn't do himself. We were trained to land first, fight behind enemy lines, and take the beaches nobody else could reach.From **Guadalcanal** to **New Georgia** , from the jungles of the Solomons to the black sand of **Iwo Jima** , we fought to free the Pacific.On **Guam** , I was stabbed in the stomach during hand-to-hand combat. On **Iwo Jima** , I was hit by machine-gun fire and knocked unconscious. Both times, my men thought I was gone. Somehow, I wasn't.When I came to, I saw the flag rising on Mount Suribachi. Every ship in the harbor sounded its horn. I had tears in my eyes then, and I still do when I think about it.That flag meant freedom still lived.
---
[](http://links.americanlibertyalert.com/u/click?_t=3c9a9e018cb54768aae32f693c7987c9&_m=3c011379caca430589d1ab8779be0a0c&_e=j3p6HqIUMM3XJvcLupMyggvjRMPLj7gWNe9ae3_VeixpD9FtwIfVq2o2lOerbIZMyANlrqnGvnozh-wljSFT0pL0dtTvItrahjUWocKt2qdPkNJsP8SgVMFi-ZYC0s6CDWpCGoFAfBjP-kepFJ6juzfByo9MBTN5aTQKm1xRWbAeDKD5gOaAzR8yeojlAJssjAqQeoLBlzu7uG9F0VLbZVia6UQbrhvE-oLAAQ-4Gtm_ZUleMO9O-28q80bT-CEbBCsTF8SY80oOD4XD8MqClHwmN5UcEoWmZJw2fTm_HVZEwBija_th20OGloQaKgyfcYP8TJGEES7WyQrJ3xB7LXWZV_oxb85dZ696gUm3H69s-1bI9flfNEdLkTLt1-GOzbS4_8YdBwbopUwPFAvPmcpr-bLMiMnFCXPIp11mqdXJnMJl3Gb9D-cHWgrAo3Ew2x8II3VoPk2WDMi6nTFB5GGrHBnTADv5Mw_hGasTFZbptB-lHxdgG2iC14CmbmOun7j0xzW4bWxwujv1XkEDTGpkLmeruWY2oMDMNwhM0eAqsg8LiWA1Za5jPbzBz45Wc-yFh7GGwzorKTi-okOe1t4QnKztaC3gg6RM5NWw8voIKj7x8pKqcLF89AJRNhtvxg_UQ2HqMCsOP8_GKtmvdA3Tvl8_G6NXAgXK8n08sAQ%3D)
---
There were dark days too...On Guadalcanal, I caught malaria and ended up in a field hospital -- but when my unit was preparing to move out, I couldn't stand being left behind.I slipped out and rejoined them. I'll now admit, I was always a little rebellious. When the war ended, I became a drill instructor, helping shape the next generation of Marines. I was in my twenties, carrying wounds inside and out.For more than a decade, I didn't share my stories. I kept them locked away, like many of my brothers did. Over the years, I began to open up and share my story with friends, family, and others who wanted to know about the realities of the battlefield at the Pacific Front.Now, more than ever, the **American Veterans Center** has given me the opportunity to share them -- on the stage at the **Annual Conference** , and by capturing them on film for future generations. Not just my story, but those of the men who never came home, and those who are no longer here to tell theirs.
---

And every year, the **American Veterans Center Annual Conference** gives us something rare -- a gathering where history stands shoulder to shoulder with the future.It's where I've met cadets who listen with the same wide eyes I once had at sixteen. It's where old comrades find each other again, and where we all remember that the values we fought for -- courage, duty, honor -- are still being carried forward.It's a living bridge between those who fought for freedom and those who will defend it next.

clean_body: I carry the scars — and the duty to remember. I still remember seeing the flag raised on Iwo Jima... | | | Fellow Patriot, My name is Corporal Frank S. Wright , United States Marine Corps, retired. I'm a hundred years old now -- one of the last living Marine Raiders of World War II.In January 1942, just weeks after the attack on Pearl Harbor, I quit high school in Arkansas and walked into the enlistment office. I was sixteen -- too young, too skinny, and too light to qualify. The sergeant told me to come back when I was heavier.So I went home, ate all the bananas I could find, and stuffed five pounds' worth into my pockets before stepping on that scale again.I passed. I lied about my age. And I became a Marine. After boot camp, I volunteered to join the 4th Marine Raiders , America's first special operations unit.They asked me why I wanted to join. I said, " Because I want to fight."Our commanding officer was James Roosevelt , the President's son -- a good man, the kind who never gave an order he wouldn't do himself. We were trained to land first, fight behind enemy lines, and take the beaches nobody else could reach.From Guadalcanal to New Georgia , from the jungles of the Solomons to the black sand of Iwo Jima , we fought to free the Pacific.On Guam , I was stabbed in the stomach during hand-to-hand combat. On Iwo Jima , I was hit by machine-gun fire and knocked unconscious. Both times, my men thought I was gone. Somehow, I wasn't.When I came to, I saw the flag rising on Mount Suribachi. Every ship in the harbor sounded its horn. I had tears in my eyes then, and I still do when I think about it.That flag meant freedom still lived. There were dark days too...On Guadalcanal, I caught malaria and ended up in a field hospital -- but when my unit was preparing to move out, I couldn't stand being left behind.I slipped out and rejoined them. I'll now admit, I was always a little rebellious. When the war ended, I became a drill instructor, helping shape the next generation of Marines. I was in my twenties, carrying wounds inside and out.For more than a decade, I didn't share my stories. I kept them locked away, like many of my brothers did. Over the years, I began to open up and share my story with friends, family, and others who wanted to know about the realities of the battlefield at the Pacific Front.Now, more than ever, the American Veterans Center has given me the opportunity to share them -- on the stage at the Annual Conference , and by capturing them on film for future generations. Not just my story, but those of the men who never came home, and those who are no longer here to tell theirs. They've given old Marines like me a place to share what we lived through, to look young cadets and future service members in the eye and say, "This is what it means to serve."They've brought veterans from every generation together -- to remember, to teach, and to inspire. And every year, the American Veterans Center Annual Conference gives us something rare -- a gathering where history stands shoulder to shoulder with the future.It's where I've met cadets who listen with the same wide eyes I once had at sixteen. It's where old comrades find each other again, and where we all remember that the values we fought for -- courage, duty, honor -- are still being carried forward.It's a living bridge between those who fought for freedom and those who will defend it next. The National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C., where America gathers to honor its heroes. The Annual Veterans Conference each November, where we share stories and pass the torch to those who will serve next. The preservation of our stories through documentaries and oral histories -- so that when we are gone, the lessons remain. At my age, I don't take many things for granted. But I still believe in this country -- in what it stands for, and in the people who keep its history alive.If you believe that too, I ask you to help the American Veterans Center continue its work. So that long after the last of us is gone, our voices will still be heard -- reminders of what America can be when its people stand together.Thank you for remembering. Thank you for caring. And thank you for keeping faith with those who served.Semper Fidelis, Frank S. Wright Corporal, U.S. Marine Corps (Ret.) Veteran, 4th Marine Raiders | 21st Marines, 3rd Marine Division P.S.** When I was sixteen, I had to lie about my age to fight for my country. Now, at one hundred, I can tell you the truth as plain as I know it: freedom isn't free, and the stories of those who fought for it are fading fast. Your gift -- whatever you can give -- helps make sure they're never forgotten. name: Heidi Campbell
subject: Session starts tomorrow. This is our last chance.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Campbell for State Senate, Lee Jones, treasurer
body:
| |
---
Peter, Tomorrow, the legislative session begins, and with it, the fundraising blackout.
That means **today is the very last day** for a while that our campaign can raise the resources we need to stand strong during the session ahead.
When I head back to the Capitol, I'll be focused every day on fighting for the people of District 20 and for Tennesseans across our state. But going into this session prepared matters, and that preparation depends on supporters like you stepping up before the window closes.
If you're able, **[I'm asking you to make a contribution today.](https://89ef5d2784b2dbb4a67d22ed19089030.us-east-1.resend-clicks-a.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fheidiemail2026%3Frefcode=0112pre-session/1/0100019bb42f294c-8fb8e89f-02f8-47f3-82fa-839f410ef0a6-000000/NLB_ys_zeWTsAsS2soxLsjeAs2x0-htyiGv9TGwmeeM=439)** Every dollar helps ensure we have the strength and support we need while the work is happening.
---
[ **DONATE TODAY** ](https://89ef5d2784b2dbb4a67d22ed19089030.us-east-1.resend-clicks-a.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fheidiemail2026%3Frefcode=0112pre-session/2/0100019bb42f294c-8fb8e89f-02f8-47f3-82fa-839f410ef0a6-000000/-s4l5JNUpIwC8DOqIh-6IgWt954Rj4gj-7299t2UuxI=439)
---
Thank you for being part of this team and for standing with me. I'm grateful for your support, and I'm ready to get to work.
Heidi Campbell

name: Chris Armstrong for Congress
subject: Welcoming 2026 as "The Year of the People"!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Armstrong for Congress]()
body:
It's Chris Armstrong, Democratic candidate for Congress in Wisconsin's 7th district...

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/8e2aec7aa8b1/welcoming-2026-as-the-year-of-the-people?e=0c0d11993f)
---
[|
---](https://armstrongforwisconsin.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cf02e39423b56c583c783bf1a&id=d966b9c7e7&e=0c0d11993f)
| | | | | |
# Welcoming 2026 as “The Year of the People”!

It’s Chris Armstrong, Democratic candidate for Congress in Wisconsin’s 7th district.
I hope everyone enjoyed time with their loved ones during the holiday season, and I want to warmly welcome you to the new year.
In these dark times (and thank goodness the days are now getting longer), it’s important to remind ourselves why we’re together in this fight to protect our democracy for today’s and future generations.

Over the past few months, we have been focused on building the foundation of our campaign to represent Wisconsin’s 7th Congressional District, and we wanted to share an update on where things stand.
Like many families across our district, life means juggling a lot at once.
For many of us, that includes working multiple jobs and keeping everything moving so the bills get paid and the lights stay on.
In our household, I am the primary income earner, so steady work has to come first.
That reality has shaped how much time we have been able to devote to the campaign in recent months, even as the work continues behind the scenes.
That being said, we are proud to have brought on a seasoned campaign team that understands the realities facing working-class candidates and knows how to build campaigns that reflect them.

I want to thank all of you for being the most wonderful and generous supporters, advisors, volunteers, and donors, to whom I am most grateful.
Your enthusiasm and passion contribute to my unwavering commitment to win the Democratic primary in August and the general election in November.
We’ve got a lot of work to do, and I’m confident we will prevail working together with friends like you.

To those who signed up to volunteer, we appreciate your support and will follow up soon to coordinate availability and opportunities to get involved.
Thanks, Chris ~:|
---
| [Donate Today!](https://armstrongforwisconsin.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cf02e39423b56c583c783bf1a&id=6b5d62af2b&e=0c0d11993f)

My name is Greg Landsman, Congressman for Ohio’s first district – now a critical district to taking on Trump and flipping the House. Here’s the story... In 2022, I ran for Congress because I believed our community needed new leadership – an anti-establishment reformer who puts our priorities ahead of the political and economic elites. Our community agreed, and we flipped our seat as the only campaign to beat a long-term Republican incumbent that year. I’ve stood by my commitment to our community and our district:
* I don’t take corporate PAC money
* I suspended my pay during the government shutdown
* I sold my stocks and am leading the effort to ban members of Congress from trading stocks
* I took on the Speaker of the House and forced a vote on the Social Security Fairness Act, which is now law

My background? I grew up in Greater Cincinnati, where I’m now raising 2 teenagers with my wife, Sarah, and have built my life and career around serving our community. I went to Ohio University, worked as a public school teacher for several years, where I learned the importance of education, and served on City Council, where I was able to make a difference in our community. To stand up to Trump, we need to take the House back. After redistricting, my district has become more competitive than ever and is now THE battleground district. The path to majority in the House runs through OH-01. The only way we can win is with organizers knocking doors, talking to voters, and ads that cut through the noise. **Your support right now will power the team, boost our outreach, and protect our ability to keep fighting for working families.** [**So, now that I’ve introduced myself, can I ask you to chip in $5 today to keep this campaign moving?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123199885/594676307/-1481197821?amount=5&refcode=01.12.26_og_fr_bio&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDg5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZWRhMjIwZS0wMWYwLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=u9iQE8c1I0ZEVt10WJMgUixYkckXDqgF3uRFh7QmpBE=) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now.

name: Team Becca Balint
subject: We Can’t Let ICE Get Away With This.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont**
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123199939/594676481/-785282667?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTFiZDk0MS0wMWYwLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EdTpW-v91pCdWG5DXy9UpioHmApAsKKQT4Y-YNwBRak=)
---
Peter, This past week has shaken the nation — and here at home in Vermont, we’re watching a community cry out for justice and accountability after the tragic killing of Renee Nicole Good. On January 7 in Minneapolis, federal immigration agents with ICE surrounded Renee’s vehicle and within seconds, an agent fired shots that ended her life. Renee — a 37-year-old mother, writer, and neighbor — was simply trying to leave when she was shot and killed. **The video tells a devastating story, and millions of people who’ve seen it are asking the same question: why was deadly force used?** **Becca Balint was one of the first leaders to stand up and speak out. She didn’t wait. She didn’t look the other way. She called this tragedy what it is — a failure of accountability. ** **[If Becca’s leadership in moments like this matters to you, will you chip in $5, $10, or whatever you can today to help her keep showing up for our communities?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123199940/594676482/-2088622038?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTFiZDk0MS0wMWYwLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EdTpW-v91pCdWG5DXy9UpioHmApAsKKQT4Y-YNwBRak=)** **[CHIP IN TODAY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123199941/594676483/-2088622038?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTFiZDk0MS0wMWYwLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EdTpW-v91pCdWG5DXy9UpioHmApAsKKQT4Y-YNwBRak=)** From Burlington to cities nationwide, thousands upon thousands of folks are protesting to demand accountability for Renee Good, condemn reckless use of force, and protect civil liberties. People are showing up peacefully and loudly to say: _**NO MORE!**_ Meanwhile, the current federal administration is doubling down on aggressive immigration enforcement and defending the actions that took Renee’s life. In moments like this, we need leaders with courage and conviction — leaders who don’t just show up for the cameras, but who actually fight to make change happen. **Becca Balint has shown up again and again with empathy and fire:**
* She has listened to families torn apart by ICE and federal overreach.
* She speaks clearly and courageously about what happened and what must change, even when others choose silence.
* She pushes back against the powerful, holding agencies and officials accountable when their actions harm everyday communities.
* She has never been afraid to fight for everyday families — advocating for safety, fairness, and dignity long before the headlines demand it.

**That’s the kind of leadership this moment demands — and the kind of leadership Becca brings _EVERY. SINGLE. clean_body: | = Peter, This past week has shaken the nation — and here at home in Vermont, we’re watching a community cry out for justice and accountability after the tragic killing of Renee Nicole Good. On January 7 in Minneapolis, federal immigration agents with ICE surrounded Renee’s vehicle and within seconds, an agent fired shots that ended her life. Renee — a 37-year-old mother, writer, and neighbor — was simply trying to leave when she was shot and killed. The video tells a devastating story, and millions of people who’ve seen it are asking the same question: why was deadly force used? Becca Balint was one of the first leaders to stand up and speak out. She didn’t wait. She didn’t look the other way. She called this tragedy what it is — a failure of accountability. = = From Burlington to cities nationwide, thousands upon thousands of folks are protesting to demand accountability for Renee Good, condemn reckless use of force, and protect civil liberties. People are showing up peacefully and loudly to say: NO MORE! Meanwhile, the current federal administration is doubling down on aggressive immigration enforcement and defending the actions that took Renee’s life. In moments like this, we need leaders with courage and conviction — leaders who don’t just show up for the cameras, but who actually fight to make change happen. Becca Balint has shown up again and again with empathy and fire: She has listened to families torn apart by ICE and federal overreach. She speaks clearly and courageously about what happened and what must change, even when others choose silence. She pushes back against the powerful, holding agencies and officials accountable when their actions harm everyday communities. She has never been afraid to fight for everyday families — advocating for safety, fairness, and dignity long before the headlines demand it. That’s the kind of leadership this moment demands — and the kind of leadership Becca brings EVERY. SINGLE. DAY. = Thank you for standing with us and for standing up for justice and accountability when so many are choosing silence. Always with you in this fight, Team Becca Balint If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = mailto:beccabalintvt@beccabalint.com | Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont P.O. Box 291 Burlington, VT 05402

America is the land of the free. Federal agents executing citizens and expanding ICE facilities is not freedom. We can be the check and balance.

| [|
---](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/71x71DMAzdhF2sv7E-waHg~~/AAUS1hA~/4O-1t-ulLqbC-pp6CwBMw5STxRrjG_HIj17mTeGXjZi8Xu-etmC9OkNYkEURau7rx6bzV0WvR8XcFEFqarR_5t7NTULCNnhfGtFPxYputvIcPyniNjQBLAkzfAO_ycEK)
| | Friend, This was not just an important weekend for my campaign. It was a defining moment for our nation. I stood shoulder to shoulder with concerned community members, veterans, parents, and people who still believe in democracy and the American dream at **two anti ICE protests right here in our community**. America calls itself the **land of the free**. That belief is supposed to mean something. Non compliance with unjust power is baked into our history and into our blood. It is part of who we are as a people. We were not in the streets to create chaos. We were there because what is happening in this country cannot be ignored. The killing of **Renee Good** by a federal ICE agent shook people across the nation. An American citizen is dead after an encounter that never required lethal force. That fact alone should stop us all in our tracks. Instead of accountability, Washington is responding with escalation. Right now there are plans being pushed to open **another ICE facility right here in Colorado’s Fourth District near Hudson run by a private prison company**. At a moment when communities are demanding answers and justice, the response is more detention, more force, and more repression. Let me be absolutely clear. **We do not stand for a nation that allows federal agents to execute American citizens. We will not stand for this level of repression.** Standing in the cold this weekend, I looked around and saw something powerful. Veterans who swore an oath to defend the Constitution. Parents worried about the country their children are inheriting. Working people who still believe that freedom, dignity, and due process matter. We can and must continue to oppose the ruthless Trump administration and ICE tactics that are brutalizing communities across this country. This is where leadership matters. We know **Lauren Boebert will rubber stamp every funding and appropriations bill** that expands ICE power and allows this to continue unchecked. She will not ask hard questions. She will not demand accountability. She will not stand with our communities. **We can be the check and the balance.** That is why I am running for Congress. I believe our government should protect communities, not intimidate them. I believe accountability is not optional. I believe civil rights and due process are not negotiable. And I believe silence in moments like this is complicity. But I cannot do this alone. **Will you stand with us today to hold ICE accountable?** Your contribution today will help us:
* * **Hold real community round tables and town halls across CO 04**
* **Defeat Lauren Boebert and stop rubber stamp funding of ICE abuses**
* **Demand transparency and accountability from those in power**
* **Build a people powered campaign that puts communities first**

If you are able, please consider a contribution - **whatever you can give today**. Every donation helps us stand stronger and speak louder. This is a defining moment for our country and for our district. I am committed to standing up. I am asking you to stand with me. With resolve, **[As we ring in the New Year, chip in now to close the year strong and power the movement to beat Lauren Boebert.](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/mfw1FpCX8-BERqe9jqmyJA~~/AAUS1hA~/UwYUB-1NO35BH7C0IIzH9DOka4S8C3gPlZJHqIGpPQZENN6djgrLyYXMmjs353rRNWteP1bOGaY-q_T1nZDUKkBua1bp0AQtEToxJVHOwNAtWdIc6XpoRdcNWzYJwyAZoxPDlUQVue0aBCzv7iBvVuIxuDb1Yso9dMYDTMToGqI~)** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | [Chip in $10 now >>](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/QE1pBnOe2QD03giJAEfNmA~~/AAUS1hA~/z6IKJeuIXMFxE6Bbp5oEzSQWMHymeoFrhSPw6mGkLHnMYhKTSt4WV06e8HZrj9ZoygCqtpxyqKJwXpCnHhzT-1hXLd0WBNK242liUGVBfGSrdgN4JDQx2HQzHe4f04rKID1l-7r3U8TcjhYqe5g54OQSpKkKs64exZsHsHRGXhRNaDyyS_BT_aG2s1SAuDo2) | [Chip in $25 now >>](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/JOOOPUS9NkUH7Sj6oXG_Ew~~/AAUS1hA~/z6IKJeuIXMFxE6Bbp5oEzSQWMHymeoFrhSPw6mGkLHnMYhKTSt4WV06e8HZrj9ZoygCqtpxyqKJwXpCnHhzT-1hXLd0WBNK242liUGVBfGSkJlVgFY3mjWkwstg6mcda4Qp1fFjrF4AmmgEMnFSm-UzhtSKvnGuJX6EPj8SVVmPGDxGHQA8n9rD6ESlf7xTd)

name: Robert Garcia
subject: Republicans think they can win here

body:
Don’t let Republicans steal this seat
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123200791/594679434/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZDEwNjM2ZC0wMmYwLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1WI9Ljp9rIjUd8Byfu7ktIgucU_-Y_0HD1HoH-9uAZw=)
Redistricting changed the game in California — and Republicans think they've found their opening.
Because of new district lines, I could soon be representing parts of conservative Orange County. That makes this race one of the GOP’s top targets in 2026 — and it means we have no time to waste.
**[So I’m asking you right now: can you chip in today to help us get ahead of this fight?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123200793/594679436/789508428?refcode=260111_RG_steal-seat&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZDEwNjM2ZC0wMmYwLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1WI9Ljp9rIjUd8Byfu7ktIgucU_-Y_0HD1HoH-9uAZw=)**
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123200794/594679438/789508428?refcode=260111_RG_steal-seat&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZDEwNjM2ZC0wMmYwLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1WI9Ljp9rIjUd8Byfu7ktIgucU_-Y_0HD1HoH-9uAZw=)**
This race is already underway.
The moment the calendar flipped to 2026, the race accelerated more than ever before. Republicans are organizing, fundraising, and preparing to flip this seat. If we wait, we fall behind — and I’m not willing to let that happen.
I’ve fought to deliver results for working families, hold the powerful accountable, and defend our democracy. Now, because of redistricting, I’ll be reaching new communities where MAGA Republicans think they can win.
**They’re wrong. But proving them wrong takes work — and it takes resources.**
**[Winning a tougher district means starting early, building a serious operation, and hitting the ground running right now. Every day matters. Every contribution helps us define this race before our opponents do. If you believe this seat is worth fighting for, I’m asking again: please chip in today and help us start strong.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123200796/594679439/789508428?refcode=260111_RG_steal-seat&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZDEwNjM2ZC0wMmYwLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1WI9Ljp9rIjUd8Byfu7ktIgucU_-Y_0HD1HoH-9uAZw=)**
[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123200798/594679441/-309737922?refcode=260111_RG_steal-seat&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZDEwNjM2ZC0wMmYwLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1WI9Ljp9rIjUd8Byfu7ktIgucU_-Y_0HD1HoH-9uAZw=)
The lines have changed. The stakes are higher. And this race has already started.
—Robert Garcia

name: Voter Action Project HQ
subject: Vengeful partisans want to control Houston's elections
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Why did the Texas governor make this threat? Because vengeful partisans want to protect their power and give themselves the ability to overturn elections. That's why they've passed these bills, started a mid-decade redistricting war, and are after any excuse for a blatant power grab in a state with a terrible history of voter suppression.
Our elections and fundamental right to vote are under attack, Team. RUSH $35 or whatever you can today to make every vote count >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=011226_2&amount=35]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Democracy Docket: Texas governor threatens state takeover of Houston elections [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=011226_2&amount=25]
Opponents of voting rights are after Houston again, Team.
Texas Gov. Greg Abbott recently threatened Harris County (which contains Houston) with a state takeover of its elections. This is just the latest step in an ongoing escalation of long-simmering threats to gain control of voting in the fourth-largest city in the United States.
Why? Because a GOP state lawmaker alleged that 100 voters were unlawfully registered using post office box addresses instead of street addresses. 100 potentially inaccurate registrations out of more than 2.5 million registered voters and a county population of 4.7 million people -- 0.04% of registrations. Imagine taking over a whole county's election process over four-hundredths of a percentage point?
The vote suppressors have targeted Houston for years. They started after widespread use of mail-in ballots drove high voter participation during the pandemic, attacking how the county collected ballots. Then Gov. Abbott signed two suppressive bills into law that only affected Harris County and no others in the state. Now he's threatening a full partisan takeover of Houston's voting process in order to restrict ballot access in the county where his party does the worst in elections.
Opponents of voting rights in Texas have gone too far, Team, and we need to fight back. Chip in with $10, $20, $35 or whatever you can right now to stop suppression like this and defend our fundamental right to vote in Houston and across the country >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=011226_2&amount=35]

Need a refresher? In 2023, Texas's state legislature passed SB-1993, which empowers a partisan state official to override the vote in counties with populations higher than 2.7 million if they run low on paper ballots for more than an hour anytime on election day. Harris County -- home to Houston and those 4.7 million people -- was the only Texas county with a population that big and potentially subject to partisan takeover.
Opponents of voting rights consistently attack our cities and the voters who live in and around them. It's part of their explicit strategy as their ability to tip the scales in elections has eroded and barriers to voting have been broken down. One of the 2023 laws was even written by an anti-voting Houston-area state senator in order to prop up his own party and protect their power in a state that's continually sliding away from MAGA.
Voter suppression anywhere hurts us everywhere, Team. Fuel our efforts to fight back with $35 or whatever you can today to make sure our votes count and everyone is heard in our elections >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=011226_2&amount=35]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.

That's why they've passed these bills, started a mid-decade redistricting war, and are after any excuse for a blatant power grab in a state with a terrible history of voter suppression. This is just the latest step in an ongoing escalation of long-simmering threats to gain control of voting in the fourth-largest city in the United States. Why? Because a GOP state lawmaker alleged that 100 voters were unlawfully registered using post office box addresses instead of street addresses. 100 potentially inaccurate registrations out of more than 2.5 million registered voters and a county population of 4.7 million people -- 0.04% of registrations. name: Chris Pappas
subject: I’m *still* sorry about Karoline Leavitt
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
We already rejected MAGA talking points in New Hampshire. Now national Republicans are dusting off their playbook.
# [](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1&akid=4532%2E582294%2EAtH_K3)
**I’m sorry about Karoline Leavitt.**
A few years ago, Karoline Leavitt ran against me for Congress here in New Hampshire. Her campaign was steeped in extreme MAGA rhetoric and zero connection to the actual issues Granite Staters face every day. **Voters saw right through it and rejected her agenda by nearly 10 points.**
[](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260112_2.r54BqW&refcode=em20260112-2&t=2&akid=4532%2E582294%2EAtH_K3)
Unfortunately that race turned into a national audition. Now the same talking points and spin that New Hampshire voters dismissed are being used daily from the White House briefing room.
So if you’re wondering how the rest of the country ended up having to listen to Karoline Leavitt, it’s because New Hampshire proved something important first: Her extreme ideas don’t hold up when you put them in front of real voters.
But that hasn’t stopped the MAGA machine from trying. **National Republicans are pouring money into New Hampshire because they think they can break through and notch a win here to cling to their Senate majority.** They’ve lined up behind John Sununu, an NRSC-backed candidate who will vote with Donald Trump and MAGA leadership when it matters most. It’s a new candidate reading from the same old playbook.
I’ve beaten their playbook before by focusing on kitchen-table issues, standing up for families, and telling the truth. **[Now I need your help to do it again. Will you chip in $5 today to help us keep this Senate seat blue and fight for what matters to Granite Staters?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260112_2.r54BqW&refcode=em20260112-2&t=3&akid=4532%2E582294%2EAtH_K3)**
Granite Staters value independence. They reward common sense. And they don’t like being sold a script written somewhere else.
If you believe New Hampshire should keep leading the way and showing the rest of the country that MAGA politics don’t work here, I’m asking you to be a part of our campaign. **If Democrats are going to flip the Senate, we need to win this open race in New Hampshire.**
**[Will you chip in today to help us keep this Senate seat blue and stop national Republicans from installing another vote for Trump’s agenda?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260112_2.r54BqW&refcode=em20260112-2&t=4&akid=4532%2E582294%2EAtH_K3)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260112_2.r54BqW&amount=5&refcode=em20260112-2&t=23&akid=4532%2E582294%2EAtH_K3)**
Thanks for being in this fight with me.
Chris
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer?refcode=em20260112-2&t=24&refcode2=4532_582294_AtH_K3&akid=4532%2E582294%2EAtH_K3)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_receive_fewer_emails/?t=25&akid=4532%2E582294%2EAtH_K3)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_update_your_information/?t=26&akid=4532%2E582294%2EAtH_K3)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrispappas.org/unsubscribe/cps_unsubscribe/?t=27&akid=4532%2E582294%2EAtH_K3)

Email us: [teampappas@chrispappas.org](mailto:teampappas@chrispappas.org)
Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
Chris Pappas for Senate
PO Box 313
Manchester, NH 03105
United States

name: Rick Larsen
subject: Our fight for health care is not over
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen
body:
Millions of Americans woke up to their health care premiums increasing with the new year.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123201302/594681295/-201694504?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NmZhMmVmOC0wNWYwLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExMWY5MDMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=498itMNgfbbiEiSVqjsKWbhEr6_FHIgEYg7jmghK-b4=&emci=039db67f-62ef-f011-8194-000d3a11f903&emdi=76fa2ef8-05f0-f011-8194-000d3a11f903&ceid=9494239)
---

| | **Ensuring access to high-quality, affordable health care is essential for people in Northwest Washington and across the country.** I am fighting for you every day to ensure you have access to affordable health care. On December 31st of 2025, Republicans let expire health care tax credits that were helping people I represent in Northwest Washington. These health care tax credits helped people purchase affordable health care. Because Republicans let those health care tax credits expire, health care prices are artificially inflated for thousands of people in Northwest Washington and millions of people across the country. Last week, the House passed a three-year extension of the health care tax credits to help lower health care premiums for millions of Americans. **Our fight for health care is not over yet.** Now, I call upon the Senate to take up the three-year health care credit extension. The Senate must take up this extension so we can deliver lower prices for Americans on their health care. clean_body: Millions of Americans woke up to their health care premiums increasing with the new year. | | | | Ensuring access to high-quality, affordable health care is essential for people in Northwest Washington and across the country. I am fighting for you every day to ensure you have access to affordable health care. On December 31st of 2025, Republicans let expire health care tax credits that were helping people I represent in Northwest Washington. These health care tax credits helped people purchase affordable health care. Because Republicans let those health care tax credits expire, health care prices are artificially inflated for thousands of people in Northwest Washington and millions of people across the country. Last week, the House passed a three-year extension of the health care tax credits to help lower health care premiums for millions of Americans. Our fight for health care is not over yet. Now, I call upon the Senate to take up the three-year health care credit extension. The Senate must take up this extension so we can deliver lower prices for Americans on their health care. More soon, Rick | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Rick Larsen instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | Paid for and authorized by Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen. | This email was sent by . name: Adam Schwarze, former Navy SEAL
subject: This could end one of two ways
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
As a former Navy SEAL, I thrive in tough fights. I’ve faced worse odds overseas and am ready to bring that same grit to Washington |

---

Fellow Conservative,

My name is Adam Schwarze, and I’m a former Navy SEAL, Marine Corps veteran, and proud Minnesotan **running for U.S. Senate against Tim Walz’s handpicked lieutenant governor—a politician even more radical than her boss.** I’m requesting your support in this critical battle to restore common-sense conservative values to Minnesota.

I hold a Master’s from Harvard, a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins in Security and Nuclear Deterrence, and an MBA. But my proudest achievement is serving our country to make my parents, especially my mom, proud. I want to serve you in the U.S. Senate, fighting for the values that have made Minnesota and America great for generations.

[](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=42f2c512efc746c4b4f99921f6180bdf&_e=PNHKYHvDcwfjYC5pOmb1c5-mZ9438uNqZYutPmpiGItkw68K1aq6yxeDYNnx2h0_zI7c3OOuRLg41L-2JU0QPyamuuYY3JfxVYzHMHnc2APQc9T7WasEKrjqY5MbK_UGHHl5I8oQM-50bX6tN-YjtrF798Lma4nK8GIezVhXE9bTPu26Xc7Y5mmL1YlBbyIE19FDtl8DlywDm9Ukfzi3hfvvHw56zUBlg8fbnXCffOs5Bq7Tp-5AbKYpRItPCCu9LU-AjQGnXVg8JWL3q8iV7eSEu1VB-nMD3BA5K8x7HxoTTXZ2mNfnrx6izSKAX3CjtE_cLB4Bw0l1SwPJCWe90P03zsxqnX4WshZfdo9q4SpK5KyNIoHHympKvNcHGn2-OsGJH2rIpsjCi2H0OsbjK1I4vo1WWT5qINdeedvZhx1FkbTZQPfUvuvZ_q2tJB-Miw7a8h2khA6v6MTQLW8tj6CXIZPWAKHvUK-qT5QZdnZhWGm1poaPKG3RCFPojCapynY_GPPFTPBZjQ0Q1cuiA8c7k-QEreGCLKZtkTuD2UMco3CdUPvD1vRzyIms8ro1Gv5-Fy2Fa6pSoZnV095IcukkzRriMD-f1-NqgkmvsHNtaTTlT81ig6A7DyXW1RmAuR6e9j3oHhMenSMgb6m7g90sLVx64QRizauWxmKblcip3cD1Q7LsqD3s9sQy92PbYbhyKDah3W7jIt5ZZWNMBcPdBwm4yV9rrYRGHk_NlEQbCVSE8kgb-nIj2x6ZbmbTn9KbTUEjrIgDQxHK_yrD4gsEWJZ_ZG-0zD53MgbLgyM%3D)

**I’m starting this race with absolutely nothing** , but because of the enormity of the race's outcome, I figured there is no harm in asking for your help. At the end of the day, my request of you will end in one of two ways:
* 1.) **[you contribute to help elect a former Navy SEAL to the United States Senate](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=42f2c512efc746c4b4f99921f6180bdf&_e=PNHKYHvDcwfjYC5pOmb1c5-mZ9438uNqZYutPmpiGItkw68K1aq6yxeDYNnx2h0_zI7c3OOuRLg41L-2JU0QPyamuuYY3JfxVYzHMHnc2APQc9T7WasEKrjqY5MbK_UGHHl5I8oQM-50bX6tN-YjtrF798Lma4nK8GIezVhXE9bTPu26Xc7Y5mmL1YlBbyIE19FDtl8DlywDm9Ukfzi3hfvvHw56zUBlg8fbnXCffOs5Bq7Tp-5AbKYpRItPCCu9LU-AjQGnXVg8JWL3q8iV7eSEu1VB-nMD3BA5K8x7HxppzEYYWoto1D2OZCPMELHf88tYFvHuGrT-jJKoARF7FuFb3pvQw3DOx2wURFKAgc4Bxke7OsssXdurItEzDpU3mSo9Sg201IykQDlcnTrIP-nG2md0NG3gCdBuJnSC-HlPr884D8zyTqdcVRGE2V3J2s8fziZHoPSueJPahqTJaxLf6T66eoMdmbUlNoIfUcqhNzbI4q9Nw1sactbKn3H5ZEfDqTNOcsrphkJeuFwMFRBj4gBSPHceM5i5NzfsIF66JMdrTYtNpcwgEgI2BxaCQcQExzef8goCzI1gFbeyDAkHxU6cfVj9BltPI0yDU9abxovJAYJCWHPFZp5Wdu6sfdEPcy21Rs4OFKmS2vILz_ahklV7uXA4DLek5c1oHRcu7P5-5cOr-2r6XfZm1Jl9klDLnWJZqcKXuU9rJEAQkixMIYxL3hGHO2k0Ldx03z1SR4FUHsofd2fi89NCUNOFblZVWRFe95DfXDHe9KO9wTSJUMRgcB9xTX0wi65hBfE%3D)**.
* 2.) you read this email without contributing, but you at least now know who I am.

[**HELP ME GET MY CAMPAIGN OFF THE GROUND
CONTRIBUTE $2 OR MORE **](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=42f2c512efc746c4b4f99921f6180bdf&_e=PNHKYHvDcwfjYC5pOmb1c5-mZ9438uNqZYutPmpiGItkw68K1aq6yxeDYNnx2h0_zI7c3OOuRLg41L-2JU0QPyamuuYY3JfxVYzHMHnc2APQc9T7WasEKrjqY5MbK_UGHHl5I8oQM-50bX6tN-YjtrF798Lma4nK8GIezVhXE9bTPu26Xc7Y5mmL1YlBbyIE19FDtl8DlywDm9Ukfzi3hfvvHw56zUBlg8fbnXCffOs5Bq7Tp-5AbKYpRItPCCu9LU-AjQGnXVg8JWL3q8iV7eSEu1VB-nMD3BA5K8x7HxpTARYOI7WA7aKcxxRB8Qf9NZd48GXfLGXAqPJUz6Ay1rO3QlSa1_VIN-IBI2Av4NnVKjLTh7bsfAXh-MC_RQbXIMrMAdpjHsoghDhCuBeKme_WCbkuiopswTkon67ax5qKU2GvpOOWU9NL-4Cet2NhMj7O30A0t2webT9LVBEJYs_6ha_CqYbWgiUXHoFzE-TRtQnM6_TQSBfry78V9N2IcdeA5z9LgLTRT4OxwI_pG-C9xMR0HlmCcnebI8tEtWrTObwrma9m1I9Ew9P25pdrNmfxJjelM32BUQicq4XECEOuHv2DOh4n9D7a1RgidH2b1HVX_lm1izgRvysZAw2AJFraMSzeRkHHzSejTD_65g8Qr29_SoYB-6bTlBy5pP1PapRzAxxtDn3tw0nX5uGdB_W26KJ02UoenCpaAo74d7D3S1PQSp4U6h_0ONYrajb7BC0rAjNhZNvAewNqNkwKHhQ9zx_NpAYYhpAnQ1BVHnyZZhQYDW057bzfdY0hjyw%3D)
**I’m not a career politician. I’m a regular guy from a working-class family who grew up playing hockey and answered our nation’s call on my 18th birthday in October 2001, just weeks after the 9/11 attacks.** I joined the Marine Corps to defend our country, and as a Navy SEAL, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries, risking my life to protect our freedoms and way of life. Unlike Tim Walz, who retired from the National Guard before his unit deployed to Iraq, I never backed down from serving our nation during war.

Minnesota is at a crossroads. Under Governor Walz and his lieutenant governor, our state has been dragged left by radical policies that fueled the violent unrest in Minneapolis in 2020, **defunded our police** , and burdened hardworking families with higher taxes and inflation. **While our streets burned, the lieutenant governor pushed lawsuits against law enforcement, undermining the officers keeping us safe.** This isn’t the Minnesota I grew up in—or the one any Minnesotan deserves.

**If you are reading this email right now and you don’t live in Minnesota, then I’m sure you’ll agree with me that every American must be represented by individuals in Congress who want to build a better version of the America we live in today—individuals of integrity.**

_**That’s not who my opponent is. **_

We have an incredible opportunity to elect a Republican to the U.S. Senate next year - something voters in this state haven’t done since they re-elected former Senator Norm Coleman in 2002. As a Republican, I know the odds are tough in a state that hasn’t voted red in a presidential election since 1976. But **as a former Navy SEAL, I thrive in tough fights. I’ve faced worse odds overseas and am ready to bring that same grit to Washington.**

[](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=42f2c512efc746c4b4f99921f6180bdf&_e=PNHKYHvDcwfjYC5pOmb1c5-mZ9438uNqZYutPmpiGItkw68K1aq6yxeDYNnx2h0_zI7c3OOuRLg41L-2JU0QPyamuuYY3JfxVYzHMHnc2APQc9T7WasEKrjqY5MbK_UGHHl5I8oQM-50bX6tN-YjtrF798Lma4nK8GIezVhXE9bTPu26Xc7Y5mmL1YlBbyIE19FDtl8DlywDm9Ukfzi3hfvvHw56zUBlg8fbnXCffOs5Bq7Tp-5AbKYpRItPCCu9LU-AjQGnXVg8JWL3q8iV7eSEu1VB-nMD3BA5K8x7Hxr3oBElB9As2u4IamCRO_v9H9WPtnCvXubylCji06Kx_p6GiX_HilxR5gs_qKMwikoTlkLiK9BAmHxdgkJP_khfts47HFctCk8s0NI41QpBOm9sdtgZlFDK8UiyzfuHMGwZu-dujFOi3lKbsIkLFW-nWtYMtBzXxT3zyVa37UhKrIsapICTuuQtNJ4FZZMJ68laHQ26OKphyMF-DEM4PdMK_789_1YgnZe3wBuZpgUs2U8g0MbHpwC51qyrCTbmS2L9IdtGe8ipGFafNNyUfOPg3ko26025Vf0iNV-ZjODpRS-zMoXCs5MM0_gKwwZZq-gz0FHfNaOvw2Mx8Jzgp5Umuu45ryuZTketKwir6j7o5uO1sEEBBEDS6lTJdmaDKlBB_3LuI2-hGCNHUv55nPt4uIOi6gs2180fI_le2Od75VtOqNRTSMKzEsKXLkcTyUPP71ZwbzrwNor-Lkcw1s04v83KbXBlvJuOfPmMzSz0ot4A992wwlMd1chTTPAGAAo%3D)

Here’s what I stand for:
* Restoring law and order by supporting our police and cracking down on crime.
* Cutting taxes and regulations to ease the burden on retirees and small businesses.
* Securing our borders to protect our communities and sovereignty.
* Defending our Second Amendment rights and traditional values.
* Stopping the radical left’s agenda that’s eroding the America we love.

You don’t know me, but you know what men like me stand for and our reputation. So, **[I’m asking you to take a chance on me and support my campaign today, in its infancy, to help me get it off the ground and give us a shot at reclaiming a Senate seat we haven’t held in a generation](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=42f2c512efc746c4b4f99921f6180bdf&_e=PNHKYHvDcwfjYC5pOmb1c5-mZ9438uNqZYutPmpiGItkw68K1aq6yxeDYNnx2h0_zI7c3OOuRLg41L-2JU0QPyamuuYY3JfxVYzHMHnc2APQc9T7WasEKrjqY5MbK_UGHHl5I8oQM-50bX6tN-YjtrF798Lma4nK8GIezVhXE9bTPu26Xc7Y5mmL1YlBbyIE19FDtl8DlywDm9Ukfzi3hfvvHw56zUBlg8fbnXCffOs5Bq7Tp-5AbKYpRItPCCu9LU-AjQGnXVg8JWL3q8iV7eSEu1VB-nMD3BA5K8x7Hxo8oqucxQieIQfJiCAgRMYLnLROPzG3I0xFqW0HyUdMihKmJhuUvJnd7moFdxuHiUu1C1WOK7Qp8qr63tAbqrkwCED98uZNyrih8T5heXSSvHfN2EjWmz8UrYCayj-pbb2fP4G8iEqZMFFfhHxH6sLdn2XU19-4HDqgmpKFoXAhI5yvgECzlcKOe-S6dc2L-A88fSWDpfDWK-VcO1YprYaBggsczkuZ3BadqYGH6RikDbF-qqopWDHSXzVa2NZ-PGeBP2ziKgdY59fMjZtCZJnBtniMlwTXdXDiU7CFfZVnEtiUopkIJV2aMIBSqIK1hyUcEM8oh9XTFo0kEG3kHp3Zx5LuGlgnBSzvsYosoMaJDyc3uCgjijS0nhV4wcN5OugyxCiKytoKL4o6m5SrzD9OBzMG0UcNPnBJfm-KZku2-G4idj-jTXHLvEBLyn-nn85uv0PxMRH9MhDMVL7CbZ_-421llkfeXfX5XY18NnNGw2PF_BAGZnZiLtoiK2n2RxE%3D)**.

clean_body: As a former Navy SEAL, I thrive in tough fights. I’ve faced worse odds overseas and am ready to bring that same grit to Washington | Fellow Conservative, My name is Adam Schwarze, and I’m a former Navy SEAL, Marine Corps veteran, and proud Minnesotan running for U.S. Senate against Tim Walz’s handpicked lieutenant governor—a politician even more radical than her boss. I’m requesting your support in this critical battle to restore common-sense conservative values to Minnesota. I hold a Master’s from Harvard, a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins in Security and Nuclear Deterrence, and an MBA. But my proudest achievement is serving our country to make my parents, especially my mom, proud. I want to serve you in the U.S. Senate, fighting for the values that have made Minnesota and America great for generations. I’m starting this race with absolutely nothing , but because of the enormity of the race's outcome, I figured there is no harm in asking for your help. At the end of the day, my request of you will end in one of two ways: 1.) . 2.) you read this email without contributing, but you at least now know who I am. I’m not a career politician. I’m a regular guy from a working-class family who grew up playing hockey and answered our nation’s call on my 18th birthday in October 2001, just weeks after the 9/11 attacks. I joined the Marine Corps to defend our country, and as a Navy SEAL, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries, risking my life to protect our freedoms and way of life. Unlike Tim Walz, who retired from the National Guard before his unit deployed to Iraq, I never backed down from serving our nation during war. Minnesota is at a crossroads. Under Governor Walz and his lieutenant governor, our state has been dragged left by radical policies that fueled the violent unrest in Minneapolis in 2020, defunded our police , and burdened hardworking families with higher taxes and inflation. While our streets burned, the lieutenant governor pushed lawsuits against law enforcement, undermining the officers keeping us safe. This isn’t the Minnesota I grew up in—or the one any Minnesotan deserves. If you are reading this email right now and you don’t live in Minnesota, then I’m sure you’ll agree with me that every American must be represented by individuals in Congress who want to build a better version of the America we live in today—individuals of integrity. That’s not who my opponent is. We have an incredible opportunity to elect a Republican to the U.S. Senate next year - something voters in this state haven’t done since they re-elected former Senator Norm Coleman in 2002. As a Republican, I know the odds are tough in a state that hasn’t voted red in a presidential election since 1976. But as a former Navy SEAL, I thrive in tough fights. I’ve faced worse odds overseas and am ready to bring that same grit to Washington. Here’s what I stand for: Restoring law and order by supporting our police and cracking down on crime. Cutting taxes and regulations to ease the burden on retirees and small businesses. Securing our borders to protect our communities and sovereignty. Defending our Second Amendment rights and traditional values. Stopping the radical left’s agenda that’s eroding the America we love. You don’t know me, but you know what men like me stand for and our reputation. So, . This race won’t be easy. The Democrats have deep pockets, and the radical left will stop at nothing to keep this seat. I’m running to serve you, to protect our way of life, and to ensure Minnesota remains a place where faith, family, and freedom thrive. I hope you’ll invest in me right now – any amount will help. With gratitude, Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA** | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate

subject: Do you see the pattern?

body:
All eyes are on Georgia’s critical Senate race.
#

Peter, we need you to see this:
[](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-jan26.wPRyEn&refcode=em260112-1-subs-a&t=1&akid=1422%2E5691347%2Ecse1K2)
First, The Hill named Georgia’s Senate seat as one of the most likely seats to flip in 2026. Then, Roll Call called Jon the most vulnerable Senator up for reelection.
And now, The New York Times has named their top races to watch in the midterms — and Jon Ossoff was one of their top picks.
_Do you see the pattern?_
**Folks are realizing all eyes are going to be on Georgia this year, and the stakes could not be higher.**
We’re expecting this race to be the most competitive and expensive in the nation. Republicans and their dark money Super PACs will flood hundreds of millions of dollars into Georgia to defeat Jon. And we need to be able to combat the outside spending and spread our message to voters across the state.
[**That’s why we’re calling on you again, Peter. If you’re ready to start the year off strong and help us win big in November, we’re asking if you’ll contribute any amount to Jon Ossoff’s campaign today. name: Mary Peltola
subject: My decision
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
| | |
---
[](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52532?refcode=jtk461-bu-fl&t=1&akid=10533%2E434096%2EazchfL)
Derek -
My name is Mary Peltola, and I want to share with you why I've made the decision to run for the United States Senate in Alaska — and to ask for your support as we begin this journey together.
But if you already understand how important this race and your support are, and you’re ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please use this link to split a contribution today:
[https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jf-email-peltola/founding-donor](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52532?refcode=jtk461-bu-fl&t=2&akid=10533%2E434096%2EazchfL)
| | [Donate](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52532?refcode=jtk461-bu-fl&t=3&akid=10533%2E434096%2EazchfL)
---
Now here’s a bit more on how I came to this decision.
When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance.
Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity.
Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find.
So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just crushing Alaska families.
And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. They don’t even believe us.
They're focused on their own wallets — not ours.
And when they actually work together on something, it’s usually to help themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors feather their own nests.
It’s why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted.
I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at home.
Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can actually afford.
Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again.
My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning.
But if we’re going to secure Alaska’s future, we need another priority: fighting corruption.
I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our future.
**This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election, and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we’ll be able to win this race... and that is together.**
So I am asking:
[Please contribute to my campaign for the U.S. Senate and John Fetterman today to become a Founding Donor. We are looking for a huge number of day one donors to get our campaign off to a strong start and put us in a position to win. No donation is too small. They will all add up fast.](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52532?refcode=jtk461-bu-fl&t=4&akid=10533%2E434096%2EazchfL)
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately and be split between Mary Peltola and John Fetterman:_ | | | [DONATE $5 NOW](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52534?refcode=jtk461-bu-fl&t=5&akid=10533%2E434096%2EazchfL)
---
| | [DONATE $25 NOW](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52535?refcode=jtk461-bu-fl&t=6&akid=10533%2E434096%2EazchfL)
---
| | [DONATE $50 NOW](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52536?refcode=jtk461-bu-fl&t=7&akid=10533%2E434096%2EazchfL)
---
| | [DONATE ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52532?refcode=jtk461-bu-fl&t=8&akid=10533%2E434096%2EazchfL)
---
Thank you,
Mary

But if you already understand how important this race and your support are, and you’re ready to become a Founding Donor to our campaign, please use this link to split a contribution today: | | Now here’s a bit more on how I came to this decision. When I was growing up in Bethel, salmon fishing with my father in the summers and dog mushing in the winters, Alaska was a place of abundance. Now, all across Alaska, we have scarcity. Multi-national companies are trawling our waters and decimating our fish stocks. The moose, caribou, and migratory birds that used to fill our freezers are harder to find. So we buy more groceries, where the prices are just crushing Alaska families. And I've got to tell you, DC people do not care that we are paying 17 bucks for a gallon of milk. They don’t even believe us. They're focused on their own wallets — not ours. And when they actually work together on something, it’s usually to help themselves. The rigged system in DC is shutting down Alaska and hurting all of us while politicians and their millionaire and billionaire donors feather their own nests. It’s why our prices are rising, and our heating bills keep going up. All while the health care and services Alaskans rely on get gutted. I'm done with politicians getting richer while things get tougher here at home. Systemic change is the only way to bring down grocery costs, save our fisheries, lower energy prices, and build new housing Alaskans can actually afford. Systemic change is the only way to fill our homes with abundance again. My agenda for Alaska will always be: fish, family, freedom. These values have been the core values of my campaigns since the very beginning. But if we’re going to secure Alaska’s future, we need another priority: fighting corruption. I'm running for Alaska's Senate seat because it is the fight for our future. This is going to be one of the closest Senate races of the next election, and I cannot do this alone. There is only one way we’ll be able to win this race... and that is together. So I am asking: If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately and be split between Mary Peltola and John Fetterman: | | | | | | | | | Thank you, Mary | | | | | | Email us: info@johnfetterman.com

For years, silver has flown under the radar. It was overshadowed by gold, dismissed as a "secondary" metal, and ignored by most investors. But something is changing.

As inflation continues to chip away at savings and the dollar shows signs of long-term weakness, silver is getting attention again — not as an industrial metal, but as **real money.**

And unlike gold, which has already surged to new highs, silver still sits at a price point that many believe does not reflect its true value.
| **Silver gives everyday investors something rare — a store of value that is historically cheap compared to gold, but capable of big percentage moves when money flows into metals.**
---
**So Why Are Investors Buying Silver Now?**
* **It is one of the last undervalued precious metals.**
* **It acts as a hedge against inflation and currency decline.**
* **Historically, silver has outperformed gold during bull runs.**
* **It is affordable, making it easier to accumulate ounces.**
* **Physical silver is finite.** Once supply tightens, premiums rise quickly.

Silver is not about hype or speculation. It is about owning something real, scarce, and recognized globally as money for over 4,000 years.

And when confidence in paper assets wobbles, people do not rush into new tech or trends. They rush into metals they can hold.

**If you have ever considered owning silver, this may be one of the most attractive windows you get.**
| [ Get Your Silver Report](https://links.research.firstinvestmentupdates.com/u/click?_t=6c5e8d0087884bb7b7cd2e3abb0e6b5f&_m=62c95d53edef419a97785c973d2a8b3c&_e=_egAVpClFjx4NHaLxTxijboU6ywkyjAUjfceapoWSUS0GDiycc0x7TAEoQqtUeqCLxT0PL1m5rjadEjXhsXUcLeTyVADciU7j0bGJ7Jvh5FUE0ZUlFkyPXl9zeucqJmkPksXBfzxCbCq-jGB62-PVwcPKnTGB-g1cq6bG_8WQTdm4ficWmoLVkMEjlaRox1tVIZ6sAWY-_XJtrR9IOUeGHEHHF7WIjomJXhK8FI7XfUxahJuAl1JtM0CjO-zp8NFDK0FZwwR57dqdvWIvAZNOoL-0s)

Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: For years, silver has flown under the radar. It was overshadowed by gold, dismissed as a "secondary" metal, and ignored by most investors. But something is changing.| | | | | | "First Investment Update" | | | For years, silver has flown under the radar. It was overshadowed by gold, dismissed as a "secondary" metal, and ignored by most investors. But something is changing. As inflation continues to chip away at savings and the dollar shows signs of long-term weakness, silver is getting attention again — not as an industrial metal, but as real money. And unlike gold, which has already surged to new highs, silver still sits at a price point that many believe does not reflect its true value. | Silver gives everyday investors something rare — a store of value that is historically cheap compared to gold, but capable of big percentage moves when money flows into metals. So Why Are Investors Buying Silver Now? It is one of the last undervalued precious metals. It acts as a hedge against inflation and currency decline. Historically, silver has outperformed gold during bull runs. It is affordable, making it easier to accumulate ounces. Physical silver is finite. Once supply tightens, premiums rise quickly. Silver is not about hype or speculation. It is about owning something real, scarce, and recognized globally as money for over 4,000 years. And when confidence in paper assets wobbles, people do not rush into new tech or trends. They rush into metals they can hold. If you have ever considered owning silver, this may be one of the most attractive windows you get.** | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. # [Chambersburg Town Hall on Healthcare and Affordability](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=http%3A%2F%2Fbethfarnhamforcongress.com%2F2026%2F01%2F12%2Fchambersburg-town-hall-on-healthcare-and-affordability%2F&sr=0&signature=30ec09db6b34b3fd0776a1793e720446&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z)
---
By **Elizabeth Farnham** on January 12, 2026
---
| **Town Hall on Healthcare and Affordability**
---
Beth Farnham for Congress is excited to host a Town Hall on Healthcare and Affordability on Saturday, January 24, 2026, at The Franklin County Visitors Bureau, 15 S. Main St. Chambersburg, PA 17701 from 2 PM to 4 PM. Check-in will begin for those registered at 1:45 PM, and the town hall will start at 2 PM and end at 4 PM. Because of limited seating, only registered attendees will be admitted. Please allow for check-in time, which is required.
---
Free registration for the Town Hall here: [**https://bethfarnhamforcongress.com/chambersburg/**](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.com/chambersburg/)
---
There is free parking along Main Street on Saturdays and in the metered public lots. Please do not park behind the church.The Town Hall will take place inside the 11/30 room on the second floor. Please use the ramped side entrance on the left of the building, enter the doors, and use the elevator to go up 1 floor. The check-in process for registered attendees will begin at 1:45 PM. If enough seats are still available, on-site registration may be held. Please note that no signs, firearms, or weapons will be permitted at the event, and security prohibits large bags, backpacks, or diaper bags, but reserves the right to check any bag. The event will be recorded. The event program will be provided at check-in and is the key for entry to the room.
---
Voters from Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional district (PA-13) are urged to attend and share concerns or ask questions of Beth Farnham, candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional district. This event is paid for by Beth Farnham for Congress.
---

name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: No matter what happens…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Defense Trust
body:
There comes a point in every man’s life...
| Dear Peter,
---
There comes a point in every man’s life when you wonder how history will remember you.
---
No matter what happens in my RIGGED trial, I know with absolute confidence that history will say: **_“Rudy W. Giuliani was a fighter.”_**
---
[](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=681d66aa30414c28ae18f8ced7b48903&_e=8Bh78DS1L77jBl97y0pAYFpgSuvzESY8UtikeZsmL7s212duwKuvMYPs8CMMvjJ3HxSqEsXRbkrb-8dk6OoFK9OM7TJkQoBcTT8vdD_v81PxTRFqKGezOLTLaDiyK6suEEQEReuB547MNoGZ_BD1m7EmhInfOX7J-r5YunocgfAvj7K8KJV2bRF0zKMmeC02SUPI1KPyLWC2VB0w7rak0v9Ph4hoIBoYC49bo3ISNl_Hlh2RWPRMYwfS2jUxO9e3XH4nv4Gi8o5z2k3M_Ti27_5G-LbSamMPcCtXnqN1lyVld-vsby43RfeP_K8VFsuT6XG7qoKctAaNgYce2e3F3u1m44-2k29yN0wfZdUEynb7v008cGyP943CSb4kxX7DJvGm61J-_mqF_FkLK9LrL5lSRrm9TmsPACEUTRP32cXLChqUbQUBK4LjvVfP6zZ5sJeWAt_u4uVUn_g5qDYDGoNY4BOOXFas1lAFvMwK60-okEvdgm2DvFSVJyhDTpLsMDwiHT293ho825wqlk-S3Mmkrif9NHKdAlIor6vsPiPBS9zucMJb526Tn1KcZ9hGjqWMUSBAqtr30Beb7yUicD7r1R0Lww9kz8BLDYuAmbA%3D)
__
---
**I fought the Mafia, I fought violent criminals, I fought corruption, I fought the Deep State agents who tried to frame and destroy my client – President Trump.**
---
_Now, at 81 years old, rather than fighting for someone else’s freedom and justice, I find myself fighting for my** _own_**._
---
**That’s why I officially filed a NEW _Defense_ _Trust_ in the conservative-friendly state of Florida that will give me _more power_ to fight the Left’s FINAL attempt to imprison me for LIFE.**
---
_Peter, I am going to be brutally honest with you: right now, I lack the necessary means to fully fund my Defense Trust. Trust me, admitting that is not my strong suit._
---
But to beat the bloodthirsty vengeful Democrats, I’m going to need every cent I can get.
---
_I’m going up against the machine that let James Comey and Letitia James off the hook – the machine that had me and the President ARRESTED in the most violent jail in America._
---
With your help, history will forever remember that we took on the machine – and _WON_!
---
_But, if you can’t give right now, then please just carry on with your day._
---
[**But if you can, please make a contribution to my brand new “Rudy Defense Trust” to help me fight for my freedom and justice and beat the Left’s FINAL attempt to imprison me for life.**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=681d66aa30414c28ae18f8ced7b48903&_e=8Bh78DS1L77jBl97y0pAYFpgSuvzESY8UtikeZsmL7s212duwKuvMYPs8CMMvjJ3HxSqEsXRbkrb-8dk6OoFK9OM7TJkQoBcTT8vdD_v81PxTRFqKGezOLTLaDiyK6suEEQEReuB547MNoGZ_BD1m7EmhInfOX7J-r5YunocgfAvj7K8KJV2bRF0zKMmeC02zFbIK6Ap4YP8mhLfmMZF3xwQzSlvfNip_LCDgGLW1Ya7XEWNVdT75kWy24EXDZNay7Laz5WLr5nRdbEiMLUM60xZwGSw_zBFoxKO75CR8L7R06ar4tvvG1arPQCStMfUoH1ypspTR3sQ7augtImP5Bl2E7W6y7-CMUq14jFTFKrYKDCNtdljeB22K7JkDufj5aZJruLno2j8rPqKZ7ZC_bltwgB9zOntJ4L1WISMoHTu5X9JZALZtTzQTkUpSDKB7iQqO1n_UzYCvq90dTYxDWE-T9Hy71B76FbILs6N3f_j5kXyhrrMt7OEDausKZZwBj2ErVHffVPvNLOkB8pKPhMhN_dh-LGs6CNo__398opnBijP5oz6EnXdotwkPsuMcNKQLQjh2F9nMIHBPlfPIDv1yNwnt2Ee9P5JHulQMlM%3D)

clean_body: There comes a point in every man’s life... | Dear Peter, There comes a point in every man’s life when you wonder how history will remember you. No matter what happens in my RIGGED trial, I know with absolute confidence that history will say: “Rudy W. Giuliani was a fighter.” I fought the Mafia, I fought violent criminals, I fought corruption, I fought the Deep State agents who tried to frame and destroy my client – President Trump. Now, at 81 years old, rather than fighting for someone else’s freedom and justice, I find myself fighting for my own. That’s why I officially filed a NEW Defense Trust in the conservative-friendly state of Florida that will give me more power to fight the Left’s FINAL attempt to imprison me for LIFE. Peter, I am going to be brutally honest with you: right now, I lack the necessary means to fully fund my Defense Trust. Trust me, admitting that is not my strong suit. But to beat the bloodthirsty vengeful Democrats, I’m going to need every cent I can get. I’m going up against the machine that let James Comey and Letitia James off the hook – the machine that had me and the President ARRESTED in the most violent jail in America. With your help, history will forever remember that we took on the machine – and WON! But, if you can’t give right now, then please just carry on with your day. - Thank you and God Bless you! Rudy Giuliani Rudy Defense Trust Paid for by Rudy Defense Trust

Fellow Conservative,

Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one's shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway.

The out-of-touch fake news "reporters" at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: **Navy SEALs.**

We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can't abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country.

_**Yet the liberal news corporations, who _desperately_ want to keep the status quo, _attacked us_ for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government.**_

All of us –**Navy SEALs Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke** – are teaming up to stop career politicians and lazy bureaucrats, but we're up against more than just the DC establishment. We're up against the liberal media, RINOs, and revenge-seeking Democrats.
