subject: [Act Now] $40 trillion wiped out?
body:
⚠️1929-style crash INCOMING?
[View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e27475730/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvQmZCOFlCNzR2OA/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNjAxMTI/LbmNsbnpCU0tXOQ/qP2hpZHM9bmNiZ0JTYVBNVDVLMFdscw/gaWWK1Q/s7h0c879f72)

| | |
---
| | A very strange pattern seems to be repeating itself...
---
But this may be **the story you’re not being told** about the AI boom we’re seeing. Some analysts — the nerds with pocket protectors who actually understand all this financial jargon — say this pattern could hammer **everyday Americans and their savings...** Especially anyone who thought they were safely diversified in “set it and forget it” portfolios that supposedly protect them from every crisis.
---
Because when you look closer at recent coverage from **Forbes** , **Bloomberg** , and **The Wall Street Journal** ...
---
| |
---
One Forbes analysis warns that if today’s AI trade really is a bubble and it pops, as much as **$40 trillion in Nasdaq wealth could be wiped out.** Because if investors suddenly decide AI isn’t as profitable as they hoped... those stocks -- and the funds tied to them -- could drop fast. That’s why more and more people are saying the AI boom looks like a **“Dot-Com 2.0 Bubble”**.
---
And that raises a brutal but necessary question for anyone with an IRA or 401(k):
---
---
| | Some commentators are already asking whether an AI-driven crash could rival past disasters like 1929 -- or possibly cause a downturn that feels worse than 2008 for many households.
---
Those are not predictions.
They’re reference points.
**They happened.**
---
| |
---
| | The _real_ question is:

_How much of my retirement depends on this whole AI-and-Wall-Street tower staying upright... and what if I’m the one left holding the bag if it doesn’t?_ _And is now the time to look at_** _physical gold & silver_** _as part of my IRA strategy?_
---
| |
---
[**That’s why you’ll want this free guide NOW.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
| | [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
It’s a brief, to-the-point handbook that shows you:
---
---
---
| | At the very least,
[**get the facts and decide for yourself.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
The full breakdown is being shared free to the public on this website. So, while this website is still online...
---
[**> > GET THE FREE GUIDE THAT COULD HELP YOUR IRA/401(k)**** ****< <**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Don’t wait to find out the hard way what a “set it and forget it” portfolio could if a Dot-Com 2.0 Bubble breaks.
---

**I support Mary because she understands something Washington too often forgets: politics should be about improving ordinary people's lives, not powerful interests.** Mary grew up fishing salmon with her dad and mushing dogs in the winter, and she's seen how Alaska has gone from abundance to scarcity as multinational corporations strip resources, drive up costs, and leave families paying $17 for a gallon of milk.
While many D.C. politicians look out for themselves and their billionaire donors, Mary is focused on fish, family, and freedom — and on fighting corruption so working families can actually get ahead. She knows that lowering grocery and energy costs, protecting fisheries, and expanding affordable housing all require systemic change, not business as usual.
This race will be one of the closest Senate races this year. Winning in Alaska is essential if we're going to retake the Democratic Senate Majority and stand up to Trump and corporate greed. **Mary is exactly the kind of leader we need in the Senate, and she can win, but only if we show up early and strong.**

clean_body: There’s a huge opportunity in Alaska right now — one that could help decide whether Democrats retake the Senate majority this November. | | | | There's a huge opportunity in Alaska right now — one that could help decide whether Democrats retake the Senate majority this November. And that's why I want to tell you about Mary Peltola, who just launched her campaign today for the United States Senate. = Donate Now If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process and split your contribution between Mary Peltola and Jeff Merkley instantly: | = | = ---|--- = | = = I support Mary because she understands something Washington too often forgets: politics should be about improving ordinary people's lives, not powerful interests. Mary grew up fishing salmon with her dad and mushing dogs in the winter, and she's seen how Alaska has gone from abundance to scarcity as multinational corporations strip resources, drive up costs, and leave families paying $17 for a gallon of milk. While many D.C. politicians look out for themselves and their billionaire donors, Mary is focused on fish, family, and freedom — and on fighting corruption so working families can actually get ahead. She knows that lowering grocery and energy costs, protecting fisheries, and expanding affordable housing all require systemic change, not business as usual. This race will be one of the closest Senate races this year. Winning in Alaska is essential if we're going to retake the Democratic Senate Majority and stand up to Trump and corporate greed. Mary is exactly the kind of leader we need in the Senate, and she can win, but only if we show up early and strong. = = Onward!
